description_plain: Your child can multiply a fraction by a whole number and solve real-life problems using this skill.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole and having like denominators, e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that add or subtract fractions with the same denominator using drawings or fraction models.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract mixed numbers with like denominators, e.g., by replacing each mixed number with an equivalent fraction, and/or by using properties of operations and the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract mixed numbers with the same bottom number by changing them into improper fractions or using addition and subtraction rules.

subject: Math
description: Decompose a fraction into a sum of fractions with like denominators in more than one way, recording each decomposition by an equation. Justify decompositions, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.
description_plain: Your child can break a fraction into a sum of smaller fractions with the same bottom number in different ways and explain their thinking using pictures.

subject: Math
description: Understand addition and subtraction of fractions as joining and separating parts referring to the same whole.
description_plain: Your child understands that adding and subtracting fractions means putting parts together or taking parts apart from the same whole.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction a/b with a>1 as a sum of fractions 1/b.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction with a numerator bigger than 1 can be seen as adding several fractions with numerator 1.

subject: Math
description: Build fractions from unit fractions.
description_plain: Your child can build and work with fractions by putting together smaller equal parts.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with different numerators and different denominators, e.g., by creating common denominators or numerators, or by comparing to a benchmark fraction such as 1/2. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.
description_plain: Your child can compare two fractions with different top and bottom numbers by finding common denominators or numerators, or by comparing to ½, and can use >, =, or < to show which is bigger, explaining their answer with pictures.

subject: Math
description: Explain why a fraction a/b is equivalent to a fraction n×a/n×b by using visual fraction models, with attention to how the number and size of the parts differ even though the two fractions themselves are the same size. Use this principle to recognize and generate equivalent fractions.
description_plain: Your child can explain why multiplying the top and bottom of a fraction by the same number makes an equal fraction, using pictures, and can find equivalent fractions.

subject: Math
description: Extend understanding of fraction equivalence and ordering.
description_plain: Your child understands how to tell if two fractions are equal and can put fractions in order from smallest to largest.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients and remainders with up to four-digit dividends and one-digit divisors, using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
description_plain: Your child can divide numbers up to four digits by one-digit numbers, find the quotient and remainder, and explain their work using equations and pictures like arrays or area models.

subject: Math
description: Multiply a whole number of up to four digits by a one-digit whole number, and multiply two two-digit numbers, using strategies based on place value and the properties of operations. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a number with up to four digits by a one-digit number and multiply two two-digit numbers using place value and properties of multiplication, and explain their work with equations or models.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract multi-digit whole numbers using the standard algorithm.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract large whole numbers using the standard method.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic.
description_plain: Your child can use their understanding of place value to solve problems with large numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round multi-digit whole numbers to any place.
description_plain: Your child can round large numbers to any place value, like the nearest ten, hundred, or thousand.

subject: Math
description: Read and write multi-digit whole numbers using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form. Compare two multi-digit numbers based on meanings of the digits in each place, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can read and write large whole numbers in different ways and compare two numbers by looking at the value of each digit, using >, =, or < to show which is bigger.

subject: Math
description: Explain that in a multi-digit whole number, a digit in one place represents ten times what it represents in the place to its right.
description_plain: Your child understands that in a multi-digit number, each digit is worth ten times more than the digit to its right.

subject: Math
description: Generalize place value understanding for multi-digit whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands how place value works for numbers with more than one digit.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child looks for and explains patterns when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child notices and uses the structure of problems to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child chooses and uses tools in smart ways to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their thinking and listen to others to understand math better.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about numbers and ideas in different ways to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child tries to understand problems and keeps working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy based on properties.
description_plain: Your child can group flat shapes based on their properties in a way that shows which shapes belong to bigger or smaller groups.

subject: Math
description: Explain that attributes belonging to a category of two-dimensional figures also belong to all subcategories of that category.
description_plain: Your child understands that if a shape belongs to a big group of 2D shapes, it also has the properties of all smaller groups inside that big group.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures into categories based on their properties.
description_plain: Your child can group flat shapes into categories based on their features.

subject: Math
description: Represent real-world and mathematical problems by graphing points in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane, and interpret coordinate values of points in the context of the situation.
description_plain: Your child can plot points on a graph in the first quadrant and understand what the points mean in real-life or math problems.

subject: Math
description: Use a pair of perpendicular number lines, called axes, to define a coordinate system, with the intersection of the lines (the origin) arranged to coincide with the 0 on each line and a given point in the plane located by using an ordered pair of numbers, called its coordinates. Understand that the first number indicates how far to travel from the origin in the direction of one axis, and the second number indicates how far to travel in the direction of the second axis, with the convention that the names of the two axes and the coordinates correspond (e.g., x-axis and x-coordinate, y-axis and y-coordinate).
description_plain: Your child can use two number lines that cross at zero to find points on a grid by using pairs of numbers that tell how far to go along each line.

subject: Math
description: Graph points on the coordinate plane to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can put points on a graph to help solve real-world and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Use the additive nature of volume to find volumes of solid figures composed of two non-overlapping right rectangular prisms by adding the volumes of the non-overlapping parts, applying this technique to solve real-world problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of shapes made by joining two rectangular boxes by adding the volumes of each box and use this to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Apply the formulas V=l×w×h and V=b×h for rectangular prisms to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with whole-number edge lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of rectangular boxes with whole number sides in real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model the volume of a right rectangular prism with whole-number side lengths by packing it with unit cubes, and show that the volume is the same as would be found by multiplying the edge lengths, equivalently by multiplying the height by the area of the base. Represent threefold whole-number products as volumes, e.g., to represent the associative property of multiplication.
description_plain: Your child can show that the volume of a rectangular box is the number of unit cubes that fit inside and that this equals multiplying the length, width, and height.

subject: Math
description: Relate volume to the operations of multiplication and addition and solve real-world and mathematical problems involving volume.
description_plain: Your child understands how volume relates to multiplication and addition and can solve problems about the volume of rectangular boxes.

subject: Math
description: Measure volumes by counting unit cubes, using cubic cm, cubic in, cubic ft, and improvised units.
description_plain: Your child can measure volume by counting unit cubes and using units like cubic centimeters, inches, feet, or other units.

subject: Math
description: A solid figure which can be packed without gaps or overlaps using n unit cubes is said to have a volume of n cubic units.
description_plain: Your child understands that a solid filled exactly with a certain number of unit cubes has a volume equal to that number of cubes.

subject: Math
description: A cube with side length 1 unit, called a "unit cube," is said to have "one cubic unit" of volume and can be used to measure volume.
description_plain: Your child knows that a cube with sides of 1 unit has a volume of one cubic unit and can use it to measure volume.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as an attribute of solid figures and understand concepts of volume measurement.
description_plain: Your child understands that volume is a property of solid shapes and learns how to measure it.

subject: Math
description: Understand concepts of volume and relate volume to multiplication and to addition.
description_plain: Your child understands what volume is and how it connects to multiplication and addition.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (½, ¼, 1/8). Use operations on fractions for this grade to solve problems involving information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot to show measurements in fractions like ½, ¼, or 1/8 and use fraction operations to solve problems from the plot.

subject: Math
description: Convert among different-sized standard measurement units within a given measurement system (e.g., convert 5 cm to 0.05 m), and use these conversions in solving multi-step, real-world problems.
description_plain: Your child can change measurements from one unit to another within the same system (like centimeters to meters) and use these changes to solve multi-step real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Convert like measurement units within a given measurement system.
description_plain: Your child can convert between units that measure the same thing within one measurement system.

subject: Math
description: Data, Statistics, and Probability
description_plain: Your child learns about data, statistics, and probability.

subject: Math
description: Generate two numerical patterns using two given rules. Identify apparent relationships between corresponding terms. Form ordered pairs consisting of corresponding terms from the two patterns, and graph the ordered pairs on a coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can create two number patterns from two rules, find relationships between the numbers in the patterns, make pairs from matching numbers, and graph these pairs on a coordinate plane.

subject: Math
description: Analyze patterns and relationships.
description_plain: Your child can find and understand patterns and relationships in numbers.

subject: Math
description: Write simple expressions that record calculations with numbers, and interpret numerical expressions without evaluating them.
description_plain: Your child can write simple math expressions to show calculations and understand what these expressions mean without solving them.

subject: Math
description: Use grouping symbols (parentheses, brackets, or braces) in numerical expressions, and evaluate expressions with these symbols.
description_plain: Your child can use parentheses, brackets, or braces in math expressions and correctly solve expressions that include these grouping symbols.

subject: Math
description: Write and interpret numerical expressions.
description_plain: Your child can write and understand math expressions.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving division of unit fractions by non-zero whole numbers and division of whole numbers by unit fractions, e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems by dividing fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by fractions, using pictures or equations to show their work.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division of a whole number by a unit fraction, and compute such quotients.
description_plain: Your child can understand and calculate what happens when a whole number is divided by a unit fraction.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division of a unit fraction by a non-zero whole number, and compute such quotients.
description_plain: Your child can understand and calculate what happens when a fraction with 1 in the numerator is divided by a whole number.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of division to divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions. (Students able to multiply fractions in general can develop strategies to divide fractions in general, by reasoning about the relationship between multiplication and division. But division of a fraction by a fraction is not a requirement at this grade.)
description_plain: Your child can divide fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by fractions, and solve real-life problems using these skills.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving multiplication of fractions and mixed numbers, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems by multiplying fractions and mixed numbers, using pictures or equations to explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Explaining why multiplying a given number by a fraction greater than 1 results in a product greater than the given number (recognizing multiplication by whole numbers greater than 1 as a familiar case); explaining why multiplying a given number by a fraction less than 1 results in a product smaller than the given number; and relating the principle of fraction equivalence a/b=n×a/n×b to the effect of multiplying a/b by 1.
description_plain: Your child can explain why multiplying by a fraction greater than 1 makes a number bigger and why multiplying by a fraction less than 1 makes it smaller, and understand how multiplying by 1 keeps a fraction the same.

subject: Math
description: Comparing the size of a product to the size of one factor on the basis of the size of the other factor, without performing the indicated multiplication.
description_plain: Your child can compare the size of a multiplication result to one of the numbers being multiplied by looking at the other number, without actually multiplying.

subject: Math
description: Interpret multiplication as scaling (resizing), by:
description_plain: Your child understands multiplication as resizing or scaling a number.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with fractional side lengths by tiling it with unit squares of the appropriate unit fraction side lengths, and show that the area is the same as would be found by multiplying the side lengths. Multiply fractional side lengths to find areas of rectangles, and represent fraction products as rectangular areas.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of rectangles with fractional side lengths by counting small squares and by multiplying the side lengths, showing that both methods give the same answer.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the product a/b×q as a parts of a partition of q into b equal parts; equivalently, as the result of a sequence of operations a×q÷b.
description_plain: Your child understands that multiplying a fraction by a number means taking parts of that number, and can explain this using multiplication and division steps.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication to multiply a fraction or whole number by a fraction.
description_plain: Your child can multiply fractions and whole numbers by fractions, building on what they already know about multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a fraction as division of the numerator by the denominator (a/b=a÷b). Solve word problems involving division of whole numbers leading to answers in the form of fractions or mixed numbers, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction means dividing the top number by the bottom number and can solve word problems that involve dividing whole numbers to get fractions or mixed numbers.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about multiplying and dividing to work with fractions.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole, including cases of unlike denominators, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem. Use benchmark fractions and number sense of fractions to estimate mentally and assess the reasonableness of answers.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that add or subtract fractions with different bottom numbers by using pictures or equations and can estimate answers to check if they make sense.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators (including mixed numbers) by replacing given fractions with equivalent fractions in such a way as to produce an equivalent sum or difference of fractions with like denominators.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions with different bottom numbers (including mixed numbers) by changing them to equivalent fractions with the same bottom number.

subject: Math
description: Use equivalent fractions as a strategy to add and subtract fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use equivalent fractions to help add and subtract fractions.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to two decimal places using drawings or place value strategies, and can check if their answers make sense by estimating.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients of whole numbers with up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors, using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
description_plain: Your child can divide large whole numbers (up to four digits) by two-digit numbers using place value and other strategies, and can explain their work with equations or pictures.

subject: Math
description: Fluently multiply multi-digit whole numbers using the standard algorithm.
description_plain: Your child can multiply multi-digit whole numbers quickly and accurately using the standard method.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations with multi-digit whole numbers and with decimals to hundredths.
description_plain: Your child can work with large whole numbers and decimals to the hundredths place using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round decimals to any place.
description_plain: Your child can round decimals to any place value.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to thousandths based on meanings of the digits in each place, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals to the thousandths place using >, =, and < symbols.

subject: Math
description: Read and write decimals to thousandths using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form, e.g., 347.392=3×100+4×10+7×1+3×110+9×1100+2×11000.
description_plain: Your child can read and write decimals to the thousandths place using numbers, words, and expanded form to show the value of each digit.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and compare decimals to thousandths.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and compare decimals up to the thousandths place.

subject: Math
description: Explain patterns in the number of zeros of the product when multiplying a number by powers of 10, and explain patterns in the placement of the decimal point when a decimal is multiplied or divided by a power of 10. Use whole-number exponents to denote powers of 10.
description_plain: Your child can explain patterns when multiplying or dividing by powers of 10, like how zeros or decimal points move, and use exponents to show powers of 10.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that in a multi-digit number, a digit in one place represents 10 times as much as it represents in the place to its right and 1/10 of what it represents in the place to its left.
description_plain: Your child understands that each place in a number is ten times bigger than the place to its right and one-tenth the place to its left.

subject: Math
description: Understand the place value system.
description_plain: Your child understands how place value works in numbers.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child looks for and explains patterns when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child notices and uses the structure of problems to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child chooses and uses tools in smart ways to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their thinking and listen to others to understand math better.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about numbers and ideas in different ways to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child tries to understand problems and keeps working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Partition shapes into parts with equal areas. Express the area of each part as a unit fraction of the whole.
description_plain: Your child can divide shapes into equal parts and describe each part as a fraction of the whole shape.

subject: Math
description: Explain that shapes in different categories (e.g., rhombuses, rectangles, and others) may share attributes (e.g., having four sides), and that the shared attributes can define a larger category (e.g., quadrilaterals). Recognize rhombuses, rectangles, and squares as examples of quadrilaterals, and draw examples of quadrilaterals that do not belong to any of these subcategories.
description_plain: Your child understands that shapes like rhombuses, rectangles, and squares share some features and belong to a bigger group called quadrilaterals, and can draw quadrilaterals that don’t fit into those smaller groups.

subject: Math
description: Reason with shapes and their attributes.
description_plain: Your child can think about shapes and their features.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving perimeters of polygons, including finding the perimeter given the side lengths, finding an unknown side length, and exhibiting rectangles with the same perimeter and different areas or with the same area and different perimeters.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about the perimeter of shapes by adding side lengths, finding missing sides, and exploring how shapes with the same perimeter can have different areas.

subject: Math
description: Recognize perimeter as an attribute of plane figures and distinguish between linear and area measures.
description_plain: Your child knows that perimeter measures the distance around a shape and can tell the difference between measuring length and measuring area.

subject: Math
description: Use tiling to show in a concrete case that the area of a rectangle with whole-number side lengths a and b+c is the sum of a×b and a×c. Use area models to represent the distributive property in mathematical reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can use small squares to show that the area of a rectangle with sides a and b + c is the same as adding the areas of rectangles with sides a and b, and a and c.

subject: Math
description: Multiply side lengths to find areas of rectangles with whole-number side lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems, and represent whole-number products as rectangular areas in mathematical reasoning.
description_plain: Your child multiplies side lengths to find the area of rectangles and uses this to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with whole-number side lengths by tiling it, and show that the area is the same as would be found by multiplying the side lengths.
description_plain: Your child finds the area of a rectangle by counting squares or multiplying side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Use concepts of area and relate area to the operations of multiplication and addition.
description_plain: Your child understands area and knows how it relates to multiplying and adding.

subject: Math
description: Measure areas by counting unit squares (square cm, square m, square in, square ft, and improvised units).
description_plain: Your child can measure area by counting square units like square centimeters or square inches.

subject: Math
description: A plane figure which can be covered without gaps or overlaps by n unit squares is said to have an area of n square units.
description_plain: Your child knows that if a shape can be covered exactly by n squares, its area is n square units.

subject: Math
description: A square with side length 1 unit, called "a unit square," is said to have "one square unit" of area, and can be used to measure area.
description_plain: Your child understands that a square with sides one unit long is called a unit square and measures one square unit of area.

subject: Math
description: Recognize area as an attribute of plane figures and understand concepts of area measurement.
description_plain: Your child knows that area is a property of flat shapes and understands what it means to measure area.

subject: Math
description: Use concepts of area and relate area to multiplication and to addition.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about area and connects it to multiplication and addition.

subject: Math
description: Generate measurement data by measuring lengths using rulers marked with halves and fourths of an inch. Show the data by making a line plot, where the horizontal scale is marked off in appropriate units—whole numbers, halves, or quarters.
description_plain: Your child measures lengths using rulers marked in halves and quarters of an inch and shows the results on a line plot.

subject: Math
description: Draw a scaled picture graph and a scaled bar graph to represent a data set with several categories. Solve one- and two-step "how many more" and "how many less" problems using information presented in scaled bar graphs.
description_plain: Your child can draw picture and bar graphs with scales to show data and solve questions about how much more or less one category has compared to another.

subject: Math
description: Measure and estimate liquid volumes and masses of objects using standard units of grams (g), kilograms (kg), and liters (l). (This excludes compound units such as cm3 and finding the geometric volume of a container.) Add, subtract, multiply, or divide to solve one-step word problems involving masses or volumes that are given in the same units, e.g., by using drawings (such as a beaker with a measurement scale) to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can measure and estimate how much liquids and objects weigh or hold using grams, kilograms, and liters, and solve simple word problems using drawings.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the nearest minute and measure time intervals in minutes. Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of time intervals in minutes, e.g., by representing the problem on a number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest minute, measure time intervals, and solve problems about adding or subtracting time using number lines.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving measurement and estimation of intervals of time, liquid volumes, and masses of objects.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems involving measuring and estimating time, liquid amounts, and weights.

subject: Math
description: Data, Statistics, and Probability
description_plain: Your child learns about data, statistics, and probability.

subject: Math
description: Identify arithmetic patterns (including patterns in the addition table or multiplication table) and explain them using properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain patterns in addition and multiplication tables using math rules.

subject: Math
description: Solve two-step word problems using the four operations. Represent these problems using equations with a letter standing for the unknown quantity. Assess the reasonableness of answers using mental computation and estimation strategies including rounding. (This evidence outcome is limited to problems posed with whole numbers and having whole-number answers; students should know how to perform operations in the conventional order of operations when there are no parentheses to specify a particular order.)
description_plain: Your child can solve two-step word problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division and check their answers by estimating.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving the four operations, and identify and explain patterns in arithmetic.
description_plain: Your child can solve math problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division, and can find and explain patterns in numbers.

subject: Math
description: Fluently multiply and divide within 100, using strategies such as the relationship between multiplication and division (e.g., knowing that 8×5=40, one knows 40÷5=8) or properties of operations. By the end of Grade 3, know from memory all products of two one-digit numbers.
description_plain: Your child can quickly multiply and divide numbers up to 100 using strategies like knowing related facts and math properties, and knows all multiplication and division facts for single-digit numbers by memory.

subject: Math
description: Multiply and divide within 100.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide numbers up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division as an unknown-factor problem.
description_plain: Your child understands division problems where one number is missing and can figure out that missing number.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to multiply and divide.
description_plain: Your child uses rules about how numbers work to help solve multiplication and division problems.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of multiplication and the relationship between multiplication and division.
description_plain: Your child uses multiplication properties and the connection between multiplication and division to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown whole number in a multiplication or division equation relating three whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can find the missing number in multiplication or division problems without needing special math words.

subject: Math
description: Use multiplication and division within 100 to solve word problems in situations involving equal groups, arrays, and measurement quantities, e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems about equal groups, arrays, or measurements using multiplication and division, and write equations with a letter for the unknown.

subject: Math
description: Interpret whole-number quotients of whole numbers, e.g., interpret 56÷8 as the number of objects in each share when 56 objects are partitioned equally into 8 shares, or as a number of shares when 56 objects are partitioned into equal shares of 8 objects each. For example, describe a context in which a number of shares or a number of groups can be expressed as 56÷8.
description_plain: Your child understands division as sharing or grouping objects equally and can explain what division means in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Interpret products of whole numbers, e.g., interpret 5×7 as the total number of objects in 5 groups of 7 objects each.
description_plain: Your child understands multiplication as finding the total number of objects in equal groups.

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems involving multiplication and division.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems using multiplication and division.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with the same numerator or the same denominator by reasoning about their size. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.
description_plain: Your child compares two fractions with the same top or bottom number by thinking about their size and uses >, =, or < to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Express whole numbers as fractions, and recognize fractions that are equivalent to whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write whole numbers as fractions and recognize when fractions are equal to whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and generate simple equivalent fractions, e.g., 1/2 = 2/4, 4/6 = 2/3. Explain why the fractions are equivalent, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.
description_plain: Your child can find and make simple equivalent fractions and explain why they are equal using pictures.

subject: Math
description: Understand two fractions as equivalent (equal) if they are the same size, or the same point on a number line.
description_plain: Your child understands that two fractions are equal if they represent the same amount or point on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Explain equivalence of fractions in special cases, and compare fractions by reasoning about their size.
description_plain: Your child can explain when two fractions are equal and compare fractions by thinking about their size.

subject: Math
description: Represent a fraction a/b on a number line diagram by marking off a lengths 1/b from 0. Recognize that the resulting interval has size a/b and that its endpoint locates the number a/b on the number line.
description_plain: Your child can show a fraction on a number line by marking equal parts and understands where the fraction fits on the line.

subject: Math
description: Represent a fraction 1/b on a number line diagram by defining the interval from 0 to 1 as the whole and partitioning it into b equal parts. Recognize that each part has size 1/b and that the endpoint of the part based at 0 locates the number 1/b on the number line.
description_plain: Your child can show the fraction 1/b on a number line by dividing the space from 0 to 1 into equal parts and identifying one part.

subject: Math
description: Describe a fraction as a number on the number line; represent fractions on a number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child can describe fractions as numbers on a number line and can place them correctly.

subject: Math
description: Describe a fraction 1/b as the quantity formed by 1 part when a whole is partitioned into b equal parts; understand a fraction a/b as the quantity formed by a parts of size 1/b.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction like 1/b means one part of a whole divided into b equal parts, and a/b means a parts of size 1/b.

subject: Math
description: Develop understanding of fractions as numbers.
description_plain: Your child learns that fractions are numbers and how they work.

subject: Math
description: Multiply one-digit whole numbers by multiples of 10 in the range 10–90 (e.g., 9×80, 5×60) using strategies based on place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can multiply one-digit numbers by multiples of 10 (like 20 or 90) using place value and math properties.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 1000 using strategies and algorithms based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to 1000 using strategies based on place value and math facts.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round whole numbers to the nearest 10 or 100.
description_plain: Your child can round whole numbers to the nearest 10 or 100 using place value.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic. A range of algorithms may be used.
description_plain: Your child uses place value and math rules to add, subtract, multiply, and divide multi-digit numbers, using different methods.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child looks for and explains patterns when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child notices and uses the structure of problems to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child chooses and uses tools in smart ways to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their thinking and listen to others to understand math better.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about numbers and ideas in different ways to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child tries to understand problems and keeps working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Represent three-dimensional figures using nets made up of rectangles and triangles, and use the nets to find the surface area of these figures. Apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can draw flat patterns (nets) made of rectangles and triangles to build 3D shapes and use these to find surface area in real problems.

subject: Math
description: Draw polygons in the coordinate plane given coordinates for the vertices; use coordinates to find the length of a side joining points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate. Apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can draw shapes on a grid using points and find side lengths when sides are straight up/down or left/right, solving real problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with fractional edge lengths by packing it with unit cubes of the appropriate unit fraction edge lengths, and show that the volume is the same as would be found by multiplying the edge lengths of the prism. Apply the formulas V=lwh and V=bh to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with fractional edge lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of boxes with fractional side lengths by filling them with small cubes and by multiplying the edges, using formulas in real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of right triangles, other triangles, special quadrilaterals, and polygons by composing into rectangles or decomposing into triangles and other shapes; apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of triangles, special four-sided shapes, and other polygons by breaking them into rectangles or triangles, and use this in real problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving area, surface area, and volume.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems involving area, surface area, and volume.

subject: Math
description: Relating the choice of measures of center and variability to the shape of the data distribution and the context in which the data were gathered.
description_plain: Your child can choose the right way to describe data based on how the data looks and where it came from.

subject: Math
description: Giving quantitative measures of center (median and/or mean) and variability (interquartile range and/or mean absolute deviation), as well as describing any overall pattern and any striking deviations from the overall pattern with reference to the context in which the data were gathered.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain the middle and spread of data, describe overall patterns, and point out anything unusual, using the context of the data.

subject: Math
description: Describing the nature of the attribute under investigation, including how it was measured and its units of measurement.
description_plain: Your child can describe what is being measured in data, how it was measured, and the units used.

subject: Math
description: Reporting the number of observations.
description_plain: Your child can tell how many observations or data points were collected.

subject: Math
description: Summarize numerical data sets in relation to their context, such as by:
description_plain: Your child can summarize sets of numbers by explaining what they mean in real life.

subject: Math
description: Display numerical data in plots on a number line, including dot plots, histograms, and box plots.
description_plain: Your child can show number data on number lines using dot plots, histograms, and box plots.

subject: Math
description: Summarize and describe distributions.
description_plain: Your child can summarize and describe how data is spread out and what patterns it shows.

subject: Math
description: Explain that a measure of center for a numerical data set summarizes all of its values with a single number, while a measure of variation describes how its values vary with a single number.
description_plain: Your child understands that one number can show the average of data, and another number can show how much the data values differ.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate that a set of data collected to answer a statistical question has a distribution that can be described by its center, spread, and overall shape.
description_plain: Your child can describe a set of data by explaining its middle value, how spread out the data is, and the overall pattern or shape of the data.

subject: Math
description: Identify a statistical question as one that anticipates variability in the data related to the question and accounts for it in the answers.
description_plain: Your child understands that a good statistical question expects different answers and includes this variation when finding the answer.

subject: Math
description: Develop understanding of statistical variability.
description_plain: Your child understands that data can vary and learns how to describe that variability.

subject: Math
description: Data, Statistics, and Probability
description_plain: Your child learns about data, statistics, and probability.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent two quantities in a real-world problem that change in relationship to one another; write an equation to express one quantity, thought of as the dependent variable, in terms of the other quantity, thought of as the independent variable. Analyze the relationship between the dependent and independent variables using graphs and tables, and relate these to the equation.
description_plain: Your child can use letters to stand for two amounts in a real-life problem that change depending on each other.

subject: Math
description: Represent and analyze quantitative relationships between dependent and independent variables.
description_plain: Your child can show and study how one quantity depends on another using math.

subject: Math
description: Write an inequality of the form x > c, x ≥ c, x < c, or x ≤ c to represent a constraint or condition in a real-world or mathematical problem. Show that inequalities of the form x > c, x ≥ c, x < c, or x ≤ c have infinitely many solutions; represent solutions of such inequalities on number line diagrams.
description_plain: Your child can write inequalities like x > 5 or x ≤ 3 to show limits in real-life or math problems and can show all possible answers on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems by writing and solving equations of the form x±p=q and px=q for cases in which p, q, and x are all nonnegative rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems by writing and solving simple equations like x + 3 = 7 or 4x = 12 when all numbers are positive or zero.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent numbers and write expressions when solving a real-world or mathematical problem; recognize that a variable can represent an unknown number, or, depending on the purpose at hand, any number in a specified set.
description_plain: Your child can use letters to stand for numbers and write expressions to solve real-life or math problems, knowing that letters can mean unknown or any number in a group.

subject: Math
description: Describe solving an equation or inequality as a process of answering a question: Which values from a specified set, if any, make the equation or inequality true? Use substitution to determine whether a given number in a specified set makes an equation or inequality true.
description_plain: Your child understands solving an equation or inequality means finding which numbers make it true, and can check if a number works by plugging it in.

subject: Math
description: Reason about and solve one-variable equations and inequalities.
description_plain: Your child can think through and solve equations and inequalities with one variable.

subject: Math
description: Identify when two expressions are equivalent (i.e., when the two expressions name the same number regardless of which value is substituted into them).
description_plain: Your child can tell when two expressions are equal because they give the same result no matter what number is used.

subject: Math
description: Apply the properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to create different expressions that are equal.

subject: Math
description: Evaluate expressions at specific values of their variables. Include expressions that arise from formulas used in real-world problems. Perform arithmetic operations, including those involving whole-number exponents, in the conventional order when there are no parentheses to specify a particular order (Order of Operations).
description_plain: Your child can find the value of expressions by plugging in numbers for letters, including using formulas from real-life problems, and follows the correct order of operations.

subject: Math
description: Identify parts of an expression using mathematical terms (sum, term, product, factor, quotient, coefficient); view one or more parts of an expression as a single entity.
description_plain: Your child can name parts of an expression using words like sum, term, product, factor, quotient, and coefficient, and can see parts of an expression as one piece.

subject: Math
description: Write expressions that record operations with numbers and with letters standing for numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write expressions that show math operations using numbers and letters that stand for numbers.

subject: Math
description: Write, read, and evaluate expressions in which letters stand for numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write, read, and find the value of expressions where letters stand for numbers.

subject: Math
description: Write and evaluate numerical expressions involving whole-number exponents.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve number expressions that include whole-number exponents.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of arithmetic to algebraic expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about arithmetic to work with algebra expressions.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems by graphing points in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane. Include use of coordinates and absolute value to find distances between points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems by plotting points in all four parts of the coordinate plane and use coordinates and absolute value to find distances between points.

subject: Math
description: Distinguish comparisons of absolute value from statements about order.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between comparing absolute values and comparing the order of numbers.

subject: Math
description: Define the absolute value of a rational number as its distance from 0 on the number line; interpret absolute value as magnitude for a positive or negative quantity in a real-world situation.
description_plain: Your child understands absolute value as how far a number is from zero on the number line and can explain this for positive or negative amounts in real life.

subject: Math
description: Write, interpret, and explain statements of order for rational numbers in real-world contexts.
description_plain: Your child can write and explain statements about the order of rational numbers in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Interpret statements of inequality as statements about the relative position of two numbers on a number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child can understand inequalities as showing which number is bigger or smaller on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Order and find absolute value of rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can put rational numbers in order and find their absolute values.

subject: Math
description: Find and position integers and other rational numbers on a horizontal or vertical number line diagram; find and position pairs of integers and other rational numbers on a coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can find and place integers and other rational numbers on number lines and coordinate planes.

subject: Math
description: Understand signs of numbers in ordered pairs as indicating locations in quadrants of the coordinate plane; explain that when two ordered pairs differ only by signs, the locations of the points are related by reflections across one or both axes.
description_plain: Your child understands that the signs in ordered pairs tell where points are on the coordinate plane and that changing signs reflects points across axes.

subject: Math
description: Use opposite signs of numbers as indicating locations on opposite sides of 0 on the number line; identify that the opposite of the opposite of a number is the number itself, e.g., −(−3)=3, and that 0 is its own opposite.
description_plain: Your child understands that numbers with opposite signs are on opposite sides of zero on the number line, knows that the opposite of the opposite of a number is the number itself, and that zero is its own opposite.

subject: Math
description: Describe a rational number as a point on the number line. Extend number line diagrams and coordinate axes familiar from previous grades to represent points on the line and in the plane with negative number coordinates.
description_plain: Your child can show rational numbers as points on a number line and use number lines and coordinate planes that include negative numbers.

subject: Math
description: Explain why positive and negative numbers are used together to describe quantities having opposite directions or values (e.g., temperature above/below zero, elevation above/below sea level, credits/debits, positive/negative electric charge); use positive and negative numbers to represent quantities in real-world contexts, explaining the meaning of 0 in each situation.
description_plain: Your child can explain why positive and negative numbers are used together to show opposite directions or values, like temperature above or below zero, and can use these numbers to describe real-life situations, including what zero means.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of numbers to the system of rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about numbers to understand and work with positive and negative numbers, including fractions and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Find the greatest common factor of two whole numbers less than or equal to 100 and the least common multiple of two whole numbers less than or equal to 12. Use the distributive property to express a sum of two whole numbers 1–100 with a common factor as a multiple of a sum of two whole numbers with no common factor.
description_plain: Your child can find the greatest common factor of two whole numbers up to 100 and the least common multiple of two whole numbers up to 12, and can use the distributive property to rewrite sums with common factors.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add, subtract, multiply, and divide multi-digit decimals using the standard algorithm for each operation.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and accurately add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals with many digits using standard methods.

subject: Math
description: Fluently divide multi-digit numbers using the standard algorithm.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and accurately divide large numbers using the standard method.

subject: Math
description: Compute fluently with multi-digit numbers and find common factors and multiples.
description_plain: Your child can work easily with large numbers and find common factors and multiples.

subject: Math
description: Interpret and compute quotients of fractions, and solve word problems involving division of fractions by fractions, e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can understand and calculate division of fractions and solve word problems about dividing fractions by fractions using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to divide fractions by fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about multiplication and division to solve problems with fractions dividing by fractions.

subject: Math
description: Use ratio reasoning to convert measurement units; manipulate and transform units appropriately when multiplying or dividing quantities.
description_plain: Your child can use ratio thinking to change measurement units and correctly adjust units when multiplying or dividing.

subject: Math
description: Find a percent of a quantity as a rate per 100 (e.g., 30% of a quantity means 30/100 times the quantity); solve problems involving finding the whole, given a part and the percent.
description_plain: Your child can find a percent of a number and solve problems where they know a part and the percent to find the whole amount.

subject: Math
description: Solve unit rate problems including those involving unit pricing and constant speed.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about unit rates, like finding the best price per item or speed.

subject: Math
description: Make tables of equivalent ratios relating quantities with whole-number measurements, find missing values in the tables, and plot the pairs of values on the coordinate plane. Use tables to compare ratios.
description_plain: Your child can make tables showing equivalent ratios with whole numbers, find missing numbers, and graph these pairs to compare ratios.

subject: Math
description: Use ratio and rate reasoning to solve real-world and mathematical problems, e.g., by reasoning about tables of equivalent ratios, tape diagrams, double number line diagrams, or equations.
description_plain: Your child can use ratios and rates to solve real-life and math problems by working with tables, diagrams, or equations that show equivalent ratios.

subject: Math
description: Apply the concept of a unit rate a/b associated with a ratio a:b with b≠0, and use rate language in the context of a ratio relationship.
description_plain: Your child understands what a unit rate is and can use rate words to describe relationships between two quantities.

subject: Math
description: Apply the concept of a ratio and use ratio language to describe a ratio relationship between two quantities.
description_plain: Your child can explain what a ratio is and use ratio words to describe how two amounts compare.

subject: Math
description: Understand ratio concepts and use ratio reasoning to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child understands ratios and can use them to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child looks for and explains patterns when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child notices and uses the structure of problems to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child chooses and uses tools in smart ways to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their thinking and listen to others to understand math better.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about numbers and ideas in different ways to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child tries to understand problems and keeps working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Apply geometric methods to solve design problems (e.g., designing an object or structure to satisfy physical constraints or minimize cost; working with typographic grid systems based on ratios).
description_plain: Your child can use geometry to solve design problems, like creating objects or structures that meet certain limits or costs.

subject: Math
description: Apply geometric methods to solve design problems (e.g., designing an object or structure to satisfy physical constraints or minimize cost; working with typographic grid systems based on ratios).
description_plain: Your child can use geometry to solve design problems, like creating objects or structures that meet certain limits or costs.

subject: Math
description: Apply geometric methods to solve design problems (e.g., designing an object or structure to satisfy physical constraints or minimize cost; working with typographic grid systems based on ratios).
description_plain: Your child can use geometry to solve design problems, like creating objects or structures that meet certain limits or costs.

subject: Math
description: Apply geometric methods to solve design problems (e.g., designing an object or structure to satisfy physical constraints or minimize cost; working with typographic grid systems based on ratios).
description_plain: Your child can use geometry to solve design problems, like creating objects or structures that meet certain limits or costs.

subject: Math
description: Apply concepts of density based on area and volume in modeling situations (e.g., persons per square mile, BTUs per cubic foot).
description_plain: Your child can use ideas about density based on area and volume to model real situations, like people per square mile or energy per cubic foot.

subject: Math
description: Apply concepts of density based on area and volume in modeling situations (e.g., persons per square mile, BTUs per cubic foot).
description_plain: Your child can use ideas about density based on area and volume to model real situations, like people per square mile or energy per cubic foot.

subject: Math
description: Apply concepts of density based on area and volume in modeling situations (e.g., persons per square mile, BTUs per cubic foot).
description_plain: Your child can use ideas about density based on area and volume to model real situations, like people per square mile or energy per cubic foot.

subject: Math
description: Apply concepts of density based on area and volume in modeling situations (e.g., persons per square mile, BTUs per cubic foot).
description_plain: Your child can use ideas about density based on area and volume to model real situations, like people per square mile or energy per cubic foot.

subject: Math
description: Use geometric shapes, their measures, and their properties to describe objects (e.g., modeling a tree trunk or a human torso as a cylinder).
description_plain: Your child can use shapes and their measurements to describe real objects, like modeling a tree trunk as a cylinder.

subject: Math
description: Use geometric shapes, their measures, and their properties to describe objects (e.g., modeling a tree trunk or a human torso as a cylinder).
description_plain: Your child can use shapes and their measurements to describe real objects, like modeling a tree trunk as a cylinder.

subject: Math
description: Use geometric shapes, their measures, and their properties to describe objects (e.g., modeling a tree trunk or a human torso as a cylinder).
description_plain: Your child can use shapes and their measurements to describe real objects, like modeling a tree trunk as a cylinder.

subject: Math
description: Use geometric shapes, their measures, and their properties to describe objects (e.g., modeling a tree trunk or a human torso as a cylinder).
description_plain: Your child can use shapes and their measurements to describe real objects, like modeling a tree trunk as a cylinder.

subject: Math
description: Apply geometric concepts in modeling situations.
description_plain: Your child can use geometry ideas to create models of real-world situations.

subject: Math
description: Apply geometric concepts in modeling situations.
description_plain: Your child can use geometry ideas to create models of real-world situations.

description_plain: Your child can use geometry ideas to create models of real-world situations.

description: Apply geometric concepts in modeling situations.
description_plain: Your child can use geometry ideas to create models of real-world situations.

subject: Math
description: Identify the shapes of two-dimensional cross-sections of three-dimensional objects, and identify three-dimensional objects generated by rotations of two-dimensional objects.
description_plain: Your child can identify the shapes you get when slicing 3D objects and recognize 3D objects made by rotating 2D shapes.

subject: Math
description: Identify the shapes of two-dimensional cross-sections of three-dimensional objects, and identify three-dimensional objects generated by rotations of two-dimensional objects.
description_plain: Your child can identify the shapes you get when slicing 3D objects and recognize 3D objects made by rotating 2D shapes.

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child can identify the shapes you get when slicing 3D objects and recognize 3D objects made by rotating 2D shapes.

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child can identify the shapes you get when slicing 3D objects and recognize 3D objects made by rotating 2D shapes.

subject: Math
description: Visualize relationships between two-dimensional and three-dimensional objects.
description_plain: Your child can picture how 2D shapes and 3D objects are related.

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child can picture how 2D shapes and 3D objects are related.

subject: Math
description: Use volume formulas for cylinders, pyramids, cones, and spheres to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of cylinders, pyramids, cones, and spheres to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Use volume formulas for cylinders, pyramids, cones, and spheres to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of cylinders, pyramids, cones, and spheres to solve problems.

description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of cylinders, pyramids, cones, and spheres to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: (+) Give an informal argument using Cavalieri's principle for the formulas for the volume of a sphere and other solid figures.
description_plain: Your child can explain informally why the volume formulas for spheres and other solids work using Cavalieri's principle.

subject: Math
description: (+) Give an informal argument using Cavalieri's principle for the formulas for the volume of a sphere and other solid figures.
description_plain: Your child can explain informally why the volume formulas for spheres and other solids work using Cavalieri's principle.

description_plain: Your child can explain informally why the volume formulas for spheres and other solids work using Cavalieri's principle.

description_plain: Your child can explain informally why the volume formulas for spheres and other solids work using Cavalieri's principle.

subject: Math
description: Give an informal argument for the formulas for the circumference of a circle, area of a circle, volume of a cylinder, pyramid, and cone.
description_plain: Your child can explain informally the formulas for the circumference and area of a circle, and the volume of cylinders, pyramids, and cones.

subject: Math
description: Give an informal argument for the formulas for the circumference of a circle, area of a circle, volume of a cylinder, pyramid, and cone.
description_plain: Your child can explain informally the formulas for the circumference and area of a circle, and the volume of cylinders, pyramids, and cones.

description_plain: Your child can explain informally the formulas for the circumference and area of a circle, and the volume of cylinders, pyramids, and cones.

description_plain: Your child can explain informally the formulas for the circumference and area of a circle, and the volume of cylinders, pyramids, and cones.

subject: Math
description: Explain volume formulas and use them to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can explain volume formulas and use them to solve problems.

description: Explain that a function is a correspondence from one set (called the domain) to another set (called the range) that assigns to each element of the domain exactly one element of the range. If f is a function and x is an element of its domain, then f(x) denotes the output of f corresponding to the input x. The graph of f is the graph of the equation y=f(x).
description_plain: Your child understands that a function pairs each input with exactly one output, and can explain how the function’s graph shows this relationship.
