subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.
description_plain: With help from adults and friends, your child can plan, revise, and edit their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas to fit the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (for example: presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (for example: small-group discussion).
description_plain: Your child can tell when to use formal language, like when presenting ideas, and when informal talk is okay, like in small groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose punctuation for effect.
description_plain: Your child can choose punctuation marks to make their writing more effective.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases to convey ideas precisely.
description_plain: Your child can pick words and phrases that clearly express their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses the rules of language when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly for their grade and use references to help when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma before a coordinating conjunction in a compound sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use a comma before words like 'and' or 'but' in compound sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks to mark direct speech and quotations from a text.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing speech or quotes from a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly use frequently confused words (for example: to, too, two; there, their)
description_plain: Your child can correctly use words that sound alike but have different meanings, like to, too, and two.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate fragments and run-ons.
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix sentence fragments and run-on sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete simple, compound, and complex sentences.
description_plain: Your child can write complete simple, compound, and complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use compound subjects (for example: Tom and Pat went to the store) and compound verbs (for example: Harry thought and worried about the things he said to Jane) to create sentence fluency in writing
description_plain: Your child can use sentences with compound subjects and verbs to make writing flow smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use prepositional phrases.
description_plain: Your child can create and use prepositional phrases in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Order adjectives within sentences according to conventional patterns (for example: a small red bag rather than a red small bag).
description_plain: Your child can put adjectives in the right order, like saying 'a small red bag' instead of 'a red small bag.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use modal auxiliaries (for example: can, may, must) to convey various conditions.
description_plain: Your child can use helping words like can, may, and must to show different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the progressive (for example: I was walking; I am walking; I will be walking) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can use verb forms that show ongoing actions, like 'I am walking' or 'I was walking.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use relative pronouns (who, whose, whom, which, that) and relative adverbs (where, when, why).
description_plain: Your child can use words like who, which, where, and when to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand why and how writers use the conventions of Standard English grammar, usage, and mechanics to clarify their meaning.
description_plain: Your child understands how using correct grammar and writing rules helps make their meaning clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that fits the story or events they told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child can use clear words and details that appeal to the senses to describe experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words and phrases to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and description to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child can use dialogue and descriptions to show what happens and how characters feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene and introducing characters, then tell events in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write engaging, real or imagined narratives using descriptive details and dialogue to convey a sequence of related events.
description_plain: Your child can write interesting stories, real or imagined, using details and dialogue to show a series of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing sentence or section that relates to the information they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within categories of information using words and phrases (for example: another, for example, also, because).
description_plain: Your child can connect ideas in writing using words like 'another,' 'for example,' 'also,' and 'because.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child will add facts, definitions, details, or examples to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize relevant ideas and details to convey a central idea or prove a point.
description_plain: Your child can organize ideas and details to explain a main point or idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a text structure appropriate to purpose (sequence, chronology, description, explanation, and comparison-and-contrast).
description_plain: Your child understands different ways to organize writing, like telling events in order or comparing things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly and group related information in paragraphs and sections; include formatting (for example: headings), illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic clearly and group related information into paragraphs, using headings or pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts using text structures appropriate for the purpose and developed through facts, definitions, concrete details, precise language, and domain-specific vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child writes clear informational texts using facts and specific words, organized in a way that fits the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing sentence or section that relates to their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words and phrases (for example: for instance, in order to, in addition).
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'for instance' or 'in addition' to connect their opinion and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons supported by facts and details to explain their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which related ideas are grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, state their opinion, and organize ideas to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts supporting a point of view with reasons and information.
description_plain: Your child writes opinion pieces that share their point of view with reasons and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal precise actions, emotions, or states of being (for example: quizzed, whined, stammered) and that are basic to a particular topic (for example: wildlife, conservation, and endangered when discussing animal preservation).
description_plain: Your child learns and uses grade-level words that describe actions, feelings, or topics clearly, like 'quizzed' or 'wildlife.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of words by relating them to their opposites (antonyms) and to words with similar but not identical meanings (synonyms).
description_plain: Your child understands words by knowing their opposites and similar words with slightly different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child can explain common sayings, like idioms and proverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the meaning of simple similes and metaphors (for example, as pretty as a picture) in context.
description_plain: Your child understands simple comparisons like similes and metaphors in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language and how words relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (for example: dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries or thesauruses, in books or online, to find word meanings and pronunciations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Infer meaning of words using explanations offered within a text.
description_plain: Your child figures out word meanings by using clues from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read multisyllabic words with and without inflectional and derivational suffixes
description_plain: Your child can read longer words, including those with endings like -ed or -ing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand words that change spelling to show past tense (for example: write/wrote, catch/caught, teach/taught)
description_plain: Your child reads and understands words that change spelling in the past tense, like 'write' and 'wrote.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand words with common prefixes (for example: un-, re-, dis-) and derivational suffixes ( for example: -ful, -ly, -ness)
description_plain: Your child can read and understand words that have common beginnings like un-, re-, dis- and endings like -ful, -ly, and -ness.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (for example: telegraph, photograph, autograph).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek and Latin parts of words, like tele- or photo-, to figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (for example: definitions, examples, or restatements in text) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the sentences around a word, like definitions or examples, to understand what the word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 4 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different helpful strategies while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child checks if they read a word correctly by using clues from the sentence and rereads if needed to understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression.
description_plain: Your child reads stories and poems out loud clearly, at a good speed, and with feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level books and stories with a clear purpose and understands what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (for example, roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about sounds, syllables, and word parts to read new longer words correctly, both in and out of stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses phonics and word study skills to figure out how to read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of spelling patterns (orthography) and word meanings (morphology) to decode multisyllable words and determine the meaning of unknown words.
description_plain: Your child uses spelling patterns and word meanings to read longer words and understand new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in the grades 4–5 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand nonfiction books about history, science, and other subjects at their grade level, with some help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Range of Reading and Complexity of Text to:
description_plain: Your child reads a variety of books and texts that get harder as they grow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from two texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can use information from two different texts about the same topic to write or talk about it clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and facts to support their ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented visually, orally, or quantitatively (for example: in charts, graphs, diagrams, time lines, animations, or interactive elements on Web pages) and explain how the information contributes to an understanding of the text in which it appears.
description_plain: Your child understands information shown in pictures, charts, or graphs and can explain how it helps explain the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Integration of Knowledge and Ideas to:
description_plain: Your child uses different kinds of information and ideas together to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event or topic; describe the differences in focus and the information provided.
description_plain: Your child can compare a story told by someone who saw it and one told by someone who did not, and explain how they are different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure (for example, chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in a text or part of a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a text is organized, like by time order, cause and effect, or problem and solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words or phrases in a text relevant to a grade 4 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words related to their grade 4 subjects or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain events, procedures, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text, including what happened and why, based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain what happened and why in history, science, or technical texts by using specific information from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text and explain how it is supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how key details support it, and summarize what the text is about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says clearly and to make guesses about what is not directly stated.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply strategies to comprehend and interpret informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses different strategies to understand and explain informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read familiar texts orally with fluency, accuracy, and prosody (expression).
description_plain: Your child can read familiar texts out loud smoothly, correctly, and with expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, in the grades 4-5 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems that are suitable for grades 4-5, with some help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Range of Reading and Complexity of Text to:
description_plain: Your child reads a variety of texts with different levels of difficulty.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the treatment of similar themes and topics (for example: opposition of good and evil) and patterns of events (for example: the quest) in stories, myths, and traditional literature from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare how similar themes and story patterns appear in stories, myths, and traditional tales from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make connections between the text of a story or drama and a visual or oral presentation of the text, identifying where each version reflects specific descriptions and directions in the text.
description_plain: Your child can connect a story or play with its visual or oral version by pointing out where they show the same details and directions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Integration of Knowledge and Ideas to:
description_plain: Your child uses information from different sources to understand stories and ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first- and third-person narrations.
description_plain: Your child can tell how stories are different when told from different points of view, like first-person or third-person.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between poems, drama, and prose, and refer to the structural elements of poems (for example: verse, rhythm, meter) and drama (for example, casts of characters, settings, descriptions, dialogue, stage directions) when writing or speaking about a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main differences between poems, plays, and regular stories, and talk about parts like rhythm in poems or characters and dialogue in plays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including those that allude to significant characters found in mythology (for example: Herculean).
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special words that refer to famous characters from myths.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the development of plot (such as the origin of the central conflict, the action of the plot, and how the conflict is resolved).
description_plain: Your child can describe how the plot of a story develops, including the problem, the events, and how the problem is solved.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (for example: a character's thoughts, words, or actions).
description_plain: Your child can describe a character, place, or event in a story or play using specific details like what a character thinks, says, or does.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the theme of a story, play, or poem using details from the text and summarize what it is about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says clearly and to make guesses about what is not directly stated.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply strategies to comprehend and interpret literary texts.
description_plain: Your child uses different strategies to understand and explain stories, plays, and poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (for example: presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (for example: small-group discussion); use formal English when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child knows when to use formal English, like when giving a presentation, and when informal talk is okay, like in small group chats.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add audio recordings and visual displays to presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can add audio or pictures to presentations to help explain main ideas or themes better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience in an organized manner, using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story or share information about a topic in an organized way, using facts and details, and speak clearly so others understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create a plan to effectively present information both informally and formally.
description_plain: Your child will make a plan to share information clearly, whether in casual talks or formal presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker provides to support particular points
description_plain: Your child understands how to find reasons and evidence a speaker uses to support their points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase portions of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can restate parts of a story or information they hear or see in different ways, like reading aloud or watching videos.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child reviews main ideas from discussions and explains their own thoughts based on what was talked about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions to clarify or follow up on information, and make comments that contribute to the discussion and link to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand better and adds helpful comments that connect to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child follows rules during group talks and does the jobs they are given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready, having read or studied the topic, and uses that knowledge to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 4 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group talks, listens to others, builds on their ideas, and shares their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose thoughtful questions after actively listening to others.
description_plain: Your child listens carefully and asks thoughtful questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experience or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child remembers things from experience or finds information to answer a question.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information and check various information sources for accuracy (for example: In a class discussion focused on butterflies, students ask questions related to a butterfly and the life cycle.).
description_plain: Your child gathers information and checks different sources to make sure the facts are correct.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of resources to answer questions of interest through guided inquiry (for example: texts read aloud or viewed, direct observation).
description_plain: Your child uses different resources, like books or watching, to find answers to questions with guidance.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask primary questions of clarity, significance, relevance, and accuracy to improve quality of thinking.
description_plain: Your child asks clear and important questions to help them think better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask a specific question and gather relevant information from various sources related to that question that inform clarity of purpose and conclusions about research.
description_plain: Your child asks a specific question and finds information from different places to help understand and learn.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a clear purpose for research or inquiry (for example: If the class is learning about trees, is my need to know more about pets related?).
description_plain: Your child knows why they are learning about something and can tell if it fits with what they want to know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (for example: explore a number of books by a favorite author and express opinions about them).
description_plain: Your child joins in group projects where they explore books or topics and share their thoughts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explore the purposes for research and inquiry by accessing resources in collaborative settings.
description_plain: Your child learns why we ask questions and look for information by working with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell simple words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of sound-letter relationships.
description_plain: Your child spells simple words by sounding them out using what they know about letters and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write a letter or letters for most consonant and short-vowel sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child writes letters for most consonant and short vowel sounds they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name end punctuation.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name punctuation marks at the end of sentences, like periods, question marks, and exclamation points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize the first word in a sentence and the pronoun I.
description_plain: Your child will capitalize the first word in a sentence and the pronoun I when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use capital letters, punctuation, and spelling correctly when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete sentences in shared language activities.
description_plain: Your child can say and make longer complete sentences during group language activities.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring prepositions (for example: to, from, in, out, on, off, for, of, by, with).
description_plain: Your child uses common small words that show location or direction, like to, from, in, out, on, and off.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand and use question words (interrogatives) (for example: who, what, where, when, why, how).
description_plain: Your child understands and uses question words like who, what, where, when, why, and how.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form regular plural nouns orally by adding /s/ or /es/ (for example: dog, dogs; wish, wishes).
description_plain: Your child can say plural nouns by adding sounds like /s/ or /es/, for example dogs or wishes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring nouns and verbs.
description_plain: Your child uses common nouns (naming words) and verbs (action words) when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print many upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can print many uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child understands basic grammar rules when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate mechanics and conventions when creating simple texts.
description_plain: Your child uses correct punctuation, capitalization, and spelling when writing simple sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child tries different digital tools to create and share writing, sometimes working with friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, respond to questions and suggestions from peers and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: With adult support, your child listens to friends’ ideas about their writing and adds details to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to narrate a single event or several loosely linked events, tell about the events in the order in which they occurred, and provide a reaction to what happened.
description_plain: Your child uses drawing, talking, and writing to tell about one or more events in the order they happened and shares how they feel about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write real or imagined narratives using labels, dictation, and drawing.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or made-up events using labels, talking, and drawing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child explores different digital tools to create and share writing, sometimes with friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, respond to questions and suggestions from peers and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: With adult help, your child listens to friends’ questions and ideas about their writing and adds details to improve it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose informative/explanatory texts in which they name what they are writing about and supply some information about the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses drawing, talking, and writing to make simple informative texts that name the topic and give some facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts on a topic using labels, dictation, and drawing.
description_plain: Your child writes simple informative texts about a topic using labels, talking, and drawing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With adult support, your child tries out different digital tools to create and share writing, sometimes working with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, respond to questions and suggestions from peers and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: Your child can answer questions and ideas from friends with help from adults and add more details to make their writing better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose opinion pieces in which they tell a reader the topic or the name of the book they are writing about and state an opinion or preference about the topic or book (for example: My favorite book is…).
description_plain: Your child will use drawing, talking, and writing to share their opinion about a topic or book, like saying what their favorite book is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinions using labels, dictation, and drawing.
description_plain: Your child understands how to write opinions using labels, talking, and drawing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the initial, medial, and final phoneme (speech sound) of spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can find and say the first, middle, and last sounds in spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and produce groups of words that begin with the same sound (alliteration).
description_plain: Your child can find and say groups of words that start with the same sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and manipulate sounds.
description_plain: Your child understands how to hear and change sounds in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring inflections and affixes (for example: -ed,-s, re-, un-, pre-, -ful, -less) as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word.
description_plain: Your child uses common word endings and beginnings (like -ed, -s, re-, un-) to help figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify new meanings for familiar words and apply them accurately (for example: knowing duck is a bird and learning the verb to duck).
description_plain: Your child learns new meanings for words they already know and uses them correctly, like knowing 'duck' is a bird and also a verb.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on kindergarten reading and content.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that look alike by listening to the different letter sounds.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Associate the long and short sounds with the common spellings (graphemes) for the five major vowels.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can change or add sounds in simple one-syllable words to make new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce the initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in three-phoneme (consonant-vowel-consonant, or CVC) words.
description_plain: Your child can say the first, middle vowel, and last sounds in three-letter words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend and segment the onset and rime of single-syllable spoken words.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can count, say, blend, and break apart the parts of words called syllables when listening.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can recognize and say words that sound the same at the end, like rhyming words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
