subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric, assessing the stance, premises, links among ideas, word choice, points of emphasis, and tone used.
description_plain: Your child evaluates a speaker’s opinion, reasoning, and use of evidence by looking at their ideas, word choices, and tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multiple sources of information presented in diverse formats and media (for example: visually, quantitatively, orally) in order to make informed decisions and solve problems, evaluating the credibility and accuracy of each source and noting any discrepancies among the data.
description_plain: Your child can use information from different types of sources like pictures, numbers, and spoken words to make smart choices and solve problems, checking if each source is trustworthy and noticing any differences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multiple sources of information presented in diverse formats and media (for example: visually, quantitatively, orally) in order to make informed decisions and solve problems, evaluating the credibility and accuracy of each source and noting any discrepancies among the data.
description_plain: Your child can use information from different types of sources like pictures, numbers, and spoken words to make smart choices and solve problems, checking if each source is trustworthy and noticing any differences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respond thoughtfully to diverse perspectives; synthesize comments, claims, and evidence made on all sides of an issue; resolve contradictions when possible; and determine what additional information or research is required to deepen the investigation or complete the task.
description_plain: Your child thinks carefully about different opinions, combines ideas and evidence from all sides, tries to fix any disagreements, and figures out what more they need to learn to understand the topic better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respond thoughtfully to diverse perspectives; synthesize comments, claims, and evidence made on all sides of an issue; resolve contradictions when possible; and determine what additional information or research is required to deepen the investigation or complete the task.
description_plain: Your child thinks carefully about different opinions, combines ideas and evidence from all sides, tries to fix any disagreements, and figures out what more they need to learn to understand the topic better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Propel conversations by posing and responding to questions that probe reasoning and evidence; ensure a hearing for a full range of positions on a topic or issue; clarify, verify, or challenge ideas and conclusions; and promote divergent and creative perspectives.
description_plain: Your child keeps conversations going by asking and answering questions that dig deeper into reasons and facts, listens to all viewpoints, clears up confusion, and encourages new and creative ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Propel conversations by posing and responding to questions that probe reasoning and evidence; ensure a hearing for a full range of positions on a topic or issue; clarify, verify, or challenge ideas and conclusions; and promote divergent and creative perspectives.
description_plain: Your child keeps conversations going by asking and answering questions that dig deeper into reasons and facts, listens to all viewpoints, clears up confusion, and encourages new and creative ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Work with peers to promote civil, democratic discussions and decision-making, set clear goals and deadlines, and establish individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child works with classmates to have respectful and fair discussions, sets clear goals and deadlines, and decides who does what.

subject: Language Arts
description: Work with peers to promote civil, democratic discussions and decision-making, set clear goals and deadlines, and establish individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child works with classmates to have respectful and fair discussions, sets clear goals and deadlines, and decides who does what.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read and researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence from texts and other research on the topic or issue to stimulate a thoughtful, well-reasoned exchange of ideas.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready by reading and researching the topic, and uses that information to share thoughtful and well-supported ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read and researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence from texts and other research on the topic or issue to stimulate a thoughtful, well-reasoned exchange of ideas.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready by reading and researching the topic, and uses that information to share thoughtful and well-supported ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Initiate and participate effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grades 11–12 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child starts and joins different kinds of group talks with classmates and teachers, listens to others' ideas, and shares their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Initiate and participate effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grades 11–12 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child starts and joins different kinds of group talks with classmates and teachers, listens to others' ideas, and shares their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow collaborative guidelines to ensure a hearing of a full range of positions on a topic or issue, and evaluate responses
description_plain: Your child follows group rules to make sure everyone’s opinions are heard and thinks carefully about the responses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow collaborative guidelines to ensure a hearing of a full range of positions on a topic or issue, and evaluate responses
description_plain: Your child follows group rules to make sure everyone’s opinions are heard and thinks carefully about the responses.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: Your child can remember things from their own experiences or find answers using information given by adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a clear and significant purpose for research. (for example: Is my purpose for researching frogs clear and is it important to understanding more about mammals?)
description_plain: Your child knows why they are researching something and understands if their reason is clear and important.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features (for example: titles, illustrations, headings, bold type) to locate, interpret, and use information.
description_plain: Your child uses parts of a book like titles, pictures, and headings to find and understand information.

subject: Language Arts
description: With peers, use a variety of resources (for example: direct observation, trade books, texts read aloud or viewed) to answer questions of interest through guided inquiry.
description_plain: Your child works with friends to use different resources like watching, reading, or listening to find answers to questions they are curious about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write or dictate questions for inquiry that arise during instruction.
description_plain: Your child writes or says questions that come up during lessons to learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects. For example: explore a number of "how-to" books on a given topic and use them to write a sequence of instructions.
description_plain: Your child takes part in group research and writing projects, like reading several 'how-to' books and writing steps to do something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and inquiry projects, writing, recalling, or gathering information to answer questions.
description_plain: Your child joins in group research and inquiry projects by writing, remembering, or finding information to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child uses computers or tablets to create and share writing, sometimes working together with friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, focus on a topic, respond to questions and suggestions from peers, and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: With adult support, your child focuses on a topic, listens to friends’ ideas and questions, and adds details to make their writing better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell untaught words phonetically, drawing on phonemic awareness and spelling conventions.
description_plain: Your child spells new words by sounding them out using what they know about sounds and spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for words with common spelling patterns and for frequently occurring irregular words.
description_plain: Your child spells common words correctly, including words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in dates and to separate single words in a series.
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly in dates and to separate words in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use end punctuation for sentences.
description_plain: Your child uses punctuation marks like periods, question marks, or exclamation points at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize dates and names of people.
description_plain: Your child can use capital letters for dates and people's names.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write complete simple sentences.
description_plain: Your child can write simple sentences that are complete.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete simple and compound declarative, interrogative, imperative, and exclamatory sentences in response to prompts.
description_plain: Your child can write and expand different types of sentences, like statements, questions, commands, and exclamations, when given a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring prepositions (for example: during, beyond, toward).
description_plain: Your child can use common words that show location or time, like during, beyond, and toward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use determiners (for example: articles, demonstratives).
description_plain: Your child can use words that point out or describe nouns, like articles and demonstratives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring conjunctions (for example: and, but, or, so, because).
description_plain: Your child can use common connecting words like and, but, or, so, and because.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring adjectives.
description_plain: Your child can use common describing words (adjectives).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs to convey a sense of past, present, and future (for example: Yesterday I walked home; Today I walk home; Tomorrow I will walk home).
description_plain: Your child can use verbs to talk about things that happened in the past, are happening now, or will happen in the future.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use personal, possessive, and indefinite pronouns (for example: I, me, my; they, them, their, anyone, everything).
description_plain: Your child can use words like I, me, my, they, them, their, anyone, and everything to replace nouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in basic sentences (for example: He hops; We hop).
description_plain: Your child can use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in simple sentences, like He hops and We hop.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, proper, and possessive nouns.
description_plain: Your child can use common nouns, proper nouns (names), and show possession with nouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print all upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can print all uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate grammar, spelling, capitalization, and punctuation.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar, spelling, capitalization, and punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can end a story or event with a clear ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include some details regarding what happened.
description_plain: Your child can include some details about what happened in a story or event.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount two or more appropriately sequenced events.
description_plain: Your child can tell two or more events in the right order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount real or imagined, sequenced events that include details and a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can tell real or made-up stories with details, events in order, and a clear ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can finish a story or event with a clear ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Supply some facts about the topic.
description_plain: Your child can share some facts about a topic they are writing about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts by naming a topic, providing related details, and giving the audience a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child will write a short report by naming a topic, giving details about it, and finishing with a closing sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that wraps up their thoughts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Supply a reason for the opinion.
description_plain: Your child can explain why they have a certain opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic or name the book they are writing about.
description_plain: Your child can start writing by introducing the topic or naming the book they are writing about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write an opinion supported by reasons
description_plain: Your child will write their opinion and give reasons to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using frequently occurring conjunctions to signal simple relationships (for example: because).
description_plain: Your child uses new words and phrases they learn from talking, reading, and listening, including words like 'because' to show simple connections.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs differing in manner (for example: look, peek, glance, stare, glare, scowl) and adjectives differing in intensity (for example: large, gigantic) by defining or choosing them or by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar action words (like look, peek, glance) and describing words (like large, gigantic) by explaining or acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (for example: note places at home that are cozy).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life things, like noticing cozy places at home.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define words by category and by one or more key attributes (for example: a duck is a bird that swims; a tiger is a large cat with stripes).
description_plain: Your child can explain what words mean by grouping them and describing important features, like saying a duck is a bird that swims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort words into categories (for example: colors, clothing) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can sort words into groups, like colors or clothes, to understand what those groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help, your child understands special language like metaphors, how words relate, and small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and understand compound words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and understand compound words made of two smaller words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify frequently occurring root words (for example: look) and their inflectional forms (for example: looks, looked, looking).
description_plain: Your child can find root words and their different forms, like look, looks, looked, and looking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring affixes as a clue to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child uses common beginnings or endings of words to help figure out what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the rest of the sentence to help understand the meaning of a new word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 1 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clear up the meaning of new or tricky words by using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the story to check if they read words right and can reread if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression.
description_plain: Your child reads aloud grade-level books clearly, at a good speed, and with expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level books with understanding and a purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension:
description_plain: Your child can read smoothly and accurately enough to understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Accurately decode unknown words that follow a predictable letter/sound relationship
description_plain: Your child can figure out new words by sounding out letters that follow common patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use onsets and rimes to create new words (for example: ip to make dip, lip, slip, ship)
description_plain: Your child can make new words by adding beginnings to word endings, like turning 'ip' into 'dip' or 'ship'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can read words that don’t follow usual spelling rules for their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read words with inflectional endings.
description_plain: Your child can read words with endings that change their meaning, like -ed or -ing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode two-syllable words following basic patterns by breaking the words into syllables.
description_plain: Your child can break two-syllable words into parts to read them correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge that every syllable must have a vowel sound to determine the number of syllables in a printed word.
description_plain: Your child knows that every syllable has a vowel sound and uses this to count syllables in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know final -e and common vowel team conventions for representing long vowel sounds.
description_plain: Your child understands that a final 'e' or vowel pairs often show long vowel sounds in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can read one-syllable words that are spelled the usual way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs (two letters that represent one sound).
description_plain: Your child knows that some letter pairs make one sound, like 'sh' or 'ch', and can use this when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills for their grade to sound out and understand words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Segment spoken single-syllable words into their complete sequence of individual sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child can break apart spoken one-syllable words into all their individual sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can say the first, middle vowel, and last sounds in spoken one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally produce single-syllable words by blending sounds (phonemes), including consonant blends.
description_plain: Your child can say whole one-syllable words by putting together individual sounds, including blends like 'bl' or 'st'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in spoken one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands how spoken words are made up of syllables and individual sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (for example: first word, capitalization, ending punctuation).
description_plain: Your child can recognize what makes a sentence, like starting with a capital letter and ending with punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print is organized and knows basic features like reading left to right.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refine foundational reading skills through understanding word structure, word relationships, and word families.
description_plain: Your child improves reading by learning how words are built, how they relate to each other, and word families.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read informational texts appropriately complex for grade 1.
description_plain: With help, your child can read simple informational texts that are right for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity to:
description_plain: Your child can read different kinds of books and stories that are right for their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic (for example: in illustrations, descriptions, or procedures).
description_plain: Your child can find simple ways two books about the same topic are alike or different, like in pictures or explanations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: Your child understands why the author gives reasons to support ideas in a book or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the illustrations and details in a text to describe its key ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and details in a book to explain the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Integration of Knowledge and Ideas to:
description_plain: Your child can put together information from different parts of a book or from two books to understand the ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between information provided by pictures or other illustrations and information provided by the words in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between information from pictures and information from the words in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (for example: headings, tables of contents, glossaries, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use parts of a book like headings, tables of contents, or glossaries to find important facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to help determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a text.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand the meaning of words and phrases in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Activate schema and background knowledge to construct meaning
description_plain: Your child uses what they already know to help understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two people, events, ideas, or facts in a book are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the main topic of a book and share important details about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about important details in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply foundational reading strategies to fluently read and comprehend informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses basic reading skills to read and understand books that give information.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read prose and poetry of appropriate complexity for grade 1.
description_plain: With help, your child reads stories and poems that are right for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity to:
description_plain: Your child reads different kinds of stories and poems that match their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in stories.
description_plain: Your child can compare what happens to characters in different stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use illustrations and details in a story to describe its characters, setting, or events.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and details in a story to talk about the characters, where it happens, or what happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Integration of Knowledge and Ideas to:
description_plain: Your child can put together information from pictures and words in a story to understand it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow and replicate patterns in predictable poems.
description_plain: Your child can follow and copy patterns in poems that repeat.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify who is telling the story at various points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is telling the story at different times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between books that tell stories and books that give information.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main differences between storybooks and books that give facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words and phrases in stories or poems that suggest feelings or appeal to the senses.
description_plain: Your child can find words in stories or poems that show feelings or help imagine sights, sounds, smells, tastes, or touch.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make predictions about what will happen in the text and explain whether they were confirmed or not and why, providing evidence from the text.
description_plain: Your child can guess what might happen next in a story and explain if their guess was right, using examples from the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters, settings, and major events in a story, using key details.
description_plain: Your child can describe the characters, places, and important events in a story using details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories, including key details, and demonstrate understanding of their central message or lesson.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories with important details and explain the main message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important parts of a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply foundational reading strategies to fluently read and comprehend literary texts.
description_plain: Your child can use basic reading skills to read stories smoothly and understand what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Give and follow simple two-step directions.
description_plain: Your child can listen to and follow simple directions that have two steps.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child can speak in complete sentences when it fits the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can add drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details, expressing ideas and feelings clearly.
description_plain: Your child can describe people, places, things, and events clearly using details and express their ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use multiple strategies to develop and expand oral communication.
description_plain: Your child can use different ways to improve how they talk and share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to gather additional information or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone says to learn more or understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in stories read aloud or information shared in other ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to clear up any confusion about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to clear up confusion about the topics or stories being talked about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by responding to the comments of others through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child can join in conversations by responding to what others say and keeping the talk going.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (for example: listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child can follow simple rules for group talks, like listening carefully and speaking one at a time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 1 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group conversations with classmates and adults about grade 1 topics and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicate using verbal and nonverbal language to express and receive information.
description_plain: Your child can use speaking and body language to share and understand information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (for example: "Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not").
description_plain: Your child can read nonfiction stories and explain the main arguments, telling which ideas have good reasons and evidence and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards to literature (for example: "Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres [for example: stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories] in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics").
description_plain: Your child can compare and talk about how different types of stories or poems handle similar ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find details in stories or information texts to explain their thoughts and support their research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources; assess the credibility of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and providing basic bibliographic information for sources.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from books and websites, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and refocusing the inquiry when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects by asking questions, using different sources, and changing their focus if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose research question(s), gather, synthesize, and credit relevant and credible resources, and present findings.
description_plain: Your child can ask research questions, gather and combine trustworthy information, give credit to sources, and share what they learned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: With some help from others, your child can plan, revise, edit, or try new ways to improve their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in grade level expectations 1 and 2 above.)
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain consistency in style and tone.
description_plain: Your child can keep the same style and tone throughout their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary sentence patterns for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can use different sentence types to make their writing more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly spell frequently used words and consult reference materials (for example: dictionaries, both print and digital, spell check, and/or trusted peers and/or adults) to determine the spelling of less frequent vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child can spell common words correctly and use tools like dictionaries or spell check to spell harder words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (commas, parentheses, dashes) to set off nonrestrictive/parenthetical elements.
description_plain: Your child can use commas, parentheses, and dashes correctly to add extra information in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize variations from Standard English in their own and others' writing and speaking, and identify and use strategies to improve expression in conventional language.
description_plain: Your child can notice when their own or others' speaking or writing doesn’t follow standard English and use ways to improve it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct vague pronouns (i.e., ones with unclear or ambiguous antecedents).
description_plain: Your child can find and fix unclear pronouns that don’t clearly show who or what they refer to.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in pronoun number and person.
description_plain: Your child can spot and correct when pronouns change in number or person in a confusing way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use intensive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child can use intensive pronouns like myself or ourselves correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure that pronouns are in the proper case (subjective, objective, and possessive).
description_plain: Your child can use pronouns in the right form depending on their role in the sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and word usage when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan, draft, edit, and revise as needed to craft clear and coherent writing that demonstrates a grasp of standard conventions for grammar, usage, and mechanics as well as a style appropriate for purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child can plan, write, and revise their work to make clear writing that uses proper grammar and fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use stylistic techniques (for example: alliteration, onomatopoeia, rhyme scheme); figurative language (for example: simile, metaphor, personification); and graphic elements (for example: capital letters, line length, word position) to express personal or narrative voice.
description_plain: Your child can use creative writing tools like alliteration, similes, and special formatting to show their own voice in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that clearly wraps up the story or experience they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can choose exact words and use details that help readers imagine what happened using their senses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child can use different words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child can use dialogue, pacing, and descriptions to develop characters and events in their stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene and introducing characters, then tell events in a clear, natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, details, and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write engaging real or imagined narratives using techniques such as sensory language, dialogue, description and sequencing to convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can write interesting stories using sensory details, dialogue, description, and clear sequencing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that sums up the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can keep a formal tone in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts. )
description_plain: Your child can use transition words to show how ideas and information connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, examples, and quotes to explain their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting (for example: headings), graphics (for example: charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic and organize information using methods like comparison or cause and effect, and use headings, charts, or videos to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and organized informative texts that explain ideas using relevant details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts characterized by appropriate organization, ample development, precise language and formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write well-organized and detailed informative texts using precise language and a formal style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that clearly follows from the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can keep a formal tone when writing arguments.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to clarify the relationships among claim(s) and reasons.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to clearly show how their reasons support their main point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with clear reasons and relevant evidence, using credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child will support their main ideas with clear reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) and organize the reasons and evidence clearly.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their main idea and organize their reasons and evidence in a clear way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments that support their main ideas using clear reasons and relevant evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments that support claim(s) using clear reasons, relevant evidence, credible sources, and a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments that support their main ideas with clear reasons, good evidence from trusted sources, and uses a formal writing style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new academic and subject-specific words correctly to help understand and express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (for example: stingy, scrimping, economical, unwasteful, and thrifty).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that have similar meanings but different feelings or associations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (for example: cause/effect, part/whole, item/category) to better understand each of the words.)
description_plain: Your child uses how words are related (like cause and effect or part and whole) to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (for example: personification) in context.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains figures of speech, like when something is described as if it were a person.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (for example: by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child checks the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at how it is used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (for example: dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in print or online, to find how to say a word or to understand its exact meaning and part of speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (for example: audience, auditory, audible).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek or Latin word parts to help figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (for example: the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the surrounding words and sentence clues to understand the meaning of a new word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 6 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using different strategies based on what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of word relationships, word structures, and sentence structures to determine the meaning of new words in context.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about word parts and sentence structure to understand new words in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6-8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand nonfiction books and articles for grades 6-8, with some help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Range of Reading and Complexity of Text to:
description_plain: Your child reads a variety of texts at different difficulty levels to improve their reading skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast one author's presentation of events with that of another (for example: a memoir written by and a biography on the same person).
description_plain: Your child can compare how two different authors tell the same story or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can follow and judge the arguments in a text, telling which claims have good reasons and evidence and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information presented in different media or formats (for example: visually, quantitatively) as well as in words to develop a coherent understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from pictures, numbers, and words to understand a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Integration of Knowledge and Ideas to:
description_plain: Your child uses different types of information to understand ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and explain how it is conveyed in the text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's opinion or reason for writing and explain how the author shows it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter, or section fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child understands how a sentence or section fits into the whole text and helps develop the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings.
description_plain: Your child figures out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or hidden meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail how a key individual, event, or idea is introduced, illustrated, and elaborated on in a text (for example: through examples or anecdotes).
description_plain: Your child looks closely at how an important person, event, or idea is introduced and explained in a text using examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child finds the main idea of a text, sees how details show it, and can summarize the text without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child uses parts of the text to support what the text says clearly and what can be understood by reading between the lines.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze organization and structure of informational text to make meaning.
description_plain: Your child looks at how informational texts are organized to understand their meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 6–8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems at a middle school level, with some help for harder parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Range of Reading and Complexity of Text to:
description_plain: Your child reads a wide range of texts and understands different levels of difficulty.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres (for example: stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories) in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child compares different types of texts, like stories and poems, to see how they handle similar topics or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the experience of reading a story, drama, or poem to listening to or viewing an audio, video, or live version of the text, including contrasting what they "see" and "hear" when reading the text to what they perceive when they listen or watch.
description_plain: Your child compares reading a story or poem to listening or watching it, noticing differences in what they see and hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Integration of Knowledge and Ideas to:
description_plain: Your child uses different kinds of information and ideas to understand texts better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author develops the point of view of the narrator or speaker in a text.
description_plain: Your child explains how an author shows the narrator's or speaker's point of view in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the theme, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child understands how a sentence, chapter, or scene fits into the whole text and helps develop the theme, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child figures out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special meanings, and explains how word choice affects the feeling or meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a particular story's or drama's plot unfolds in a series of episodes as well as how the characters respond or change as the plot moves toward a resolution.
description_plain: Your child describes how a story or play's plot happens step by step and how characters change as the story moves toward the ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child finds the main theme or idea of a text, sees how details show it, and can summarize the text without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child uses parts of the text to support what the text says clearly and what can be understood by reading between the lines.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze literary elements within different types of literature to make meaning.
description_plain: Your child can identify parts of stories and other writings to understand their meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use proper English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components (for example: graphics, images, music, sound) and visual displays in presentations to clarify information.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, sounds, or videos to presentations to help explain ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, sequencing ideas logically and using pertinent descriptions, facts, and details to accentuate main ideas or themes; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can share ideas in order, using facts and details, while speaking clearly and making eye contact.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop, organize, and present ideas and opinions effectively.
description_plain: Your child can organize and share their thoughts and opinions clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker’s argument and tell which parts have good reasons and evidence and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented in diverse media and formats (for example: visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how it contributes to a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can understand information shown in different ways, like pictures or numbers, and explain how it relates to the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and demonstrate understanding of multiple perspectives through reflection and paraphrasing.
description_plain: Your child understands main ideas and can explain different points of view by thinking about and restating them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions with elaboration and detail by making comments that contribute to the topic, text, or issue under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer detailed questions that help the discussion stay on topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, set specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow rules for group talks, set goals and deadlines, and know their role in the group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying, and uses evidence to think about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 6 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different kinds of group talks, listen to others, and share their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Employ appropriate presentation and collaboration strategies to meet the needs of a given task and purpose.
description_plain: Your child uses the right ways to present and work with others to complete tasks well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grades 9-10 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (for example: "Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is valid and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; identify false statements and fallacious reasoning").
description_plain: Your child can read nonfiction texts and decide if the arguments and evidence make sense and are true.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grades 9-10 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (for example: "Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is valid and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; identify false statements and fallacious reasoning").
description_plain: Your child can read nonfiction texts and decide if the arguments and evidence make sense and are true.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grades 9-10 Reading standards to literature (for example: "Analyze how an author draws on and transforms source material in a specific work [for example: how Shakespeare treats a theme or topic from Ovid or the Bible or how a later author draws on a play by Shakespeare]").
description_plain: Your child can see how authors use and change ideas from other works in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grades 9-10 Reading standards to literature (for example: "Analyze how an author draws on and transforms source material in a specific work [for example: how Shakespeare treats a theme or topic from Ovid or the Bible or how a later author draws on a play by Shakespeare]").
description_plain: Your child can see how authors use and change ideas from other works in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find evidence in stories or informational texts to support their thoughts and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find evidence in stories or informational texts to support their thoughts and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple authoritative sources, using advanced searches effectively; assess the usefulness of each source in answering the research question; integrate information into the text selectively to maintain the flow of ideas, avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find good information from trusted sources, decide what is useful, and include it properly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple authoritative sources, using advanced searches effectively; assess the usefulness of each source in answering the research question; integrate information into the text selectively to maintain the flow of ideas, avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find good information from trusted sources, decide what is useful, and include it properly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question) or solve a problem; narrow or broaden the inquiry when appropriate; synthesize multiple sources on the subject, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do research projects to answer questions or solve problems, adjusting the focus as needed and combining information from different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question) or solve a problem; narrow or broaden the inquiry when appropriate; synthesize multiple sources on the subject, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do research projects to answer questions or solve problems, adjusting the focus as needed and combining information from different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Synthesize multiple, authoritative literary and/or informational sources, creating cohesive research projects that show an understanding of the subject.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from many trusted sources to create clear research projects that show understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Synthesize multiple, authoritative literary and/or informational sources, creating cohesive research projects that show an understanding of the subject.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from many trusted sources to create clear research projects that show understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly, both over longer times with research and revision, and in shorter times for different purposes and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly, both over longer times with research and revision, and in shorter times for different purposes and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce, publish, and update individual or shared writing products, taking advantage of technology's capacity to link to other information and to display information flexibly and dynamically.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and the Internet to create, share, and update their own or group writing, including adding links and showing information in different ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce, publish, and update individual or shared writing products, taking advantage of technology's capacity to link to other information and to display information flexibly and dynamically.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and the Internet to create, share, and update their own or group writing, including adding links and showing information in different ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on addressing what is most significant for a specific purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ideas to make it better for a specific purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on addressing what is most significant for a specific purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ideas to make it better for a specific purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write and edit work so that it conforms to the guidelines in a style manual (for example: MLA Handbook, or APA handbook) appropriate for the discipline and writing type.
description_plain: Your child writes and edits their work following the rules of a style guide like MLA or APA, depending on the subject and type of writing.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child writes and edits their work following the rules of a style guide like MLA or APA, depending on the subject and type of writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and chooses words and styles that make their meaning clear when reading, writing, or listening.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and chooses words and styles that make their meaning clear when reading, writing, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly spell frequently used words and consult reference materials (for example: dictionaries, both print and digital, spell check, and/or trusted peers and/or adults) to determine the spelling of less frequent vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child spells common words correctly and uses dictionaries, spell check, or asks others to check the spelling of harder words.

description_plain: Your child uses different types of phrases and clauses to add clear meaning and make their writing or speaking more interesting.

description_plain: Your child uses creative tools like poetry, figurative language, images, or graphics to make their writing interesting and to share themes or ideas.

description_plain: Your child carefully reads and compares classic and modern stories, looking closely at story elements.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze traditional and contemporary literary texts with scrutiny and comparison of literary elements.
description_plain: Your child carefully reads and compares classic and modern stories, looking closely at story elements.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use feedback to evaluate and revise the presentation.
description_plain: Your child uses feedback to improve and change their presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use feedback to evaluate and revise the presentation.
description_plain: Your child uses feedback to improve and change their presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak to fit different situations and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak to fit different situations and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media (for example: textual, graphical, audio, visual, and interactive elements) in presentations to enhance understanding of findings, reasoning, and evidence and to add interest.
description_plain: Your child uses digital tools like pictures, sounds, and videos to make presentations clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media (for example: textual, graphical, audio, visual, and interactive elements) in presentations to enhance understanding of findings, reasoning, and evidence and to add interest.
description_plain: Your child uses digital tools like pictures, sounds, and videos to make presentations clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence clearly, concisely, and logically such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, substance, and style are appropriate to purpose, audience, and task.
description_plain: Your child shares information and ideas clearly and logically so listeners can easily understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence clearly, concisely, and logically such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, substance, and style are appropriate to purpose, audience, and task.
description_plain: Your child shares information and ideas clearly and logically so listeners can easily understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and develop credible presentations tailored to purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child plans and creates presentations that fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and develop credible presentations tailored to purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child plans and creates presentations that fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric, identifying any fallacious reasoning or exaggerated or distorted evidence.
description_plain: Your child can judge a speaker’s opinion and reasons, noticing if their arguments are wrong or their evidence is exaggerated.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric, identifying any fallacious reasoning or exaggerated or distorted evidence.
description_plain: Your child can judge a speaker’s opinion and reasons, noticing if their arguments are wrong or their evidence is exaggerated.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multiple sources of information presented in diverse media or formats (for example: visually, quantitatively, orally) evaluating the credibility and accuracy of each source.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from different types of media like videos, charts, and speeches, and decide if each source is trustworthy and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multiple sources of information presented in diverse media or formats (for example: visually, quantitatively, orally) evaluating the credibility and accuracy of each source.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from different types of media like videos, charts, and speeches, and decide if each source is trustworthy and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respond thoughtfully to diverse perspectives, summarize points of agreement and disagreement, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views and understanding and make new connections in light of the evidence and reasoning presented.
description_plain: Your child can listen to different opinions, explain where people agree or disagree, and explain their own ideas clearly using evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respond thoughtfully to diverse perspectives, summarize points of agreement and disagreement, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views and understanding and make new connections in light of the evidence and reasoning presented.
description_plain: Your child can listen to different opinions, explain where people agree or disagree, and explain their own ideas clearly using evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Propel conversations by posing and responding to questions that relate the current discussion to broader themes or larger ideas; actively incorporate others into the discussion; and clarify, verify, or challenge ideas and conclusions.
description_plain: Your child can keep conversations going by asking and answering questions that connect to bigger ideas, include others, and check or challenge what is said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Propel conversations by posing and responding to questions that relate the current discussion to broader themes or larger ideas; actively incorporate others into the discussion; and clarify, verify, or challenge ideas and conclusions.
description_plain: Your child can keep conversations going by asking and answering questions that connect to bigger ideas, include others, and check or challenge what is said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Work with peers to set rules for collegial discussions and decision-making (for example: informal consensus, taking votes on key issues, presentation of alternate views), clear goals and deadlines, and individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can work with classmates to set rules, goals, deadlines, and roles for group discussions and decisions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Work with peers to set rules for collegial discussions and decision-making (for example: informal consensus, taking votes on key issues, presentation of alternate views), clear goals and deadlines, and individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can work with classmates to set rules, goals, deadlines, and roles for group discussions and decisions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read and researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence from texts and other research on the topic or issue to stimulate a thoughtful, well-reasoned exchange of ideas.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading and researching, and uses evidence from what they learned to share thoughtful ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read and researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence from texts and other research on the topic or issue to stimulate a thoughtful, well-reasoned exchange of ideas.
