description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading and researching, and uses evidence from what they learned to share thoughtful ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Initiate and participate effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grades 9–10 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can start and join different kinds of group talks, listen to others, and clearly share their own ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Initiate and participate effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grades 9–10 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can start and join different kinds of group talks, listen to others, and clearly share their own ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respond to others' ideas, and evaluate perspective and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can respond to others' ideas and think about different points of view and how they are expressed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respond to others' ideas, and evaluate perspective and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can respond to others' ideas and think about different points of view and how they are expressed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from print and digital sources; take brief notes on sources and sort evidence into provided categories.
description_plain: Your child can remember information from experiences or find facts in books and online, take short notes, and organize the information into categories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present a brief report of the research findings to an audience.
description_plain: Your child can share a short report about what they learned with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop supporting visual information (for example: charts, maps, illustrations, models).
description_plain: Your child can create simple visuals like charts, maps, or drawings to help explain information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret and communicate the information learned by developing a brief summary with supporting details.
description_plain: Your child can explain what they learned by writing a short summary with important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge about a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather, interpret, and communicate information discovered during short research projects.
description_plain: Your child can find, understand, and share information from short research projects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over different amounts of time, like a few minutes or several days, for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use technology to produce and publish writing (using keyboarding skills) as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: With help, your child can use technology to write and share their work and work together with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.
description_plain: With help from adults and friends, your child can plan, revise, and edit their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose.
description_plain: With support, your child can write pieces that are organized and fit the purpose of the task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and observe differences between the conventions of spoken and written standard English. )
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between how we speak and how we write in standard English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases for effect.
description_plain: Your child can choose words and phrases that make their writing more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when they write, speak, read, or listen.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or other reference books to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use spelling patterns and generalizations (for example: word families, position-based spellings, syllable patterns, ending rules, meaningful word parts) in writing words.
description_plain: Your child uses spelling rules and patterns, like word families and syllables, to help spell words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for high-frequency and other studied words and for adding suffixes to base words (for example: sitting, smiled, cries, happiness).
description_plain: Your child spells common words and words with endings like -ing or -ed correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks in dialogue.
description_plain: Your child uses commas and quotation marks correctly when writing conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize appropriate words in titles.
description_plain: Your child knows when to use capital letters in titles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary sentence beginnings, and use long and short sentences to create sentence fluency in longer texts
description_plain: Your child starts sentences in different ways and uses both short and long sentences to make writing flow well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce simple, compound, and complex sentences using coordinating and subordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child writes simple, compound, and complex sentences using words like 'and,' 'but,' and 'because.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use coordinating and subordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child uses connecting words called conjunctions to join ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use comparative and superlative adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child uses words that compare things, like 'bigger' or 'biggest,' and knows when to use each.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure pronoun-antecedent agreement.
description_plain: Your child makes sure pronouns (like he, she, they) match the nouns they replace.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the simple (for example: I walked; I walk; I will walk) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child uses different verb tenses to talk about the past, present, and future.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular verbs.
description_plain: Your child uses both regular verbs (like 'walked') and irregular verbs (like 'ran') correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use abstract nouns (for example: childhood).
description_plain: Your child understands and uses nouns that name ideas or feelings, like 'childhood.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs in general and their functions in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child knows what nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs do in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a recursive process to plan, draft, revise, and edit writing, applying knowledge of the conventions of grammar, capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.
description_plain: Your child plans, writes, revises, and edits their work, using correct grammar, capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use temporal words and phrases to signal event order.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'first,' 'then,' and 'after' to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to develop experiences and events or show the response of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses dialogue and descriptions of actions and feelings to tell about events and how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a situation and introduce a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child sets up a story by introducing characters and telling events in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write real or imagined narratives that use descriptive details, have a clear sequence of events, and provide closure.
description_plain: Your child can write stories, real or made-up, that include clear details, happen in order, and have a good ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write a sentence or section that wraps up their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (for example: also, another, and, more, but) to connect ideas within categories of information.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'also,' 'and,' or 'but' to connect ideas in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, and details.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, and details to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic and group related information together; include illustrations when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic, group related information together, and add pictures when they help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts developed with facts, definitions, and details, ending with a related concluding statement.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain topics using facts and details, and finish with a closing sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write a sentence or section that finishes their opinion writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (for example: because, therefore, since, for example) to connect opinion and reasons.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'because' or 'for example' to connect their opinion with reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that support the opinion.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons to support their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic or text they are writing about, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure that includes reasons.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic, say their opinion, and organize their writing with reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that share their point of view and explain why they think that way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate conversational, general academic, and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal spatial and temporal relationships (for example: After dinner that night we went looking for them).
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words and phrases that help describe time and place in conversations and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words that describe states of mind or degrees of certainty (for example: knew, believed, suspected, heard, wondered).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar words that show feelings or how sure someone is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (for example: describe people who are friendly or helpful).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life examples, like describing friendly or helpful people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the literal and nonliteral meanings of words and phrases in context (for example: take steps).
description_plain: Your child can understand when words mean exactly what they say and when they mean something different, like in sayings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language and how words can have different meanings and relationships.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 3 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words in texts about grade 3 topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries or beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or glossaries, in books or online, to find the meaning of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (for example: company, companion).
description_plain: Your child can use a word they know to help understand a new word that has the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of word relationships to identify antonyms or synonyms to clarify meaning.
description_plain: Your child can use words that mean the same or the opposite to help understand new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known affix is added to a known word (for example: agreeable/disagreeable, comfortable/uncomfortable, care/careless, heat/preheat).
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of a new word when a common prefix or suffix is added to a word they already know, like turning 'agreeable' into 'disagreeable.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the rest of the sentence to help understand the meaning of a tricky word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning word and phrases based on grade 3 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clear up the meaning of unknown words or words with more than one meaning by using different helpful strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the sentence to check if they read a word right and can go back and reread if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression.
description_plain: Your child reads stories and poems out loud with correct words, at a good speed, and with feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level books with a clear purpose and understands what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common Latin suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can read words that have common Latin endings like -tion or -able.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and know the meaning of the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes.
description_plain: Your child knows common beginnings and endings of words and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word parts to sound out and understand words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of spelling patterns (orthography), word meanings (morphology), and word relationships to decode words and increase vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child uses spelling patterns, word meanings, and how words relate to each other to read new words and learn new vocabulary.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, at the high end of the grades 2–3 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of third grade, your child can read and understand nonfiction books about history, science, and other subjects on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Range of Reading and Complexity of Text to:
description_plain: Your child reads a variety of books and texts that are just right for their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points and key details presented in two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain the main ideas and important details when reading two books about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the logical connection between particular sentences and paragraphs in a text (for example: comparison, cause/effect, first/second/third in a sequence).
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs connect, like showing cause and effect or the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from illustrations (for example: maps, photographs) and the words in a text to demonstrate understanding of the text (for example: where, when, why, and how key events occur).
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and words in a book to understand important details like where and when things happen.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Integration of Knowledge and Ideas to:
description_plain: Your child uses what they learn from pictures and words together to understand the story or information better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use semantic cues and signal words (for example: because and although) to identify cause/effect and compare/contrast relationships.
description_plain: Your child uses clue words like 'because' and 'although' to find cause and effect or compare and contrast in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the author of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own opinion and the author’s point of view in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features and search tools (for example: key words, sidebars, hyperlinks) to locate information relevant to a given topic efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use things like keywords, sidebars, and links to quickly find information about a topic in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 3 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words related to a third-grade topic when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the relationship between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text, using language that pertains to time, sequence, and cause/effect.
description_plain: Your child can explain how events or ideas in a text are connected by time, order, or cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a main topic of a multi-paragraph text as well as the focus of specific paragraphs within the text
description_plain: Your child can find the main topic of a text with many paragraphs and also understand what each paragraph focuses on.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text; recount the key details and explain how they support the main idea.
description_plain: Your child can tell the main idea of a text, remember key details, and explain how those details support the main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a text by looking back at the text for answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply strategies to fluently read and comprehend various informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can use different strategies to read and understand informational texts smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade level text accurately and fluently, attending to phrasing, intonation, and punctuation.
description_plain: Your child can read third-grade level texts clearly and smoothly, paying attention to phrasing, tone, and punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, at the high end of the grades 2-3 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of third grade, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems that are a bit challenging for their grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Range of Reading and Complexity of Text to:
description_plain: Your child can read a variety of texts with different levels of difficulty.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the themes, settings, and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters (for example: in books from a series).
description_plain: Your child can compare and tell the differences and similarities in themes, settings, and plots of stories by the same author about similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific aspects of a text's illustrations contribute to what is conveyed by the words in a story (for example: create mood, emphasize aspects of a character or setting).
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures in a story help show feelings, characters, or settings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Integration of Knowledge and Ideas to:
description_plain: Your child can connect ideas and information from different parts of a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the narrator or those of the characters.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own opinion and the opinions of the story’s narrator or characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to parts of stories, dramas, and poems when writing or speaking about a text, using terms such as chapter, scene, and stanza; describe how each successive part builds on earlier sections.
description_plain: Your child can talk or write about parts of stories, plays, and poems using words like chapter, scene, and stanza, and explain how each part adds to the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use signal words (such as before, after, next) and text structure (narrative, chronology) to determine the sequence of major events
description_plain: Your child can use words like before, after, and next to figure out the order of important events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, distinguishing literal from nonliteral language.
description_plain: Your child can understand the meaning of words in a text and tell when words are used literally or in a different way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters in a story (for example: their traits, motivations, or feelings) and explain how their actions contribute to the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can describe characters’ traits, feelings, and reasons for acting, and explain how their actions move the story along.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe and draw inferences about the elements of plot, character, and setting in literary pieces, poems, and plays.
description_plain: Your child can describe and make guesses about the plot, characters, and setting in stories, poems, and plays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize central ideas and important details from a text.
description_plain: Your child can summarize the main ideas and important details from a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables, folktales, and myths from diverse cultures; determine the central message, lesson, or moral and explain how it is conveyed through key details in the text.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories like fables and myths from different cultures and explain the main lesson or message using important details from the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of comprehension strategies to interpret text (attending, searching, predicting, checking, and self-correcting).
description_plain: Your child uses different ways to understand what they read, like paying close attention, looking for clues, guessing what will happen next, checking their ideas, and fixing mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about a story or text, using parts of the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply strategies to fluently read and comprehend various literary texts.
description_plain: Your child uses strategies to read smoothly and understand different kinds of stories and books.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak in complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child speaks in full sentences when needed to give details or explain something clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create engaging audio recordings of stories or poems that demonstrate fluid reading at an understandable pace; add visual displays when appropriate to emphasize or enhance certain facts or details.
description_plain: Your child makes interesting audio recordings of stories or poems, reading clearly and at a good speed, and adds pictures when it helps explain important points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select and organize ideas sequentially or around major points of information that relate to the formality of the audience.
description_plain: Your child picks and arranges ideas in order or by main points, thinking about who they are talking to.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak clearly, using appropriate volume and pitch for the purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child speaks clearly using the right volume and tone for the situation and listeners.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish different levels of formality.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses different levels of formality when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child shares information, tells stories, or talks about experiences with clear facts and details, speaking clearly and at a good pace.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicate using appropriate language in informal and formal situations.
description_plain: Your child uses the right kind of language when talking in both casual and formal situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about information from a speaker, offering appropriate elaboration and detail.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about what someone says, adding details when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main ideas and supporting details of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas and important details from stories read aloud or information shown in pictures, numbers, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child explains their own thoughts and what they understand during group talks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to check understanding of information presented, stay on topic, and link their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child asks questions to make sure they understand, stays on topic, and connects their ideas to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (for example: gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child follows rules for group talks, like waiting their turn, listening carefully, and speaking one at a time about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready by reading or studying ahead and uses what they learned to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 3 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group talks, listens to others, and shares their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate cooperatively in group activities.
description_plain: Your child works well with others in group activities.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 8 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (for example: "Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; recognize when irrelevant evidence is introduced").
description_plain: Your child reads nonfiction texts and can explain the main argument, check if the reasons and evidence make sense, and notice when information is not relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 8 Reading standards to literature (for example: "Analyze how a modern work of fiction draws on themes, patterns of events, or character types from myths, traditional stories, or religious works such as the Bible, including describing how the material is rendered new").
description_plain: Your child can explain how a modern story uses ideas, events, or characters from old myths, traditional tales, or religious stories, and show how the story makes these ideas new.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find details in stories or information texts to support their thoughts, reflections, or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from books and online, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use that information correctly without copying, giving credit to the original authors.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer questions they come up with, using several sources and asking more focused questions to explore the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose important questions; identify, locate, and evaluate sources; extract and synthesize relevant information, and communicate findings appropriately.
description_plain: Your child can ask important questions, find and judge sources, pick out useful information, and share what they learn in the right way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over longer periods (with time to research and revise) and shorter periods (like in one sitting) for different subjects, reasons, and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas efficiently as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, share their work, show how ideas connect, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help from others, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to make sure it fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs in the active and passive voice and in the conditional and subjunctive mood to achieve particular effects (for example: emphasizing the actor or the action; expressing uncertainty or describing a state contrary to fact).
description_plain: Your child can use different verb forms like active or passive voice and special moods to highlight who is doing something or to show uncertainty or imaginary situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly spell frequently used words and consult reference materials (for example: dictionaries, both print and digital, spell check, and/or trusted peers and/or adults) to determine the spelling of less frequent vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child can spell common words correctly and use tools like dictionaries or spell check to find the spelling of harder words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an ellipsis to indicate an omission.
description_plain: Your child can use three dots (an ellipsis) to show that some words have been left out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (comma, ellipsis, dash) to indicate a pause or break.
description_plain: Your child can use punctuation like commas, ellipses, and dashes to show pauses or breaks in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb voice and mood.
description_plain: Your child can notice and fix when verb voice or mood changes in a way that doesn’t fit.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the indicative, imperative, interrogative, conditional, and subjunctive mood.
description_plain: Your child can form and use verbs to make statements, give commands, ask questions, express conditions, or show wishes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the active and passive voice.
description_plain: Your child can form and use verbs in active voice (showing who does the action) and passive voice (showing the action done to someone or something).

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of verbals (gerunds, participles, infinitives) in general and their function in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child can explain what verbals (words like gerunds, participles, and infinitives) do in sentences and how they work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent final drafts that demonstrate a command of the conventions for grammar, usage, and mechanics as well as a style appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write final drafts that are clear and follow grammar and punctuation rules, using a style that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use stylistic techniques (for example: alliteration, onomatopoeia); figurative language (for example: simile, metaphor, personification, hyperbole); and graphic elements (for example: capital letters, line length, word position) to express personal or narrative voice.
description_plain: Your child uses creative language like alliteration, similes, and special text features to show their personal or story voice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that sums up and reflects on the experiences or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words, detailed descriptions, and sensory language to clearly show actions and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence, signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another, and show the relationships among experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, description, and reflection, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child uses storytelling techniques like dialogue and description to develop events and characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing characters, then organizes events in a natural, logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, clear details, and well-organized events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write engaging real or imagined narratives effectively using techniques such as relevant and sufficient descriptive details, sensory language, logical pacing and dialogue to detail actions and to develop and reflect on experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child writes interesting stories with enough details, sensory words, good pacing, and dialogue to explain actions and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports and follows the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes using a formal tone appropriate for informative or explanatory writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain or inform about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words to connect ideas and make their writing clear and easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child develops their topic using relevant facts, definitions, examples, and quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories; include formatting (for example: headings), graphics (for example: charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly, organizes information into categories, and uses headings, charts, or multimedia to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child writes informative or explanatory texts that explore a topic by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write well-developed and logically organized informative/explanatory texts, conveying relevant content through precise language, domain-specific vocabulary, and formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes clear and organized informative texts using precise language, subject vocabulary, and a formal style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports and follows the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes using a formal style appropriate for making an argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to connect claims, counterclaims, reasons, and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child can back up their opinions with clear reasons and facts from trustworthy sources, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can clearly state their opinion, recognize other viewpoints, and organize their reasons and facts in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that explain their opinions using clear reasons and supporting facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write well-organized and cohesive arguments, distinguishing claim(s) from opposing claims and using language to clarify connections among claims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child writes well-organized arguments that separate their opinion from others and use clear language to connect ideas and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new academic and subject-specific words correctly, especially words important for understanding or expressing ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (for example: bullheaded, willful, firm, persistent, resolute).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference in feelings or ideas between words that have similar meanings, like 'bullheaded' and 'persistent.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses the relationship between words to better understand what each word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (for example: verbal irony, puns) in context.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains figures of speech, like jokes or irony, based on how they are used in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (for example: by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child checks the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at how it’s used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (for example: dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in print or online, to find how to say a word, what it means, or its part of speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (for example: precede, recede, secede).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek or Latin word parts to help figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (for example: the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the surrounding words and sentence to guess the meaning of a new word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words or phrases based on grade 8 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using different strategies like context clues or word parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of word structure, grammar, and context to determine the meaning of new words and phrases in increasingly complex texts.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about word parts, grammar, and context to understand new words in harder texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand challenging nonfiction books on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
description_plain: Your child reads a wide range of books and texts that get harder as they improve.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a case in which two or more texts provide conflicting information on the same topic and identify where the texts disagree on matters of fact or interpretation.
description_plain: Your child compares two or more texts that disagree on the same topic and points out where they differ in facts or opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; recognize when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child looks closely at the reasons and facts in a text to decide if they make sense and notices if unrelated facts are included.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using different mediums (for example: print or digital text, video, multimedia) to present a particular topic or idea.
description_plain: Your child thinks about the pros and cons of using different ways, like books, videos, or websites, to share information on a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Integration of Knowledge and Ideas to:
description_plain: Your child can use information from different sources to better understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author acknowledges and responds to conflicting evidence or viewpoints.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's main opinion or goal and explain how the author deals with different or opposing ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail the structure of a specific paragraph in a text, including the role of particular sentences in developing and refining a key concept.
description_plain: Your child can look closely at a paragraph to understand how each sentence helps explain an important idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.
description_plain: Your child can find the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a text makes connections among and distinctions between individuals, ideas, or events (for example: through comparisons, analogies, or categories).
description_plain: Your child can explain how a text connects or compares people, ideas, or events using examples like comparisons or categories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to supporting ideas; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, follow how it develops, and give a clear summary without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use strong examples from the text to support what the text says clearly and what can be understood from it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze and evaluate an author's choices to understand informational text.
description_plain: Your child can study and judge the choices an author makes to help understand informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories, plays, and poems for their grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Range of Reading and Complexity of Text to:
description_plain: Your child can read a variety of texts that are more difficult and interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a modern work of fiction draws on themes, patterns of events, or character types from myths, traditional stories, or religious works such as the Bible, including describing how the material is rendered new.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a modern story uses ideas or characters from old myths or religious stories and shows them in a new way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the extent to which a filmed or live production of a story or drama stays faithful to or departs from the text or script, evaluating the choices made by the director or actors.
description_plain: Your child can compare a movie or play version of a story to the original text and talk about the choices made by the director or actors.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Integration of Knowledge and Ideas to:
description_plain: Your child can use different sources and ideas to better understand stories and plays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how differences in the points of view of the characters and the audience or reader (for example: created through the use of dramatic irony) create such effects as suspense or humor.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different points of view between characters and the audience create feelings like suspense or humor.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the structure of two or more texts and analyze how the differing structure of each text contributes to its meaning and style.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts are organized and explain how their structure affects their meaning and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.
description_plain: Your child can find the meaning of words and phrases in a story, including special meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular lines of dialogue or incidents in a story or drama propel the action, reveal aspects of a character, or provoke a decision.
description_plain: Your child can explain how certain dialogue or events in a story move the plot forward, show character traits, or cause decisions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to the characters, setting, and plot; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main theme or idea of a story and explain how it develops with the characters, setting, and plot, then give a clear summary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find strong examples in the story to support what it says clearly and what can be understood from it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze and evaluate literary elements and an author's choices to understand literary text.
description_plain: Your child can study and judge the parts of stories and the author's choices to better understand literary texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when it is needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multimedia and visual displays into presentations to clarify information, strengthen claims and evidence, and add interest.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, videos, or other media to presentations to make ideas clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with relevant evidence, sound valid reasoning, and well-chosen details; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can share their ideas clearly, focusing on important points and using good reasons and details, while speaking loudly and clearly and making eye contact.

subject: Language Arts
description: Design organized presentations incorporating key details and claims while tailored for purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child can create organized presentations that include key details and are made for the right audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and relevance and sufficiency of the evidence and identifying when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker’s argument, check if the reasons and evidence make sense, and notice when unrelated information is added.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the purpose of information presented in diverse media and formats (for example: visually, quantitatively, orally) and evaluate the motives (for example: social, commercial, political) behind its presentation.
description_plain: Your child can understand why information is shared in different ways and figure out the reasons behind it, like social, business, or political goals.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views in light of the evidence presented.
description_plain: Your child can listen to new information from others and change or explain their own ideas based on the evidence they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that connect the ideas of several speakers and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant evidence, observations, and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions that connect what different people say and answer others’ questions with good reasons and ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions and decision-making, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow rules during group talks, keep track of goals and deadlines, and understand their role in the group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or researching and uses that information to think about and talk about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 8 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different kinds of group talks, listen to others, and share their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in effective collaborative discussions and analyze information presented.
description_plain: Your child can join group discussions well and understand the information shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 7 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (for example: "Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims").
description_plain: Your child can follow grade 7 reading rules to understand nonfiction by checking if the reasons and evidence support the author’s points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 7 Reading standards to literature (for example: "Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history").
description_plain: Your child can compare stories and real history to see how authors change facts in fiction.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find proof from stories or information texts to help explain their thoughts and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find good information from books and online, check if it’s trustworthy, and use it correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions for further research and investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects by using several sources and asking more questions to learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose research questions, synthesize answers from multiple credible sources, and present conclusions in an appropriate format.
description_plain: Your child can ask research questions, gather answers from trustworthy sources, and share what they learn in the right way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly, sometimes taking longer to research and revise, and other times writing quickly for different tasks and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and link to and cite sources as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the internet to write, share their work, give credit to sources, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ideas with some help from others, focusing on the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child will write clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose language that expresses ideas precisely and concisely, recognizing and eliminating wordiness and redundancy.
description_plain: Your child can pick words that express ideas clearly and briefly, avoiding extra or repeated words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly spell frequently used words and consult reference materials (for example, dictionaries, both print and digital, spell check, and/or trusted peers and/or adults) to determine the spelling of less frequently used vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child spells common words correctly and looks up harder words using dictionaries, spell check, or trusted people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate coordinate adjectives (for example: It was a fascinating, enjoyable movie but not He wore an old[,] green shirt).
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly to separate adjectives that equally describe something, like in 'a fascinating, enjoyable movie.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Place phrases and clauses within a sentence, recognizing and correcting misplaced and dangling modifiers.
description_plain: Your child places phrases and clauses correctly in sentences and fixes mistakes like misplaced or dangling modifiers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose among simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences to signal differing relationships among ideas.
description_plain: Your child chooses different types of sentences (simple, compound, complex) to show how ideas relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of phrases and clauses in general and their function in specific sentences.
description_plain: Your child can explain what phrases and clauses do in sentences and how they work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan, draft, edit, and revise as needed to ensure that writing is clear and coherent, that it conforms to standard conventions for grammar, usage, and mechanics, and that its style is appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child plans, writes, edits, and revises their work to make sure it is clear, follows grammar and spelling rules, and fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use stylistic techniques (for example: alliteration, onomatopoeia, rhyme scheme, repetition); figurative language (for example: simile, metaphor, personification); and graphic elements (for example: capital letters, line length, word position) to express personal or narrative voice.
description_plain: Your child uses creative writing tools like alliteration, rhyme, similes, and formatting to show their personal or story voice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that reflects on the experiences or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words, details, and sensory language to show actions and describe experiences clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child uses storytelling techniques like dialogue, pacing, and description to develop events and characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and point of view, introducing characters, and organizing events in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, clear details, and well-organized events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write engaging real or imagined narratives effectively using techniques such as relevant description, sensory language, dialogue, and logical pacing to capture the action and detail experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child writes interesting stories using description, sensory details, dialogue, and pacing to show actions and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a strong ending that sums up and supports the information they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child will write using a formal tone that fits the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words, including special vocabulary related to the topic, to explain ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses linking words and phrases to connect ideas and make their writing easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, examples, and quotes to explain the topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting (for example: headings), graphics (for example: charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child starts by clearly introducing the topic and organizes information using methods like comparing or explaining causes, and uses headings, pictures, or charts to help understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic by choosing and organizing important facts and ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write well-developed informative/explanatory texts using logical organizational strategies, relevant supporting information, domain-specific vocabulary, and formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes clear and well-organized informative texts using facts, special vocabulary, and a formal tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes using a formal style that fits the argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to connect ideas and show how their reasons and evidence relate to their claim.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child supports their claims with clear reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child introduces their claim, mentions other viewpoints, and organizes reasons and evidence in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments that clearly support their claims with good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write well-organized arguments using logical reasoning, relevant and credible evidence, acknowledgement of opposing claims, clear language, and formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes well-organized arguments using clear reasons, good evidence, mentions opposing views, and uses formal language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new academic and topic-specific words correctly, especially words important for understanding or expressing ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (for example: refined, respectful, polite, diplomatic, and condescending).
description_plain: Your child understands the different feelings or ideas that similar words can have, like how polite and diplomatic are different even though they seem alike.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (for example: synonym/antonym, analogy) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (for example: literary, biblical, and mythological allusions) in context.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains figures of speech and references, like stories or myths, based on the context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (for example: by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check if their first guess about a word's meaning is correct by looking at the sentence or using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (for example: dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in books or online, to find how to say a word, what it means, or what part of speech it is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (for example: belligerent, bellicose, rebel).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin word parts to help figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (for example: the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the words around a new word and how it is used in a sentence to understand its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 7 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different helpful strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of word relationships, word structures, and sentence structures to determine the meaning of new words in increasingly complex texts.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about word parts and sentence structure to understand new words in harder reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6–8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
