description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand nonfiction books and articles for grades 6–8, with some help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Range of Reading and Complexity of Text to:
description_plain: Your child reads a variety of texts at different difficulty levels to build strong reading skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more authors writing about the same topic shape their presentations of key information by emphasizing different evidence or advancing different interpretations of facts.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more writers present the same topic by looking at the different facts or ideas they focus on.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims.
description_plain: Your child can follow and judge the reasons and evidence an author uses to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a text to an audio, video, or multimedia version of the text, analyzing each medium's portrayal of the subject (for example: how the delivery of a speech affects the impact of the words).
description_plain: Your child can compare a text with its audio, video, or multimedia version and explain how each way shows the subject differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Integration of Knowledge and Ideas to:
description_plain: Your child uses different sources and ideas together to better understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author distinguishes his or her position from that of others.
description_plain: Your child can find the author's opinion or purpose and explain how the author shows their view is different from others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author organizes a text and how each part helps develop the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child understands the meaning of words in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and can explain how word choice affects the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the interactions between individuals, events, and ideas in a text (for example: how ideas influence individuals or events, or how individuals influence ideas or events).
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas in a text influence each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more central ideas in a text and analyze their development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how they develop, and give a clear summary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find several examples from the text to support what it says and ideas they figure out from reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize and evaluate to show understanding of informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can summarize and think about informational texts to show they understand them.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 6-8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems for grades 6–8, with some help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Range of Reading and Complexity of Text to:
description_plain: Your child can read different kinds of texts and understand their difficulty levels.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history.
description_plain: Your child can compare a made-up story about a time or place with a real historical account to see how fiction changes history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a written story, drama, or poem to its audio, filmed, staged, or multimedia version, analyzing the effects of techniques unique to each medium (for example: lighting, sound, color, or camera focus and angles in a film).
description_plain: Your child can compare a story, play, or poem with its movie, audio, or live version and explain how things like lighting or sound change the experience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Integration of Knowledge and Ideas to:
description_plain: Your child understands how to connect ideas from different sources to better understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author develops and contrasts the points of view of different characters or narrators in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows different characters' or narrators' points of view and how they are different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a drama's or poem's form or structure (for example: soliloquy, sonnet) contributes to its meaning.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the way a poem or play is written (like a sonnet or soliloquy) helps show its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of rhymes and other repetitions of sounds (for example: alliteration) on a specific verse or stanza of a poem or section of a story or drama.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story or poem, including special meanings and sounds like rhymes or alliteration.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular elements of a story or drama interact (for example: how setting shapes the characters or plot).
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a story or play, like setting and characters, work together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea or theme of a story and explain how it develops, then summarize the story clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use several examples from the text to explain what it says directly and what it suggests.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the connections between interrelated literary elements to understand literary texts.
description_plain: Your child can understand how different parts of a story work together to make the whole story meaningful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use proper English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components and visual displays in presentations to clarify claims and findings and emphasize salient points.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, videos, or other media to presentations to help explain their ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with pertinent descriptions, facts, details, and examples; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can give clear and focused presentations using facts and examples, while speaking loudly and clearly and making eye contact.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for formal presentations and use appropriate delivery techniques.
description_plain: Your child can get ready for formal talks and use good speaking skills when presenting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker's argument and decide if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the main ideas and supporting details presented in diverse media and formats (for example: visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how the ideas clarify a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can understand main ideas and details from different types of media and explain how they help explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others and, when warranted, modify their own views.
description_plain: Your child can listen to new information from others and change their own ideas when it makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that elicit elaboration and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant observations and ideas that bring the discussion back on topic as needed.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions that make others explain more and respond with ideas that keep the discussion on track.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow rules for group talks, keep track of goals and deadlines, and understand their role in the group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can come to discussions prepared by reading or researching the topic and use evidence from what they learned to think about and talk about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 7 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can join different kinds of group talks about grade 7 topics, listen to others, and share their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Incorporate language, tools, and techniques appropriate for task and audience during formal presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use the right words and tools when giving formal presentations to fit the task and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 5 Reading standards to informational texts (for example: "Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text, identifying which reasons and evidence support which point[s]").
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support points in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 5 Reading standards to literature (for example: "Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a story or a drama, drawing on specific details in the text [for example: how characters interact]").
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast characters, settings, or events in stories or plays using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from stories or informational texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide documentation of sources used in a grade-appropriate format.
description_plain: Your child can show where they found their information using simple, grade-appropriate source lists.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop relevant supporting visual information (for example: charts, maps, graphs, photo evidence, models).
description_plain: Your child can create helpful visuals like charts, maps, or graphs to support their information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; summarize or paraphrase information in notes and finished work, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child can remember or find important information from books or online, summarize it in notes or work, and list their sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and present a brief (oral or written) research report with clear focus and supporting detail for an intended audience.
description_plain: Your child can make and share a short research report with clear focus and details for others to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate comprehension of information with supporting logical and valid inferences.
description_plain: Your child can understand information and make logical guesses based on it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize and support key ideas.
description_plain: Your child can summarize main ideas and support them with details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that use several sources to build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects using several sources to learn about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Research to locate, summarize, synthesize and document information from print and digital sources, and communicate findings appropriately.
description_plain: Your child can find, summarize, combine, and share information from books and online sources in a clear way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long times for different subjects, reasons, and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: With some help, your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: With help from others, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organized in a way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell grade-level words correctly and use references when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to indicate titles of works.
description_plain: Your child can show titles of books, stories, or other works by underlining, using quotation marks, or italics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to set off the words yes and no (for example: Yes, thank you), to set off a tag question from the rest of the sentence (for example: It's true, isn't it?), and to indicate direct address (for example: Is that you, Steve?).
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly with words like yes and no, tag questions, and when directly talking to someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate an introductory element from the rest of the sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use a comma after an introductory word or phrase at the start of a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation to separate items in a series.
description_plain: Your child can use punctuation like commas to separate items in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine, and reduce sentences for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can make sentences longer, shorter, or combine them to make writing clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correlative conjunctions (for example: either/or, neither/nor).
description_plain: Your child can use pairs of connecting words like either/or and neither/nor correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb tense.
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix mistakes when verb tenses change incorrectly in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verb tense to convey various times, sequences, states, and conditions.
description_plain: Your child uses verb tenses to show when things happen and how they relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the perfect (for example: I had walked; I have walked; I will have walked) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can use perfect verb tenses like 'had walked' or 'have walked' to show completed actions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections in general and their function in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands what conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections do in sentences and can explain their use.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply understanding of the conventions of Standard English grammar, usage, and mechanics to make meaning clear and to strengthen style.
description_plain: Your child uses grammar, word usage, and punctuation correctly to make writing clear and well-styled.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that fits the story or events they have told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child uses clear words and details that appeal to the senses to describe experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words, phrases, and clauses to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words and phrases to show the order of events in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, description, and pacing, to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses storytelling tools like dialogue and description to show events and how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing characters, then tells events in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write engaging, real or imagined narratives using literary techniques, character development, sensory and descriptive details, and a variety of transition words to signal a clear sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child writes interesting stories, real or made-up, using details, characters, and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a closing statement that relates to the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within and across categories of information using words, phrases, and clauses (for example: in contrast, especially).
description_plain: Your child can connect ideas within and between groups of information using words like 'in contrast' or 'especially' to make writing clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child will add facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, provide a general observation and focus, and group related information logically; include formatting (for example: headings), illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can start a topic clearly, share a main idea, organize related information in a logical way, and use headings, pictures, or videos to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts that provide a clear focus and the use of text features to group related information on a well-developed topic, using precise language and domain-specific vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child writes clear informational texts that focus on a topic, group related facts together, and use exact words and special vocabulary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing sentence or section that relates to their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words, phrases, and clauses (for example: consequently, specifically).
description_plain: Your child links their opinion and reasons using words like 'consequently' or 'specifically' to show how ideas connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide logically ordered reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons in a logical order and supports them with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which ideas are logically grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, state their opinion, and organize ideas in a way that supports their point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information, for a variety of purposes and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes opinion pieces on different topics, giving reasons and information to support their views for different readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal contrast, addition, and other logical relationships (for example: however, although, nevertheless, similarly, moreover, in addition).
description_plain: Your child learns and uses grade-level academic and subject-specific words, including words that show contrast or addition like 'however' or 'moreover.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (for example: synonyms, antonyms, homographs) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other, like synonyms and antonyms, to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child recognizes and explains common sayings, like idioms and proverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language, including similes and metaphors, in context.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains figurative language like similes and metaphors when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and identify the meaning of roots and related word families in which the pronunciation of the root does not change.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands root words and word families where the root sounds the same.

subject: Language Arts
description: Infer meaning of words using structural analysis, context, and knowledge of multiple meanings.
description_plain: Your child figures out word meanings by looking at parts of the word, the sentence, and knowing words can have more than one meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of derivational suffixes that change the part of speech of the base word (for example active and activity).
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of suffixes that change a word’s type, like turning 'active' into 'activity.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and identify the meaning of words with sophisticated prefixes and suffixes.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands words with advanced prefixes and suffixes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (for example, dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in print or online, to find how to say words and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (for example: photograph, photosynthesis).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek and Latin roots and prefixes to help figure out word meanings, like in 'photograph' or 'photosynthesis.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (for example: cause/effect relationships and comparisons in text) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues in the text, like cause and effect or comparisons, to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 5 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child will figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different strategies based on what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the varieties of English (for example: dialects, registers) used in stories, dramas, or poems.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain differences and similarities in types of English, like dialects or formal and informal language, in stories, plays, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine, and reduce sentences for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can make sentences longer, shorter, or combine them to make their writing or speaking more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses the words around a tricky word to check if they read it right and can reread if needed to understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression.
description_plain: Your child reads stories and poems out loud clearly, at a good speed, and with expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level books with a clear purpose and understands what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (for example, roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about letter sounds, syllables, and word parts to read new long words correctly, both in and out of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses grade-level phonics and word study skills to sound out words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of word meanings (morphology) and word relationships to determine the meaning of unknown words in and out of context.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about word parts and word relationships to figure out the meaning of new words in different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, at the high end of the grades 4–5 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand challenging nonfiction texts about history, science, and technical subjects on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Range of Reading and Complexity of Text to:
description_plain: Your child reads a variety of texts at different difficulty levels to build their reading skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from several texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from several texts on the same topic and use it to write or talk about the subject clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text, identifying which reasons and evidence support which point(s).
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and facts to support their ideas and can point out which reasons go with which ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw on information from multiple print or digital sources, demonstrating the ability to locate an answer to a question quickly or to solve a problem efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can find answers or solve problems quickly by using information from different books or websites.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Integration of Knowledge and Ideas to:
description_plain: Your child uses information from different sources and ideas to understand and explain topics better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use informational text features (such as bold type, headings, graphic organizers, numbering schemes, glossary) and text structures to organize or categorize information, to answer questions, or to perform specific tasks.
description_plain: Your child uses parts of nonfiction texts like bold words, headings, charts, numbers, and glossaries to organize information and answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple accounts of the same event or topic, noting important similarities and differences in the point of view they represent.
description_plain: Your child looks at different versions of the same event or topic and notices important similarities and differences in the viewpoints.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the overall structure (for example: chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in two or more texts.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain how two or more texts organize their information, like by time order, comparing ideas, cause and effect, or solving problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 5 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words related to grade 5 topics when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between fact and opinion, providing support for judgments made.
description_plain: Your child understands the difference between facts and opinions and can explain why they think so.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships or interactions between two or more individuals, events, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, or ideas connect or affect each other in history, science, or technical texts using details from the reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more main ideas of a text and explain how they are supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how details support them, and tell the main points in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use exact quotes from a text to explain what it says clearly and to make guesses based on the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply strategies to interpret and analyze various types of informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses different strategies to understand and think about many kinds of informational reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, at the high end of the grades 4-5 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand harder stories, plays, and poems for grades 4-5 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Range of Reading and Complexity of Text to:
description_plain: Your child reads a wide range of books and texts that get harder as they grow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of literary devices (such as imagery, rhythm, foreshadowing, and simple metaphors) to understand and respond to text.
description_plain: Your child uses ideas like pictures in words, rhythm, hints about what will happen, and simple comparisons to understand and enjoy what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast stories in the same genre (for example: mysteries and adventure stories) on their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can tell how stories in the same type, like mysteries or adventures, are alike and different in their themes and topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how visual and multimedia elements contribute to the meaning, tone, or beauty of a text (for example: graphic novel, multimedia presentation of fiction, folktale, myth, and poem).
description_plain: Your child notices how pictures, videos, or other media add meaning, feeling, or beauty to stories, poems, or myths.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Integration of Knowledge and Ideas to:
description_plain: Your child combines ideas from different sources to understand stories and poems better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate information to support opinions, predictions, inferences, and identification of the author's message or theme.
description_plain: Your child finds information in a text to support their opinions, guesses, or understanding of the author's message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a narrator's or speaker's point of view influences how events are described.
description_plain: Your child explains how the narrator’s or speaker’s point of view changes how events are told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a series of chapters, scenes, or stanzas fits together to provide the overall structure of a particular story, drama, or poem.
description_plain: Your child explains how chapters, scenes, or stanzas work together to form the whole story, play, or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative language such as metaphors and similes.
description_plain: Your child figures out what words and phrases mean in a text, including when they use comparisons like metaphors and similes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more character's points of view, settings, or events in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (for example: how characters interact).
description_plain: Your child compares and contrasts characters’ views, settings, or events in stories or plays using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text, including how characters in a story or drama respond to challenges or how the speaker in a poem reflects upon a topic; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child finds the theme of a story, play, or poem by looking at details, including how characters handle problems or how a poem’s speaker thinks about a topic, and can summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child uses exact quotes from a text to explain what it says clearly and to make guesses based on the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use pre-reading strategies, such as identifying a purpose for reading, generating questions to answers while reading, previewing sections of texts and activating prior knowledge.
description_plain: Your child can get ready to read by thinking about why they are reading, asking questions, looking over parts of the text, and remembering what they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply strategies to interpret and analyze various types of literary texts.
description_plain: Your child can use different ways to understand and think about stories and other types of writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt language as appropriate to purpose: to persuade, explain/provide information, or express an opinion.
description_plain: Your child can change how they use words to persuade, explain, or share their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation or task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components (for example: graphics, sound) and visual displays in presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, sounds, or other media to presentations to help explain main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate eye contact and speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can look at people while speaking and talk clearly at a good speed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text or present an opinion, sequencing ideas logically and using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can share information or opinions in order, using facts and details to support their main points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present to express an opinion, persuade, or explain/provide information.
description_plain: Your child can give presentations to share opinions, persuade others, or explain information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the points a speaker makes and explain how each claim is supported by reasons and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main points a speaker makes and how they support those points with reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize a written text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can summarize stories or information they hear, see, or read in different ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and draw conclusions in light of information and knowledge gained from the discussions.
description_plain: Your child can think about important ideas from discussions and make conclusions based on what they learned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions by making comments that contribute to the discussion and elaborate on the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions during discussions and add to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child can follow rules during discussions and do their assigned jobs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying and uses what they know to talk about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 5 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different kinds of group talks, listen to others, and share their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Listen to others' ideas and form their own opinions.
description_plain: Your child listens to what others think and forms their own opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Collaborate in discussions that serve various purposes and address various situations
description_plain: Your child works with others in discussions for different reasons and situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: Your child can remember things from their own experiences or find information to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask primary questions of depth and breadth.
description_plain: Your child can ask important and detailed questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of multimedia sources to answer questions of interest.
description_plain: Your child can use videos, pictures, and other media to find answers to questions they are curious about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features to locate and interpret information. For example: table of contents, illustrations, diagrams, headings and bold type.
description_plain: Your child can use parts of a book like the table of contents, pictures, diagrams, and headings to find and understand information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a specific question and gather information for purposeful investigation and inquiry.
description_plain: Your child can ask a clear question and find information to learn more about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a variety of resources and the information they might contain (for example: dictionary, trade book, library databases, internet web page).
description_plain: Your child knows different places to find information, like dictionaries, books, library websites, and the internet.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects. For example: read a number of books on a single topic to produce a report; record science observations.
description_plain: Your child takes part in group research and writing projects, like reading several books on one topic to write a report or recording science observations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and inquiry, gathering information from a variety of resources to answer questions.
description_plain: Your child works with others to research and find information from different sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child uses computers and other digital tools to write and share their work, sometimes working with friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, focus on a topic and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing.
description_plain: With help from adults and friends, your child stays focused on a topic and improves their writing by making changes and fixing mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child uses reference books like beginner dictionaries to check and fix spelling when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generalize learned spelling patterns when writing words (for example: cage → badge; boy → boil).
description_plain: Your child uses spelling patterns they have learned to write new words correctly, like changing 'cage' to 'badge' or 'boy' to 'boil.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an apostrophe to form contractions and frequently occurring possessives.
description_plain: Your child uses apostrophes correctly to make contractions (like can't) and show possession (like the dog's bone).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in greetings and closings of letters.
description_plain: Your child uses commas properly in the greetings and endings of letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize holidays, product names, and geographic names.
description_plain: Your child knows to capitalize names of holidays, products, and places.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell high-frequency words correctly.
description_plain: Your child spells common words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce, expand, and rearrange complete simple and compound sentences (for example: The boy watched the movie; The little boy watched the movie; The action movie was watched by the little boy).
description_plain: Your child writes complete sentences and can make them longer or change their order, like turning 'The boy watched the movie' into 'The little boy watched the movie' or 'The action movie was watched by the little boy.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply accurate subject-verb agreement while writing.
description_plain: Your child uses the right verb form to match the subject in their sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child uses describing words (adjectives and adverbs) and chooses the right one depending on what they want to describe.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the past tense of frequently occurring irregular verbs (for example: sat, hid, told).
description_plain: Your child uses the past tense correctly for common irregular verbs like 'sat,' 'hid,' and 'told.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reflexive pronouns (for example: myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child uses reflexive pronouns like 'myself' and 'ourselves' correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use collective nouns (for example: group).
description_plain: Your child uses words that name groups of things or people, like 'group.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and word choices when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a process to revise and edit so that thoughts and ideas are communicated clearly with appropriate spelling, capitalization, grammar, and punctuation.
description_plain: Your child will revise and edit their writing to make sure ideas are clear and spelling, capitalization, grammar, and punctuation are correct.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop characters both internally (thoughts and feelings) and externally (physical features, expressions, clothing).
description_plain: Your child can describe characters by explaining what they think and feel as well as how they look and dress.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge about structure and craft gained from mentor text to narrative writing.
description_plain: Your child uses examples from books they read to help write their own stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write with precise nouns, active verbs, and descriptive adjectives.
description_plain: Your child writes using specific nouns, strong action verbs, and detailed adjectives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write simple, descriptive poems.
description_plain: Your child can write simple poems that describe things clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use temporal words to signal event order.
description_plain: Your child uses words like first, next, and then to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include details to describe actions, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child includes details that explain actions, thoughts, and feelings in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write real or imagined narratives that describe events in sequence and provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events that happen in order and end with a clear conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Writers use technology to support the writing process.
description_plain: Your child uses computers or other technology to help with writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply appropriate transition words to writing.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words to connect ideas in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize informational texts using main ideas and specific supporting details.
description_plain: Your child organizes informational writing by stating main ideas and adding supporting details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write letters and "how-to's" (for example: procedures, directions, recipes) that follow a logical order and appropriate format.
description_plain: Your child writes letters and instructions that follow a clear order and proper format.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child adds a closing sentence or section to finish their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use facts and definitions to develop points, including relevant details when writing to questions about texts.
description_plain: Your child uses facts and definitions with details to answer questions about texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts organized around main ideas which are supported by relevant details, facts, and definitions.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that focus on main ideas supported by facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child includes a closing sentence or section to end their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words (for example: because, and, also) to connect opinion and reasons.
description_plain: Your child uses words like because, and, or also to connect their opinions and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Supply reasons that support the opinion.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons to support their opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic or book they are writing about.
description_plain: Your child introduces the topic or book they are writing about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write pieces on a topic or book that state opinions and give supporting reasons.
description_plain: Your child can write about a topic or book by sharing their opinion and giving reasons to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine which strategies should be used to decode multisyllabic words
description_plain: Your child knows how to use different ways to sound out long words with many syllables.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using adjectives and adverbs to describe (for example: When other kids are happy that makes me happy).
description_plain: Your child uses new words and phrases they learn from talking, reading, and listening, including describing things with adjectives and adverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among closely related verbs (for example: toss, throw, hurl) and closely related adjectives (for example: thin, slender, skinny, scrawny).
description_plain: Your child can tell the small differences in meaning between similar action words and describing words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (for example: describe foods that are spicy or juicy).
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to real life, like describing foods as spicy or juicy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands special language like figures of speech and how words can have different meanings or connections.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries and beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use simple dictionaries and glossaries, in books or online, to find out what words and phrases mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create new words by combining base words with affixes to connect known words to new words.
description_plain: Your child can make new words by adding beginnings or endings to words they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of the meaning of individual words to predict the meaning of compound words (for example: birdhouse, lighthouse, housefly; bookshelf, notebook, bookmark).
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about words to figure out the meaning of compound words made of two smaller words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (for example: addition, additional).
description_plain: Your child uses a root word they know to help understand a new word that has the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known prefix is added to a known word (for example: happy/unhappy, tell/retell).
description_plain: Your child knows how adding a prefix (a word part at the start) changes the meaning of a word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the sentence to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 2 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clear up the meaning of tricky or multiple-meaning words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare formal and informal uses of English.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between formal and informal ways of speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text accurately and fluently, attending to phrasing, intonation, and punctuation.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text smoothly and correctly, paying attention to phrasing, tone, and punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses the words around a tricky word to check or fix their reading and understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text out loud with the right speed, accuracy, and expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text with a clear purpose and understands what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level words that are spelled in unusual ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words with inconsistent but common spelling-sound correspondences.
description_plain: Your child can recognize words that have unusual but common ways of matching letters to sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common prefixes and suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can read words by breaking down common beginnings and endings like prefixes and suffixes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled two-syllable words with long vowels.
description_plain: Your child can read two-syllable words with long vowel sounds that follow regular spelling patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read multisyllabic words accurately and fluently.
description_plain: Your child can read longer words with multiple syllables smoothly and correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know spelling-sound correspondences for additional common vowel teams.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read vowel pairs that make common sounds in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long and short vowels when reading regularly spelled one syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in simple one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses grade-appropriate phonics skills to sound out and read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of complex spelling patterns (orthography) and word meanings (morphology) to decode words with accuracy.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of spelling patterns and word parts to read words accurately.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in the grades 2–3 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of second grade, your child can read and understand informational texts about history, science, and technical topics, with some help for harder parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adjust reading rate according to type of text and purpose for reading.
description_plain: Your child changes how fast they read depending on the type of text and why they are reading it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity to:
description_plain: Your child reads different kinds of texts at the right difficulty level for second grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how reasons support specific points the author makes in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how reasons in a text support the author's main points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific images (for example: a diagram showing how a machine works) contribute to and clarify a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how pictures or diagrams help explain what the text is about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Integration of Knowledge and Ideas to:
description_plain: Your child uses information from pictures and text together to understand ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read text to perform a specific task such as follow a recipe or play a game.
description_plain: Your child can read instructions or directions to complete a task like following a recipe or playing a game.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main purpose of a text, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe.
description_plain: Your child can tell what the main purpose of a text is, like what the author wants to explain or answer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (for example: captions, bold print, subheadings, glossaries, indexes, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text efficiently.
description_plain: Your child uses features like captions, bold words, and headings to find important information quickly in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 2 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of words and phrases related to second grade topics when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how events, ideas, or steps in a text are connected in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the main idea using relevant and significant details in a variety of texts.
description_plain: Your child can summarize the main idea of a text using important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic of a multiparagraph text as well as the focus of specific paragraphs within the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main topic of a text with several paragraphs and understand what each paragraph focuses on.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply specific skills to comprehend and fluently read informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can use specific skills to understand and read informational texts smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories and poetry, in the grades 2-3 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories and poems at a second to third grade level, with some help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity to:
description_plain: Your child reads a variety of texts at different difficulty levels to improve their reading skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more versions of the same story (for example: Cinderella stories) by different authors or from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare and talk about differences and similarities between two or more versions of the same story from different authors or cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from the illustrations and words in a print or digital text to demonstrate understanding of its characters, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and words in books or digital stories to understand characters, where the story happens, and what happens in the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use Integration of Knowledge and Ideas to:
description_plain: Your child uses what they learn from different parts of a story or text to understand it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify how word choice (for example: sensory details, figurative language) enhances meaning in poetry.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the choice of words, like sensory details or special language, makes poems more meaningful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge differences in the points of view of characters, including by speaking in a different voice for each character when reading dialogue aloud.
description_plain: Your child notices when characters have different points of view and can read their dialogue using different voices.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure of a story, including describing how the beginning introduces the story and the ending concludes the action.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a story is organized, including how it starts and how it ends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how words and phrases (for example: regular beats, alliteration, rhymes, repeated lines) supply rhythm and meaning in a story, poem, or song.
description_plain: Your child can explain how words and phrases like rhymes, beats, or repeated lines add rhythm and meaning to stories, poems, or songs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how characters in a story respond to major events and challenges.
description_plain: Your child can describe how characters react to big events and challenges in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables and folktales from diverse cultures, and determine their central message, lesson, or moral.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories from different cultures and explain the main message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer who, what, where, when, why, and how questions to show they understand important details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate use of self-monitoring comprehension strategies: rereading, checking context clues, predicting, questioning, clarifying, activating schema/background knowledge to construct meaning and draw inferences.
description_plain: Your child uses strategies like rereading, guessing, asking questions, and using what they already know to understand and think about what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply specific skills to comprehend and fluently read literary texts.
description_plain: Your child uses specific skills to understand and read stories and poems smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use content-specific vocabulary to ask questions and provide information.
description_plain: Your child uses words related to a topic to ask questions and share information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child can speak in complete sentences when needed to give details or explain something clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create audio recordings of stories or poems; add drawings or other visual displays to stories or recounts of experiences when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can make audio recordings of stories or poems and add drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Contribute knowledge to a small group or class discussion to develop a topic.
description_plain: Your child can share ideas in a small group or class to help develop a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Tell a story or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking audibly in coherent sentences.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story or talk about an experience using clear facts and details, speaking loudly and clearly in full sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Deliver presentations while maintaining focus on topic and be prepared to discuss.
description_plain: Your child can give presentations that stay on topic and be ready to talk about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to clarify comprehension, gather additional information, or deepen understanding of a topic or issue
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone says to better understand or learn more about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount or describe key ideas or details from a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can retell or describe important ideas or details from stories or information they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask for clarification and further explanation as needed about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask for more explanation or help when they don’t understand the topic or story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by linking their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can add to conversations by connecting their ideas to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (for example: gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child can follow simple rules for talking with others, like waiting their turn, listening carefully, and speaking one at a time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 2 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child can join in conversations with classmates and adults about second-grade topics and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in dialogue and learn new information through active listening.
description_plain: Your child can listen carefully and talk with others to learn new things.

subject: Science
description: Communicate solutions that will reduce the impact of humans on the land, water, air and/or other living things in the local environment.
description_plain: Your child can share ideas to help reduce how people affect land, water, air, and living things nearby.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions to obtain information about the purpose of weather forecasting to prepare for, and respond to, severe weather.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to learn why weather forecasts help people get ready for bad weather.

subject: Science
description: Use a model to represent the relationship between the needs of different plants or animals (including humans) and the places they live.
description_plain: Your child can use a model to show how different plants and animals, including people, have needs that depend on where they live.

subject: Science
description: Plants and animals meet their needs in their habitats and impact one another; people can prepare for severe weather.
description_plain: Your child understands that plants and animals get what they need from their homes and affect each other, and that people can prepare for bad weather.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument supported by evidence for how plants and animals (including humans) can change the environment to meet their needs.
description_plain: Your child can explain with evidence how plants and animals, including people, can change their environment to meet their needs.

subject: Science
description: Use and share observations of local weather conditions to describe patterns over time.
description_plain: Your child can use and share observations about local weather to describe patterns over time.

subject: Science
description: Patterns are observed when measuring the local weather, including how humans and other organisms impact their environment.
description_plain: Your child notices patterns in local weather and how people and other living things affect their environment.

subject: Science
description: Use observations to describe patterns of what plants and animals (including humans) need to survive.
description_plain: Your child can describe what plants and animals, including people, need to live by watching them.

subject: Science
description: To live and grow, animals obtain food they need from plants or other animals, and plants need water and light.
description_plain: Your child understands that animals get food from plants or other animals, and plants need water and light to grow.

subject: Science
description: Use tools and materials to design and build a structure that will reduce the warming effect of sunlight on an area.
description_plain: Your child can use tools and materials to build something that helps keep an area cooler by blocking sunlight.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to determine the effect of sunlight on Earth's surface.
description_plain: Your child can watch and describe how sunlight affects the Earth's surface.

subject: Science
description: Sunlight affects the Earth's surface.
description_plain: Your child understands that sunlight changes the Earth's surface.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data to determine if a design solution works as intended to change the speed or direction of an object with a push or a pull.
description_plain: Your child can look at data to see if a design changes how fast or in what direction an object moves when pushed or pulled.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to compare the effects of different strengths or different directions of pushes and pulls on the motion of an object.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do an experiment to compare how different strengths or directions of pushes and pulls affect how an object moves.

subject: Science
description: Pushes and pulls can have different strengths and directions, and can change the speed or direction of an object's motion or start or stop it.
description_plain: Your child understands that pushes and pulls can be strong or weak and can change how things move or stop them.

subject: Science
description: Make observations at different times of year to relate the amount of daylight to the time of year.
description_plain: Your child can watch how daylight changes during the year and connect it to the seasons.

subject: Science
description: Use observations of the sun, moon, and stars to describe patterns that can be predicted.
description_plain: Your child can observe the sun, moon, and stars and notice patterns that happen regularly.

subject: Science
description: Patterns of movement of the sun, moon and stars as seen from Earth can be observed, described and predicted.
description_plain: Your child understands that the sun, moon, and stars move in patterns we can see, describe, and predict.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to construct an evidence-based account that young plants and animals are like, but not exactly like, their parents.
description_plain: Your child can observe young plants and animals and explain how they are similar but not exactly like their parents.

subject: Science
description: Young organisms are very much, but not exactly, like their parents, and also resemble other organisms of the same kind.
description_plain: Your child understands that young plants and animals look a lot like their parents and others of the same kind.

subject: Science
description: Read texts and use media to determine patterns in behavior of parents and offspring that help offspring survive.
description_plain: Your child can read and watch videos to learn how parents and their babies behave to help the babies survive.

subject: Science
description: Use materials to design a solution to a human problem by mimicking how plants and/or animals use their external parts to help them survive, grow and meet their needs.
description_plain: Your child can use materials to create a solution to a problem by copying how plants or animals use their body parts to survive.

subject: Science
description: All organisms have external parts that they use to perform daily functions.
description_plain: Your child understands that all living things have outside parts they use to do daily activities.

subject: Science
description: Use tools and materials to design and build a device that used light or sound to solve the problem of communicating over a distance.
description_plain: Your child can build a device using light or sound to help people communicate from far away.

subject: Science
