description: Plan and conduct an investigation to determine the effect of placing objects made with different materials in the path of a beam of light.
description_plain: Your child can test how different materials affect a beam of light when placed in its path.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to construct an evidence-based account that objects can be seen only when illuminated.
description_plain: Your child understands that we can only see objects when light shines on them.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct investigations to provide evidence that vibrating materials can make a sound and that sound can make materials vibrate.
description_plain: Your child can investigate how vibrating things make sound and how sound can make things vibrate.

subject: Science
description: Sound can make matter vibrate and vibrating matter can make sound.
description_plain: Your child understands that sound comes from vibrating things and can also make things vibrate.

subject: Science
description: Obtain information to identify where water is found on Earth and that it can be solid or liquid.
description_plain: Your child can find out where water is on Earth and that it can be either solid (ice) or liquid.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to represent the shapes and kinds of land and bodies of water in an area.
description_plain: Your child can make a model showing different land shapes and bodies of water in an area.

subject: Science
description: Compare multiple solutions designed to slow or prevent wind or water from changing the shape of the land.
description_plain: Your child can compare different ways to stop wind or water from changing the land's shape.

subject: Science
description: Wind and water can change the shape of the land; models can show the shape and these changes to the land.
description_plain: Your child understands that wind and water can change land shapes and can use models to show these changes.

subject: Science
description: Use information from several sources to provide evidence that Earth events can occur quickly or slowly.
description_plain: Your child can use information to show that some Earth events happen fast and others happen slowly.

subject: Science
description: Some events on Earth occur quickly; others can occur very slowly.
description_plain: Your child understands that some events on Earth happen quickly while others take a long time.

subject: Science
description: Make observations of plants and animals to compare the diversity of life in different habitats.
description_plain: Your child can observe plants and animals to compare how many different kinds live in various habitats.

subject: Science
description: A range of different organisms lives in different places.
description_plain: Your child understands that different kinds of living things live in different places.

subject: Science
description: Develop a simple model that mimics the function of an animal in dispersing seeds or pollinating plants.
description_plain: Your child can build a simple model that shows how an animal helps spread seeds or pollinate plants.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to determine if plants need sunlight and water to grow.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do an experiment to see if plants need sunlight and water to grow.

subject: Science
description: Plants depend on water and light to grow and on animals for pollination or to move their seeds around.
description_plain: Your child understands that plants need water and light to grow and rely on animals to help spread seeds or pollinate.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument with evidence that some changes caused by heating or cooling can be reversed and some cannot.
description_plain: Your child can explain with evidence which changes caused by heating or cooling can be undone and which cannot.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to construct an evidence-based account of how an object made of a small set of pieces can be disassembled and made into a new object.
description_plain: Your child can observe how an object made of parts can be taken apart and made into something new.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data obtained from testing different materials to determine which materials have the properties that are best suited for an intended purpose.
description_plain: Your child can test different materials and decide which ones work best for a specific use.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to describe and classify different kinds of materials by their observable properties.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do an investigation to describe and sort materials by what they look and feel like.

subject: Science
description: Matter exists as different substances that have observable different properties.
description_plain: Your child understands that matter is made of different substances that have different properties you can see or feel.

subject: Science
description: Make a claim about the merit of a design solution that reduces the impacts of a weather-related hazard.
description_plain: Your child can explain why a design helps reduce problems caused by bad weather.

subject: Science
description: A variety of weather hazards result from natural process; humans cannot eliminate weather-related hazards but can reduce their impacts.
description_plain: Your child understands that weather hazards happen naturally and people can’t stop them but can reduce their effects.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information to describe climates in different regions of the world.
description_plain: Your child can gather and combine information to describe climates in different parts of the world.

subject: Science
description: Represent data in tables and graphical displays to describe typical weather conditions expected during a particular season.
description_plain: Your child can show data in tables or graphs to describe usual weather during a season.

subject: Science
description: Climate describes patterns of typical weather conditions over different scales and variations; historical weather patterns can be analyzed.
description_plain: Your child understands that climate means the usual weather patterns over time and can study past weather.

subject: Science
description: Make a claim about the merit of a solution to a problem caused when the environment changes and the types of plants and animals that live there may change.
description_plain: Your child can explain why a solution helps when changes in the environment affect which plants and animals live there.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument with evidence that in a particular habitat some organisms can survive well, some survive less well and some cannot survive at all.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to show that in one habitat some animals survive well, some less well, and some not at all.

subject: Science
description: Sometimes differences in characteristics between individuals of the same species provide advantages in survival and reproduction.
description_plain: Your child understands that differences between animals of the same kind can help them survive and have babies.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to construct an explanation for how the variations in characteristics among individuals of the same species may provide advantages in surviving, finding mates and reproducing.
description_plain: Your child can explain how differences in animals help them survive, find mates, and have babies.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data from fossils to provide evidence of the organisms and the environments in which they lived long ago.
description_plain: Your child can study fossils to learn about animals and environments from long ago.

subject: Science
description: Some living organisms resemble organisms that once lived on Earth .
description_plain: Your child understands that some living things look like others that lived long ago on Earth.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to support the explanation that traits can be influenced by the environment
description_plain: Your child can use examples to show how the environment can change the traits of living things.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data to provide evidence that plants and animals have traits inherited from parents and that variation of these traits exists in a group of similar organisms.
description_plain: Your child looks at information to show that plants and animals get traits from their parents and that these traits can be different within a group.

subject: Science
description: Different organisms vary in how they look and function because they have different inherited information; the environment also affects the traits that an organism develops.
description_plain: Your child understands that living things look and act differently because of inherited traits and the environment.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument that some animals form groups that help members survive.
description_plain: Your child can explain why some animals live in groups to help each other survive.

subject: Science
description: Being part of a group helps animals obtain food, defend themselves and cope with changes.
description_plain: Your child understands that being in a group helps animals find food, protect themselves, and handle changes.

subject: Science
description: Develop models to describe that organisms have unique and diverse life cycles but all have in common birth, growth, reproduction and death.
description_plain: Your child can create models to show that all living things go through birth, growth, reproduction, and death, even though their life cycles are different.

subject: Science
description: Organisms have unique and diverse life cycles.
description_plain: Your child understands that living things have different life cycles.

subject: Science
description: Define a simple design problem that can be solved by applying scientific ideas about magnets.
description_plain: Your child can describe a simple problem that can be solved by using what they know about magnets.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions to determine cause-and-effect relationships of electric or magnetic interactions between two objects not in contact with each other.
description_plain: Your child asks questions to find out how electric or magnetic forces can affect objects without touching them.

subject: Science
description: Objects in contact exert forces on each other; electric and magnetic forces between a pair of objects do not require contact.
description_plain: Your child understands that objects push or pull each other when touching, and electric or magnetic forces can act even when objects are not touching.

subject: Science
description: Make observations and/or measurements of an object's motion to provide evidence that a pattern can be used to predict future motion.
description_plain: Your child watches and measures how something moves to show that its movement follows a pattern that can help predict what will happen next.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to provide evidence of the effects of balanced and unbalanced forces on the motion of an object.
description_plain: Your child plans and does an experiment to show how balanced and unbalanced forces affect how something moves.

subject: Science
description: Patterns of motion can be used to predict future motion.
description_plain: Your child understands that movement follows patterns that can help predict future motion.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple solutions to reduce the impacts of natural Earth processes on humans.
description_plain: Your child can think of and compare different ways to reduce how natural Earth events affect people.

subject: Science
description: A variety of hazards result from natural process; humans cannot eliminate natural hazards but can reduce their impacts' effect.
description_plain: Your child understands that natural events can cause dangers, and while people can't stop these events, they can lessen their effects.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information to describe that energy and fuels are derived from natural resources and their uses affect the environment.
description_plain: Your child gathers and combines information to explain that energy and fuels come from natural sources and using them can affect the environment.

subject: Science
description: Energy and fuels that humans use are derived from natural sources and their use affects the environment in multiple ways.
description_plain: Your child understands that energy and fuels come from nature and using them can change the environment in many ways.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data from maps to describe patterns of Earth's features.
description_plain: Your child looks at map data to find and describe patterns in Earth's features.

subject: Science
description: Earth's physical features occur in patterns.
description_plain: Your child understands that Earth's physical features appear in patterns.

subject: Science
description: Make observations and/or measurements to provide evidence of the effects of weathering or the rate of erosion by water, ice, wind, or vegetation.
description_plain: Your child makes observations or measurements to show how weathering or erosion by water, ice, wind, or plants changes the land.

subject: Science
description: Four major earth systems interact.
description_plain: Your child understands that Earth's four main systems work together.

subject: Science
description: Identify evidence from patterns in rock formations and fossils in rock layers to support an explanation for changes in a landscape over time.
description_plain: Your child finds evidence in rocks and fossils to explain how landscapes have changed over time.

subject: Science
description: Use a model to describe that animals receive different types of information through their senses, process the information in their brain, and respond to the information in different ways.
description_plain: Your child uses a model to show how animals get information through their senses, think about it in their brain, and respond in different ways.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument that plants and animals have internal and external structures that function to support survival, growth, behavior and reproduction.
description_plain: Your child can explain that plants and animals have body parts inside and outside that help them survive, grow, behave, and reproduce.

subject: Science
description: Organisms have both internal and external structures that serve various functions.
description_plain: Your child understands that living things have inside and outside parts that do different jobs.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple solutions that use patterns to transfer information.
description_plain: Your child can come up with and compare different ways to use patterns to send and receive information.

subject: Science
description: Patterns can encode, send, receive and decode information.
description_plain: Your child understands that patterns can be used to send, receive, and understand information.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe that light reflecting from objects and entering the eye allows objects to be seen.
description_plain: Your child can make a model to show that we see objects because light bounces off them and enters our eyes.

subject: Science
description: An object can be seen when light reflected from its surface enters the eyes.
description_plain: Your child understands that we see things when light reflects off their surfaces and goes into our eyes.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model of waves to describe patterns in terms of amplitude and wavelength and that waves can cause objects to move.
description_plain: Your child can create a model of waves to show their patterns and how waves can make things move.

subject: Science
description: Waves are regular patterns of motion.
description_plain: Your child understands that waves move in regular patterns.

subject: Science
description: Apply scientific ideas to design, test and refine a device that converts energy from one form to another.
description_plain: Your child uses scientific ideas to design, test, and improve a device that changes energy from one type to another.

subject: Science
description: Energy can be produced, used or released by converting stored energy.
description_plain: Your child understands that energy can be made, used, or released by changing stored energy into other forms.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions and predict outcomes about the changes in energy that occur when objects collide.
description_plain: Your child asks questions and guesses what will happen when objects collide and their energy changes.

subject: Science
description: When objects collide contact forces transfer so as to change objects' motion.
description_plain: Your child understands that when objects hit each other, forces change how they move.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to provide evidence that energy can be transferred from place to place by sound, light, heat and electric currents.
description_plain: Your child makes observations to show that energy can move from one place to another through sound, light, heat, and electricity.

subject: Science
description: Energy can be moved from place to place.
description_plain: Your child understands that energy can be transferred from one place to another.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to construct an explanation relating the speed of an object to the energy of that object.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence to explain that an object moving faster has more energy.

subject: Science
description: The faster an object moves the more energy it has.
description_plain: Your child understands that the faster something moves, the more energy it has.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information about ways individual communities use science ideas to protect the Earth's resources and environment.
description_plain: Your child can find and combine information about how different communities use science to protect Earth's resources and environment.

subject: Science
description: Societal activities have had major effects on land, ocean, atmosphere and even outer space.
description_plain: Your child understands that human activities have greatly changed land, oceans, air, and even space.

subject: Science
description: Describe and graph the amounts and percentages of saltwater and freshwater in various reservoirs to provide evidence about the distribution of water on Earth.
description_plain: Your child can describe and make graphs showing how much saltwater and freshwater are in different places on Earth.

subject: Science
description: Most of Earth's water is in the ocean and much of Earth's freshwater in glaciers or underground.
description_plain: Your child knows that most of Earth's water is saltwater in oceans, and much freshwater is frozen in glaciers or underground.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model using an example to describe ways the geosphere, biosphere, hydrosphere and/or atmosphere interact.
description_plain: Your child can create a model to show how Earth's land, living things, water, and air work together.

subject: Science
description: Earth's major systems interact in multiple ways to affect Earth's surface materials and processes.
description_plain: Your child understands that Earth's main systems interact in many ways to change the surface and natural processes.

subject: Science
description: Represent data in graphical displays to reveal patterns of daily changes in length and direction of shadows, day and night, and the seasonal appearance of some stars in the night sky.
description_plain: Your child can make graphs to show daily changes in shadows, day and night, and how some stars appear in different seasons.

subject: Science
description: Earth's orbit and rotation and the orbit of the moon around earth cause observable patterns.
description_plain: Your child knows that Earth's and the moon's movements cause patterns we can observe in the sky.

subject: Science
description: Support an argument that differences in the apparent brightness of the sun compared to other stars is due to their relative distances from the Earth.
description_plain: Your child can explain that stars look brighter or dimmer mainly because of how far away they are from Earth.

subject: Science
description: Stars range greatly in size and distance from Earth, and this can explain their relative brightness.
description_plain: Your child understands that stars are different sizes and distances from Earth, which affects how bright they seem.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe the movement of matter among plants, animals, decomposers, and the environment.
description_plain: Your child can build a model to show how matter moves between plants, animals, decomposers, and the environment.

subject: Science
description: Matter cycles between air and soil and among plants, animals and microbes as these organisms live and die.
description_plain: Your child knows that matter cycles through air, soil, plants, animals, and microbes as they live and die.

subject: Science
description: Support an argument that plants get the materials they need for growth chiefly from air and water.
description_plain: Your child can explain that plants get most of what they need to grow from air and water.

subject: Science
description: Plants acquire their material from growth chiefly from air and water.
description_plain: Your child understands that plants mainly get their growth materials from air and water.

subject: Science
description: Use models to describe that energy in animals' food (used for body repair, growth and motion and to maintain body warmth) was once energy from the sun.
description_plain: Your child can use models to show that the energy animals get from food originally came from the sun.

subject: Science
description: The energy released from food was once energy from the sun.
description_plain: Your child knows that the energy released from food was once energy from the sun.

subject: Science
description: Support an argument that the gravitational force exerted by Earth on objects is directed down.
description_plain: Your child can explain that Earth's gravity pulls things downward.

subject: Science
description: The gravitational force of Earth acting on an object near Earth's surface pulls that object toward the planet's center.
description_plain: Your child understands that Earth's gravity pulls objects toward its center.

subject: Science
description: Conduct an investigation to determine whether the mixing of two or more substances results in new substances.
description_plain: Your child can do experiments to see if mixing substances creates new substances.

subject: Science
description: Measure and graph quantities to provide evidence that regardless of the type of change that occurs when heating, cooling or mixing substances, the total weight of matter is conserved.
description_plain: Your child can measure and graph data to show that matter's total weight stays the same when substances are heated, cooled, or mixed.

subject: Science
description: Chemical Reactions that occur when substances are mixed can be identified by the emergence of substances with different properties; the total mass remains the same.
description_plain: Your child knows that when substances mix and react, new substances with different properties can form, but the total mass stays the same.

subject: Science
description: Make observations and measurements to identify materials based on their properties.
description_plain: Your child can observe and measure things to identify materials by their properties.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe that matter is made of particles too small to be seen.
description_plain: Your child can create a model to show that matter is made of tiny particles too small to see.

subject: Science
description: Matter exists as particles that are too small to be seen; measurements of a variety of observable properties can be used to identify particular materials.
description_plain: Your child understands that matter is made of tiny particles and can use measurements of properties to identify materials.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions to clarify evidence of the factors that have caused the rise in global temperatures over the past century.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to better understand what has caused global temperatures to rise over the last 100 years.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions to clarify evidence of the factors that have caused the rise in global temperatures over the past century.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to better understand what has caused global temperatures to rise over the last 100 years.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions to clarify evidence of the factors that have caused the rise in global temperatures over the past century.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to better understand what has caused global temperatures to rise over the last 100 years.

subject: Science
description: Human activities affect global warming. Decisions to reduce the impact of global warming depend on understanding climate science, engineering capabilities, and social dynamics.
description_plain: Your child understands that human actions affect global warming and that making good choices depends on knowing about climate, technology, and society.

subject: Science
description: Human activities affect global warming. Decisions to reduce the impact of global warming depend on understanding climate science, engineering capabilities, and social dynamics.
description_plain: Your child understands that human actions affect global warming and that making good choices depends on knowing about climate, technology, and society.

subject: Science
description: Human activities affect global warming. Decisions to reduce the impact of global warming depend on understanding climate science, engineering capabilities, and social dynamics.
description_plain: Your child understands that human actions affect global warming and that making good choices depends on knowing about climate, technology, and society.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument supported by evidence for how increases in human population and per-capita consumption of natural resources impact Earth's systems.
description_plain: Your child can explain with evidence how more people and using more resources affect Earth's systems.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument supported by evidence for how increases in human population and per-capita consumption of natural resources impact Earth's systems.
description_plain: Your child can explain with evidence how more people and using more resources affect Earth's systems.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument supported by evidence for how increases in human population and per-capita consumption of natural resources impact Earth's systems.

subject: Social Studies
description: Demonstrate curiosity, imagination, and eagerness to learn more (Creativity and Innovation).
description_plain: Your child shows curiosity and excitement to learn new things and be creative.

subject: Social Studies
description: Determine how to spend money depending on values and choices (Self-Advocacy and Initiative).
description_plain: Your child decides how to spend money based on what is important to them.

subject: Social Studies
description: Make personal financial decisions based on spending options (Self-Advocacy and Initiative).
description_plain: Your child makes simple choices about spending money.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the difference between a want and a need and how that impacts purchasing decisions when resources are limited. For example: Buying a healthy snack vs. a candy bar, new shoes vs. a new toy, or a coat vs. a new game.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between wants and needs and understands how that affects what they buy when money is limited.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how money gives people the ability to buy goods and services.
description_plain: Your child understands that money lets people buy things and services.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize choices people make with their money and explain how financial decisions are made.
description_plain: Your child notices how people make choices with their money and can explain those decisions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe choices people make about how to use the money they earn.
description_plain: Your child can describe how people decide to use the money they earn.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize how personal actions have had a positive or negative impact with feedback as needed.
description_plain: Your child recognizes how their actions can have good or bad effects and can accept feedback.

subject: Social Studies
description: Within democratic traditions, articulate personal strengths and challenges using information and communication technologies to express themselves.
description_plain: Your child can talk about their strengths and challenges and use technology to express themselves.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the difference between democratic decision-making and decisions made by authorities. For example: A parent, teacher, principal, and a police officer.
description_plain: Your child can explain the difference between decisions made by groups of people and those made by leaders like parents or teachers.

subject: Social Studies
description: Contribute to making and maintaining class community decisions.
description_plain: Your child helps make and keep class decisions together.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how a class rule may promote fairness and resolve conflict and compare against a rule that may not.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a class rule helps fairness and solves problems and compare it to a rule that might not.

subject: Social Studies
description: Create and follow classroom rules.
description_plain: Your child can create and follow rules in the classroom.

subject: Social Studies
description: Participate in making logical decisions using democratic principles.
description_plain: Your child takes part in making fair decisions using democratic ideas.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and reflect upon personal connections to community systems.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and think about how they connect to their community.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare one's attitudes and beliefs about civic participation to others.
description_plain: Your child can compare their thoughts about being part of the community with others.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the difference between fact and opinion.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts and opinions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Practice citizenship skills when working with others including courtesy, honesty, and fairness.
description_plain: Your child practices being a good citizen by being polite, honest, and fair when working with others.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the qualities of an informed and engaged citizen.
description_plain: Your child can explain what it means to be an informed and involved citizen.

subject: Social Studies
description: Differentiate among examples of civic participation. For example: Describe personal connections to community events, such as voting, debating, running for office, advocating, fundraising, and volunteering.
description_plain: Your child can recognize different ways people participate in their community, like voting, debating, or volunteering.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify ways in which civic participation takes place across multiple groups.
description_plain: Your child can name different ways people take part in their communities with various groups.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize that problems can be identified, and possible solutions can be created when making choices.
description_plain: Your child understands that problems can be found and solved by making choices.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize and engage in ways to use another individual's items. For example: Asking for permission to share and taking turns.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use someone else's things by asking first and taking turns.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the costs and benefits of a choice an individual makes when acquiring an item.
description_plain: Your child can talk about what is gained and what is given up when choosing to get something.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how individuals make choices based on needs and wants.
description_plain: Your child understands that people make choices based on what they need and want.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize and describe cause-and-effect relationships between people and their surroundings.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people and their surroundings affect each other.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare attitudes and beliefs as an individual to others.
description_plain: Your child can compare their own thoughts and beliefs with those of others.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify how the environment, geographic features, and climate impact lifestyles. For example: Food, sports, shelter, transportation, school, etc.
description_plain: Your child knows how the environment, land features, and weather affect how people live, like what they eat, play, and where they live.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify ways students' lives are similar and different from those in other communities.
description_plain: Your child can point out ways their life is like or different from kids in other places.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify how the environment influences the way people live.
description_plain: Your child understands that the environment changes how people live.

subject: Social Studies
description: Find information using geographic technologies.
description_plain: Your child can find information using tools like maps or computers.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use geographic tools to describe places. For example: Globes, maps, and GPS.
description_plain: Your child uses tools like globes, maps, and GPS to describe places.

subject: Social Studies
description: Distinguish between a map and a globe as ways to show places people live.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between a map and a globe as ways to show where people live.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize that geographic tools represent places and spaces.
description_plain: Your child knows that maps and globes show places and spaces.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize and describe patterns in the sequence of events from the past.
description_plain: Your child can notice and describe patterns in the order of past events.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain why knowing the order of events is important.
description_plain: Your child understands why it is important to know the order of events.

subject: Social Studies
description: Sequence information using words. For example: Present, future, days, weeks, months, years, first, next, last, before, and after.
description_plain: Your child can put events in order using words like first, next, last, before, and after.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explore differences and similarities in the lives of children and families from different time periods by using a variety of sources. For example: Personal artifacts and stories, texts, pictures, and videos from different societies.
description_plain: Your child explores how kids and families lived differently or the same long ago by looking at stories, pictures, and objects.

subject: Social Studies
description: Understand that the sequence of events is important when describing the past.
description_plain: Your child understands that the order of events helps us talk about the past.

subject: Social Studies
description: Demonstrate curiosity about the past.
description_plain: Your child shows interest and asks questions about things that happened in the past.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize and describe cause-and-effect relationships about the past.
description_plain: Your child can explain how one event in the past caused another.

subject: Social Studies
description: Provide rationale about something from the past using statements. For example: Because, as a result, I know ___.
description_plain: Your child can explain why something happened in the past using words like because and as a result.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify information from primary and/or secondary sources that answer questions about the past and contribute to the collective memory.
description_plain: Your child can find answers about the past by looking at stories, pictures, or objects.

subject: Social Studies
description: Ask questions about the past using question starters. For example: What did? Where did? When did? Which did? Who did? Why did? How did? From whose perspective?
description_plain: Your child asks questions about the past using words like what, where, when, who, why, and how.

subject: Social Studies
description: Ask questions and discuss ideas about the past.
description_plain: Your child asks questions and talks about ideas related to the past.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply economic reasoning skills to make informed personal financial decisions.
description_plain: Your child can use economic thinking to make smart choices about their own money.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the origins, structures, and functions of governments to evaluate the impact on citizens and the global society.
description_plain: Your child can study how governments start, work, and affect people and the world.

subject: Social Studies
description: Express an understanding of how civic participation affects policy by applying the rights and responsibilities of a citizen.
description_plain: Your child understands how being a citizen and taking part in the community can change laws and rules.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate how scarce resources are allocated in societies through the analysis of individual choice, market interaction, and public policy.
description_plain: Your child can look at how limited resources are shared through choices, markets, and government rules.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine the characteristics of places and regions, and the changing nature among geographic and human interactions.
description_plain: Your child studies places and regions and how people and the environment change over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply geographic representations and perspectives to analyze human movement, spatial patterns, systems, and the connections and relationships among them.
description_plain: Your child uses maps and other tools to study how people move and connect in different places.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze historical time periods and patterns of continuity and change, through multiple perspectives, within and among cultures and societies.
description_plain: Your child looks at history from many views to understand what stays the same and what changes in cultures and societies.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply the process of inquiry to examine and analyze how historical knowledge is viewed, constructed, and interpreted.
description_plain: Your child learns how to ask questions and think about how history is made and understood.

subject: Social Studies
description: Demonstrate curiosity, imagination, and eagerness to learn more.
description_plain: Your child shows curiosity and excitement to learn new things.

subject: Social Studies
description: Determine different choices they can make with their money and how those choices may affect others.
description_plain: Your child thinks about different ways to use their money and how those choices affect others.

subject: Social Studies
description: Consider how to earn, spend, share, and save money knowing funds in any scenario are limited.
description_plain: Your child understands that money is limited and talks about how to earn, spend, share, and save it.

subject: Social Studies
description: Find the value of a collection of a variety of coins.
description_plain: Your child can count the total value of different coins together.

subject: Social Studies
description: Define types of transactions such as deposit, purchase, borrow, donate, and barter.
description_plain: Your child knows words for different money activities like depositing, buying, borrowing, giving, and trading.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify types of currency and how currency is used. For example: Cash, coins, credit card, and debit card.
description_plain: Your child can name different kinds of money like cash, coins, credit cards, and debit cards and how they are used.

subject: Social Studies
description: Discuss ways to earn or receive money.
description_plain: Your child talks about ways people can earn or get money.

subject: Social Studies
description: Plan how to spend, share, and save money.
description_plain: Your child can plan how to spend money, share it with others, and save some for later.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize diversity among notable people, places, holidays, and civic symbols that constitute multiple perspectives within society.
description_plain: Your child understands that people, places, holidays, and symbols come from many different backgrounds and views.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare attitudes and beliefs as an individual to others.
description_plain: Your child can compare their own thoughts and beliefs with those of other people.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the significance of major civic holidays. For example: Veterans Day, Martin Luther King Day, Independence Day, Presidents' Day, Memorial Day, Juneteenth, Labor Day, Indigenous Peoples Day, and César Chávez Day.
description_plain: Your child can explain why important holidays like Veterans Day and Independence Day matter.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and explain the relevance of significant civic places. For example: The state and national Capitol, the White House, and sites within the local community.
description_plain: Your child can name important civic places like the Capitol or White House and explain why they are special.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and explain the meaning of various civic symbols important to diverse community groups. For example: The American flag, the National Anthem, Statue of Liberty, Mount Rushmore, Crazy Horse Memorial, Liberty Bell, Emancipation Proclamation, a yellow sash (i.e., for women's rights), tribal flags of Native Nations whose ancestral homelands include present-day Colorado, LGBTQ Pride Flag, and the Colorado Flag.
description_plain: Your child can identify important symbols like the American flag or the Liberty Bell and explain what they mean to different groups.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and explain the relevance of notable civic leaders from different community groups, including African American, Latino, Asian American, Hawaiian/Pacific Islander, Indigenous Peoples, LGBTQ, and religious minorities.
description_plain: Your child can recognize important leaders from different groups and explain why they are important.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and explain how the significance of notable people, places, holidays, and civic symbols reflect the origins and values of the government and its citizens.
description_plain: Your child understands how important people, places, holidays, and symbols show what the government and citizens value.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize how members of a community rely on each other, considering personal contributions as applicable.
description_plain: Your child understands that people in a community depend on each other and can think about how they help.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize emotional responses to ideas that differ from one's own when working in a group setting.
description_plain: Your child notices how people feel when others have different ideas in a group.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize personal characteristics, preferences, thoughts, and feelings regarding leadership.
description_plain: Your child understands their own likes, thoughts, and feelings about being a leader.

subject: Social Studies
description: Listen and consider the ideas of others in order to make decisions as a group.
description_plain: Your child listens to others and thinks about their ideas to help make group decisions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the qualities of an effective team. For example: Respectful disagreement, advocating for self and others, sharing responsibilities, and including others' perspectives.
description_plain: Your child can describe what makes a good team, like respecting others and sharing tasks.

subject: Social Studies
description: Demonstrate the ability to be both a leader and team member.
description_plain: Your child can show how to be a leader and also a helpful team member.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the attributes of a responsible team member and how they communicate with others.
description_plain: Your child knows what makes someone a responsible team member and how they talk with others.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the characteristics of responsible leaders and how they communicate with others.
description_plain: Your child can describe what responsible leaders do and how they communicate.

subject: Social Studies
description: Summarize how effective groups have responsible leaders and team members.
description_plain: Your child understands that good groups have responsible leaders and team members.

subject: Social Studies
description: Demonstrate an understanding of cause and effect related to personal decisions such as jobs.
description_plain: Your child can show how personal choices, like jobs, can cause different results.

subject: Social Studies
description: Ask questions to learn more about careers and other life pursuits.
description_plain: Your child asks questions to learn more about jobs and other life goals.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare at least two different job choices that individuals have with respect to the benefits and costs of each job.
description_plain: Your child can compare two jobs by talking about what is good and not so good about each.

subject: Social Studies
description: Give examples of different types of jobs held by family members and/or individuals in the local community.
description_plain: Your child can give examples of jobs that family members or people in the community have.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the goods and/or services that different types of businesses produce in the local community.
description_plain: Your child can name goods or services made by different businesses in the community.

subject: Social Studies
description: Understand that individuals work in different types of jobs to earn an income.
description_plain: Your child understands that people work different jobs to earn money.

subject: Social Studies
description: Make observations and draw conclusions about the relationship between groups of people and their surroundings.
description_plain: Your child can observe and explain how groups of people relate to where they live.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and reflect upon personal connections to one or more families within the community.
description_plain: Your child can identify and think about their connections to families in the community.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare and contrast at least two different communities with respect to schools, neighborhoods, and culture.
description_plain: Your child can compare two communities by looking at their schools, neighborhoods, and cultures.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze how weather, climate and environmental characteristics influence individuals and the cultural characteristics of a family.
description_plain: Your child can explain how weather and environment affect people and family traditions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Provide examples of how individuals and families interact with their environment.
description_plain: Your child can give examples of how people and families use and change their environment.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the characteristics of a community and how they are influenced by the environment.
description_plain: Your child can describe what makes a community and how the environment shapes it.

subject: Social Studies
description: Find information using geographic technologies.
description_plain: Your child can find information using tools like maps or GPS.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify key attributes of a variety of geographic tools. For example: Globes, maps, and GPS.
description_plain: Your child can identify different tools like globes, maps, and GPS and what they do.

subject: Social Studies
description: Create simple maps showing both human and natural features.
description_plain: Your child can make simple maps that show places made by people and nature.

subject: Social Studies
description: Distinguish between land and water on a map and globe.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between land and water on maps and globes.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recite an address including city, state, and country, and explain how those labels help find places on a map.
description_plain: Your child can say their address with city, state, and country and explain how this helps find places on a map.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe locations using terms related to direction and distance. For example: Forward and backward, left and right, near and far, is next to, and close.
description_plain: Your child can describe where things are using words like near, far, left, right, and next to.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain that maps and globes are different representations of Earth.
description_plain: Your child understands that maps and globes both show Earth but in different ways.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use geographic terms and tools to describe places and spaces.
description_plain: Your child can use words and tools to describe places and spaces.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and explain several cultural perspectives that constitute our diverse society.
description_plain: Your child can identify and explain different cultural views that make our society diverse.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare one's attitudes and beliefs to others.
description_plain: Your child can compare their own attitudes and beliefs with those of others.

subject: Social Studies
description: Understand that the United States is made up of the diverse perspectives and traditions of many cultures. For example: The diversity of foods available in the local community.
description_plain: Your child understands that the United States includes many cultures and traditions, like different foods in the community.

subject: Social Studies
description: Discuss common and unique characteristics of different cultures, including African American, Latino, Asian American, Hawaiian/Pacific Islanders, Indigenous Peoples, LGBTQ, and religious minorities, using multiple sources of information.
description_plain: Your child can talk about what makes different cultures special and what they have in common, like African American, Latino, Asian American, Hawaiian/Pacific Islanders, Indigenous Peoples, LGBTQ, and religious groups, using different kinds of information.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the unique characteristics of oneself as well as the similarities and differences between themselves and others. For example: Eye color, ability, individuality, family composition, etc.
description_plain: Your child can recognize what makes them unique and also see how they are similar or different from others, like eye color, abilities, and family.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how the diverse perspectives and traditions of families from many cultures have shaped the United States.
description_plain: Your child understands how families from many cultures have helped shape the United States by sharing their traditions and views.

subject: Social Studies
description: Demonstrate curiosity about patterns from the past.
description_plain: Your child shows interest in noticing patterns from the past.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize and describe cause-and-effect relationships and patterns from the past.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain how one event in the past caused another and notice patterns.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify information from primary and/or secondary sources that answer questions about patterns and chronological order of events from the past.
description_plain: Your child can find answers to questions about past events by looking at original and other sources of information.

subject: Social Studies
description: Distinguish between primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between original sources from the past and information created later.

subject: Social Studies
description: Determine events from the past, present, and future, using the components of a calendar.
description_plain: Your child can use a calendar to figure out if events happened in the past, are happening now, or will happen in the future.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the organizational components of a calendar. For example: Year, months, weeks, days, and notable events.
description_plain: Your child can name parts of a calendar like years, months, weeks, days, and special events.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use words related to time, sequence, and change. For example: Past, present, future, change, first, next, and last.
description_plain: Your child uses words about time and order, like past, present, future, first, next, and last.

subject: Social Studies
description: Arrange life events in chronological order.
description_plain: Your child can put events from their life in the order they happened.

subject: Social Studies
description: Ask questions and discuss ideas about patterns and chronological order of events from the past.
description_plain: Your child asks questions and talks about how events from the past happen in order and follow patterns.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply economic reasoning skills to make informed personal financial decisions.
description_plain: Your child can use economic thinking to make smart choices about their own money.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the origins, structures, and functions of governments to evaluate the impact on citizens and the global society.
description_plain: Your child can study how governments start, work, and affect people and the world.

subject: Social Studies
description: Express an understanding of how civic participation affects policy by applying the rights and responsibilities of a citizen.
description_plain: Your child understands how being a citizen and taking part in the community can change laws and rules.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate how scarce resources are allocated in societies through the analysis of individual choice, market interaction, and public policy.
description_plain: Your child can look at how limited resources are shared through choices, markets, and government rules.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine the characteristics of places and regions, and the changing nature among geographic and human interactions.
description_plain: Your child studies places and regions and how people and the environment change over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply geographic representations and perspectives to analyze human movement, spatial patterns, systems, and the connections and relationships among them.
description_plain: Your child uses maps and other tools to study how people move and connect in different places.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze historical time periods and patterns of continuity and change, through multiple perspectives, within and among cultures and societies.
description_plain: Your child looks at history from many views to understand what stays the same and what changes in cultures and societies.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply the process of inquiry to examine and analyze how historical knowledge is viewed, constructed, and interpreted.
description_plain: Your child learns how to ask questions and think about how history is made and understood.

subject: Social Studies
description: Demonstrate an understanding of cause and effect related to different financial decisions.
description_plain: Your child understands how different money choices can cause different results.

subject: Social Studies
description: Understand how to reduce risk depending on the financial choices they make.
description_plain: Your child knows ways to lower risks when making money decisions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify consequences (positive and negative) of a financial decision.
description_plain: Your child can name good and bad results of a money choice.

subject: Social Studies
description: Solve simple financial word problems to aid in making financial decisions. For example: A video game costs $20. If I earn $5 per week in allowance, I will need to save for ____ weeks to earn enough money.
description_plain: Your child can solve simple money problems, like figuring out how many weeks to save allowance to buy a video game.

subject: Social Studies
description: Predict positive and negative consequences when making financial decisions. For example: If… Then… decision making.
description_plain: Your child can guess what good or bad things might happen before making a money decision.

subject: Social Studies
description: Acknowledge that financial priorities vary among people and communities for goods and services. For example: Going to the hairdresser, buying brand name items.
description_plain: Your child knows that people and communities have different money priorities, like going to the hairdresser or buying brand-name items.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize that different goods and services have different monetary values.
description_plain: Your child understands that different things and services cost different amounts of money.

subject: Social Studies
description: Classify financial goals. For example: A need or want, or short-term or long-term goals.
description_plain: Your child can sort money goals into groups like needs or wants, and short-term or long-term goals.

subject: Social Studies
description: Assess priorities when making financial decisions.
description_plain: Your child can decide what is most important when making money choices.

subject: Social Studies
description: Investigate costs and benefits to make informed financial decisions.
description_plain: Your child looks at the costs and benefits to make smart money decisions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Appropriately express a range of emotions to communicate personal ideas/needs.
description_plain: Your child can show different feelings in a way that helps them share their ideas and needs.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare attitudes and beliefs as an individual to others.
description_plain: Your child can compare their own thoughts and beliefs with those of others.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify services provided by the local or state government. For example: police and fire protection, maintenance of roads, and snow removal.
description_plain: Your child can name services the local or state government provides, like police, fire protection, road care, and snow removal.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify qualities of positive leadership and how leaders contribute to a group or community.
description_plain: Your child knows what makes a good leader and how leaders help groups or communities.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the roles and characteristics of various leaders at the local, state, and national levels. For example: The President of the United States, the Governor of Colorado, and the city mayor.
description_plain: Your child can name leaders at local, state, and national levels, like the mayor, governor, and president, and know what they do.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the role(s)of government. For example: Establish order, provide security, and accomplish common goals.
description_plain: Your child understands what government does, like keeping order, providing safety, and reaching common goals.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the roles and characteristics of people who govern different communities.
description_plain: Your child can explain what people who govern communities do and what makes them good leaders.

subject: Social Studies
description: Model positive behaviors for others.
description_plain: Your child shows good behavior that others can follow.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and reflect upon personal connections to community systems.
description_plain: Your child thinks about how they connect to their community and its systems.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the characteristics that enable a community member to engage in the community responsibly and effectively.
description_plain: Your child can describe what qualities help someone be a responsible and helpful community member.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and compare examples of civic responsibilities, such as volunteering in the community, that are important to privileged and marginalized individuals, families, and communities.
description_plain: Your child can find and compare examples of ways people help their community, like volunteering, including people from different backgrounds.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe ways in which you can take an active part in improving your school or community.
description_plain: Your child can describe ways they can help improve their school or community.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze how community members, including African American, Latino, Asian American, Hawaiian/Pacific Islander, Indigenous Peoples, LGBTQ, and religious minorities advocate for their interests and responsibly influence decisions in their community.
description_plain: Your child learns how different community members, including those from various cultural and minority groups, speak up for their needs and help make decisions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare ways that people may effectively express their ideas and viewpoints while being respectful to others.
description_plain: Your child can compare ways people share their ideas respectfully.

subject: Social Studies
description: Investigate ways in which ideas and actions can improve communities.
description_plain: Your child explores how ideas and actions can make communities better.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize problems that arise from scarcity and their respective solutions.
description_plain: Your child can recognize problems caused by having limited resources and think of ways to solve them.

subject: Social Studies
description: Demonstrate an understanding of cause and effect related to personal decisions.
description_plain: Your child understands how their personal choices can cause different results.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify possible solutions when there are limited resources and unlimited wants.
description_plain: Your child can find possible solutions when there are not enough resources but many wants.

subject: Social Studies
description: Give examples of choices people make when resources are scarce.
description_plain: Your child can give examples of choices people make when there are not enough resources.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and categorize goods and services and provide examples of each.
description_plain: Your child can name and sort things people buy (goods) and services people do for others, giving examples of each.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain scarcity of goods and resources.
description_plain: Your child understands that sometimes there are not enough goods or resources for everyone.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how scarcity of resources means individuals may not have access to the goods and services they want or need.
description_plain: Your child understands that when resources are limited, people might not get all the things they want or need.

subject: Social Studies
description: Make observations and draw conclusions about the relationship between a community and their environment.
description_plain: Your child can observe and explain how a community and its environment affect each other.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize problems within a community related to the environment and their respective solutions.
description_plain: Your child can find problems in a community related to the environment and suggest ways to fix them.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify examples of how culture and lifestyle are impacted by environmental characteristics.
description_plain: Your child can give examples of how the environment affects people's culture and way of life.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify examples of how human activity influences environmental characteristics of a place over time.
description_plain: Your child can show how people’s actions change the environment over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how the environment influences why people settle in certain areas.
description_plain: Your child understands why people choose to live in certain places because of the environment.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how communities manage and use nonrenewable and renewable resources.
description_plain: Your child can explain how communities use and take care of resources that can be used up or replaced.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how people in communities manage, modify, and depend on their environment.
description_plain: Your child understands how people in communities depend on, change, and take care of their environment.

subject: Social Studies
description: Find information using geographic technologies. For example: GPS and satellite imagery.
description_plain: Your child can find information using tools like GPS and satellite pictures.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify key attributes of a variety of geographic tools. For example: Globes, maps, and a compass rose.
description_plain: Your child can recognize important parts of tools like globes, maps, and compass roses.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and locate cultural, human, political, and natural features using map keys and legends.
description_plain: Your child can use map keys and legends to find and name cultural, human, political, and natural features.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the hemispheres, Equator/Prime Meridian, and the North/South poles.
description_plain: Your child can name the hemispheres, the Equator, Prime Meridian, and the North and South Poles.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the purpose of the map being presented. For example: A weather map vs. street map.
description_plain: Your child can tell what a map is for, like knowing the difference between a weather map and a street map.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use map keys, legends, symbols, intermediate directions, and a compass rose to locate and describe spaces and places.
description_plain: Your child can use map keys, symbols, directions, and a compass rose to find and describe places.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use geographic terms and tools to locate and describe spatial patterns and places.
description_plain: Your child can use geographic words and tools to find and describe places and patterns on maps.

subject: Social Studies
description: Investigate to make observations and draw conclusions about neighborhoods and communities.
description_plain: Your child can explore and explain what they notice about neighborhoods and communities.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize and describe patterns within and between neighborhoods and communities.
description_plain: Your child can find and describe patterns inside and between neighborhoods and communities.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize and describe cause-and-effect relationships and patterns in everyday experiences.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain cause-and-effect and patterns in everyday life.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the interactions and contributions of various people and cultures that have lived in or migrated to neighborhoods and/or communities, including African Americans, Latinos, Asian Americans, Hawaiian/Pacific Islander, Indigenous Peoples, LGBTQ, and religious minorities.
description_plain: Your child can learn about how different people and cultures, like African Americans, Latinos, Asian Americans, Indigenous Peoples, LGBTQ, and others, have helped shape neighborhoods and communities.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the changes within one neighborhood and/or community over time.
description_plain: Your child can describe how one neighborhood or community has changed over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare and contrast neighborhoods and/or communities, both past and present, through studies of their people and events. For example: The National Western Stock Show, state/county fairs, and community events.
description_plain: Your child can compare neighborhoods or communities from the past and now by studying their people and events.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe how people of various cultures influence neighborhoods and communities over time.
description_plain: Your child understands how people from different cultures have influenced neighborhoods and communities over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Demonstrate curiosity about events and people from the past using primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child shows interest in learning about past events and people by using original and secondhand sources.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify key attributes of a variety of information products. For example: Books, newspapers, online or print articles, etc.
description_plain: Your child can recognize important features of different information sources like books, newspapers, and articles.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify local historical primary and secondary sources from multiple diverse perspectives and generate questions about their functions, significance, and perspective. For example: Maps, photographs, letters, etc.
description_plain: Your child can find local historical sources like maps, photos, and letters, and ask questions about what they show and why they matter.

subject: Social Studies
description: Organize historical events by creating timelines and explaining the information conveyed by them.
description_plain: Your child can put historical events in order on a timeline and explain what the timeline shows.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain that the nature of history involves stories of the past preserved in various primary and secondary sources. For example: Images, oral and written accounts, etc.
description_plain: Your child understands that history is made up of stories from the past found in things like pictures and written or spoken accounts.

subject: Social Studies
description: Ask questions and discuss ideas taken from primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions and talk about ideas from original and secondhand historical sources.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply economic reasoning skills to make informed personal financial decisions.
description_plain: Your child can use economic thinking to make smart choices about their own money.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the origins, structures, and functions of governments to evaluate the impact on citizens and the global society.
description_plain: Your child can study how governments start, work, and affect people and the world.

subject: Social Studies
description: Express an understanding of how civic participation affects policy by applying the rights and responsibilities of a citizen.
description_plain: Your child understands how being a citizen and taking part in the community can change laws and rules.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate how scarce resources are allocated in societies through the analysis of individual choice, market interaction, and public policy.
description_plain: Your child can look at how limited resources are shared through choices, markets, and government rules.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine the characteristics of places and regions, and the changing nature among geographic and human interactions.
description_plain: Your child studies places and regions and how people and the environment change over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply geographic representations and perspectives to analyze human movement, spatial patterns, systems, and the connections and relationships among them.
description_plain: Your child uses maps and other tools to study how people move and connect in different places.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze historical time periods and patterns of continuity and change, through multiple perspectives, within and among cultures and societies.
description_plain: Your child looks at history from many views to understand what stays the same and what changes in cultures and societies.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply the process of inquiry to examine and analyze how historical knowledge is viewed, constructed, and interpreted.
description_plain: Your child learns how to ask questions and think about how history is made and understood.

subject: Social Studies
description: Articulate task requirements and identify deadlines when developing a plan to meet a financial goal.
description_plain: Your child can explain what tasks need to be done and when they are due when making a plan to reach a money goal.

subject: Social Studies
description: Demonstrate an understanding of cause and effect related to personal decisions they make regarding reaching a financial goal.
description_plain: Your child understands how their choices affect reaching a money goal.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize how members of a community rely on each other, considering personal contributions as applicable, when creating and completing a plan to reach a financial goal.
description_plain: Your child knows that people in a community depend on each other and can think about their own part when planning to reach a money goal.

subject: Social Studies
description: Set goals and develop strategies to remain focused on learning and reaching financial goals.
description_plain: Your child can set goals and make plans to stay focused on learning and reaching money goals.

subject: Social Studies
description: Demonstrate flexibility, imagination, and inventiveness in taking on tasks and activities that will help reach a financial goal.
description_plain: Your child can be flexible and creative when working on tasks to reach a money goal.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use a variety of strategies to achieve a financial goal, such as buying a new toy.
description_plain: Your child can use different ways to reach a money goal, like saving to buy a new toy.

subject: Social Studies
description: Model strategies to achieve a personal financial goal using arithmetic operations.
description_plain: Your child can use math operations to show ways to reach a personal money goal.

subject: Social Studies
description: Create a plan with specific steps to reach a short-term financial goal.
description_plain: Your child can make a step-by-step plan to reach a short-term money goal.

subject: Social Studies
description: Differentiate the role of income and expenses when creating a budget.
description_plain: Your child understands the difference between money coming in and money going out when making a budget.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify activities that individuals can do to earn money to reach personal financial goals.
description_plain: Your child can name different jobs people do to earn money for their personal goals.

subject: Social Studies
description: Give examples of short-term spending and savings goals.
description_plain: Your child can give examples of things people might save or spend money on soon.

subject: Social Studies
description: Create a plan to meet a financial goal.
description_plain: Your child can make a simple plan to reach a money goal.

subject: Social Studies
description: Connect knowledge from personal experiences in schools and communities to civic engagement.
description_plain: Your child understands how their own school and community experiences relate to being involved in their community.

subject: Social Studies
description: Discuss the role of elections in choosing specific candidates to represent the public interest in local government.
description_plain: Your child can talk about how elections help people choose leaders for local government.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe how local government provides opportunities for people to exercise their rights and initiate change.
description_plain: Your child understands how local government lets people use their rights and make changes.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and explain a variety of roles leaders, citizens, and others play in local government.
description_plain: Your child can name and explain different jobs that leaders, citizens, and others have in local government.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the services local governments provide and how those services are funded.
description_plain: Your child can explain what services local governments provide and how they pay for them.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the origins and structures of local government.
description_plain: Your child understands where local government started and how it is organized.

subject: Social Studies
