subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate how scarce resources are allocated in societies through the analysis of individual choice, market interaction, and public policy.
description_plain: Your child can look at how limited resources are shared through choices, markets, and government rules.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine the characteristics of places and regions, and the changing nature among geographic and human interactions.
description_plain: Your child studies places and regions and how people and the environment change over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply geographic representations and perspectives to analyze human movement, spatial patterns, systems, and the connections and relationships among them.
description_plain: Your child uses maps and other tools to study how people move and connect in different places.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze historical time periods and patterns of continuity and change, through multiple perspectives, within and among cultures and societies.
description_plain: Your child looks at history from many views to understand what stays the same and what changes in cultures and societies.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply the process of inquiry to examine and analyze how historical knowledge is viewed, constructed, and interpreted.
description_plain: Your child learns how to ask questions and think about how history is made and understood.

subject: Social Studies
description: Interpret information and draw conclusions based on the best analysis.
description_plain: Your child reads information carefully and makes good conclusions based on the best evidence.

subject: Social Studies
description: Investigate to form hypotheses, make observations, and draw conclusions.
description_plain: Your child investigates questions by making guesses, watching closely, and deciding what the results mean.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the impact of taxes on the people of the United States over time.
description_plain: Your child studies how taxes have affected people in the United States over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the changes in the development of human capital over time. For example: Gaining knowledge and skill through education, apprenticeship, entrepreneurship, and work experience.
description_plain: Your child looks at how people have gained skills and knowledge through education, work, and experience over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain factors that have impacted borrowing and investing over time. For example: Currency stability, stocks, and banking practices.
description_plain: Your child explains what has affected borrowing and investing money over time, like banking and stock markets.

subject: Social Studies
description: Calculate how the value of money has changed over time impacting earning, spending, borrowing, and investing. For example: Inflation and recession.
description_plain: Your child calculates how money’s value has changed over time and how that affects earning, spending, borrowing, and investing.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine the role of consumer decisions and taxes within the market economies of early American history.
description_plain: Your child studies how consumer choices and taxes worked in early American market economies.

subject: Social Studies
description: Connect knowledge as citizens from personal ideas/understandings to civic engagement about the origins, structures, and functions of governments.
description_plain: Your child connects what they know as citizens to how governments start, work, and are organized.

subject: Social Studies
description: Make connections as citizens between information gathered and personal experiences to apply and/or test solutions to analyze the structures and functions of government.
description_plain: Your child uses information and personal experience to try out and test solutions about how governments work.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use a variety of resources, including Supreme Court decisions, to identify and evaluate issues that involve civic responsibility, individual rights, and the common good.
description_plain: Your child uses different resources, like Supreme Court cases, to find and think about issues involving rights, responsibilities, and the common good.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how the state and federal courts' power of judicial review is reflected in the United States' form of constitutional government. For example: Marbury v. Madison.
description_plain: Your child explains how courts use judicial review to check laws and how this fits into the U.S. government system.

subject: Social Studies
description: Discuss the tensions between individual rights and liberties with state, tribal, and national laws.
description_plain: Your child discusses how individual rights sometimes conflict with laws at state, tribal, and national levels.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate the historical significance of the Declaration of Independence.
description_plain: Your child evaluates why the Declaration of Independence is important in history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply knowledge about the three branches of government and how they interact with one another to historical and contemporary problems.
description_plain: Your child applies what they know about the three branches of government to understand and solve past and current problems.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the role and importance of the Constitution and the strength of amendments made during this time period. Including but not limited to: the Bill of Rights, and the 13th, 14th, and 15th Amendments.
description_plain: Your child can explain why the Constitution and important changes like the Bill of Rights and the 13th, 14th, and 15th Amendments matter.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate the strengths of rule of law.
description_plain: Your child understands why having laws that everyone must follow is important.

subject: Social Studies
description: Investigate and evaluate the purpose and place of rule of law in a constitutional system.
description_plain: Your child investigates why rules and laws are important in a government with a Constitution.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply knowledge as citizens to set goals, make informed decisions and transfer to new contexts about the roles and responsibilities of individual citizens.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about being a citizen to set goals and make smart choices about their responsibilities.

subject: Social Studies
description: Make connections as citizens between information gathered and personal experiences to apply and/or test solutions to analyze citizenship and examine the individual role in government.
description_plain: Your child connects what they learn with their own experiences to understand how citizens take part in government.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine ways members of society may effectively and civilly voice opinions, monitor government, and bring about change on a local, state, and national level, and which could have global implications.
description_plain: Your child learns how people can respectfully share opinions, watch the government, and create change locally and beyond.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze primary sources supporting democratic freedoms and the founding of our government and explain how they provide for both continuity and change. Including but not limited to: the Declaration of Independence, the Constitution, Bill of Rights, and the Declaration of Sentiments.
description_plain: Your child looks at important documents like the Declaration of Independence and explains how they support freedoms and show both change and tradition.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate the results of various strategies used to enact political change over time.
description_plain: Your child thinks about different ways people have tried to make political changes and what happened because of those efforts.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare how the leadership of various individuals has influenced the United States government and politics. For example: Women, American Indians, African Americans, and people in the unsettled territories.
description_plain: Your child compares how leaders from different groups, like women and Native Americans, have influenced U.S. government and politics.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the relationship between the debate about citizenship and national identity in early U.S History, and how those ideas shaped political institutions and society.
description_plain: Your child analyzes how early debates about who counts as a citizen shaped the country’s government and society.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe major political and/or social changes that affected the definition of citizenship, expanded or restricted the rights of citizens, and how those changes impacted women, Indigenous Peoples, African Americans, Latinos, Asian Americans, LGBTQ, religious minorities, and people living in annexed territories.
description_plain: Your child describes big political and social changes that affected who is a citizen and what rights they have, including impacts on many groups.

subject: Social Studies
description: Construct an understanding of the changing definition of citizenship and the expansion of rights of citizens in the United States.
description_plain: Your child builds an understanding of how citizenship and citizens’ rights have changed and grown in the U.S.

subject: Social Studies
description: Educate and inspire others using sound economic understandings to evaluate economic policy and inform economic decisions based upon consideration of past experiences.
description_plain: Your child shares what they know about economics to help others understand and make good economic choices based on history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply knowledge and skills as an economist and citizen to implement sophisticated, appropriate, and workable solutions to address complex global problems using interdisciplinary perspectives independently or with others in order to inform public policy.
description_plain: Your child uses economic knowledge to create smart solutions for big global problems and help guide public decisions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Make predictions and design data/information collection and analysis strategies concerning economic policy through evaluation of historical events.
description_plain: Your child makes predictions and plans how to collect and study information about economic policies by looking at past events.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain why nations sometimes restrict trade by using quotas, tariffs, and nontariff barriers.
description_plain: Your child explains why countries sometimes limit trade using things like taxes or limits on goods.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the effects of domestic policies on international trade.
description_plain: Your child explains how a country’s own rules can affect trade with other countries.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the factors that lead to a region or nation having a comparative and absolute advantage in trade. For example: The American System proposed by Henry Clay.
description_plain: Your child describes why some places are better at making or trading certain goods, like in Henry Clay’s American System.

subject: Social Studies
description: Give examples of regional, national, and international differences in resources, productivity, and costs that provide a basis for trade.
description_plain: Your child gives examples of how differences in resources and costs between places lead to trade.

subject: Social Studies
description: Investigate how economic freedom, including free trade, was important for economic growth in early American history.
description_plain: Your child investigates how having freedom to trade helped America’s economy grow early on.

subject: Social Studies
description: Demonstrate ways to adapt and reach workable solutions in understanding differing perspectives over use of resources and space.
description_plain: Your child shows ways to work together and find solutions when people have different ideas about using land and resources.

subject: Social Studies
description: Act on creative ideas to make a tangible and useful contribution to analyze and explain interdependence, cooperation, and conflict over land and resources in early American history.
description_plain: Your child uses creative ideas to explain how people depended on or fought over land and resources in early America.

subject: Social Studies
description: Synthesize ideas in original and surprising ways while comparing differing geographic perspectives.
description_plain: Your child combines ideas in new ways to compare different views about geography.

subject: Social Studies
description: Interpret information and draw conclusions as geographers based on the best analysis to recognize and understand the processes that interact in shaping human population patterns and historical events.
description_plain: Your child looks at information like maps and data to understand how people and events shaped where people live.

subject: Social Studies
description: Interpret geographic and environmental data to understand how competition over land and resources shaped the development of the early United States. For example: Establishing the Mason/Dixon line, development of railroads, the Louisiana Purchase, and the Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo.
description_plain: Your child uses geographic and environmental facts to explain how fights over land shaped early U.S. history, like the Mason-Dixon line and the Louisiana Purchase.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare how differing geographic perspectives apply to a historic issue.
description_plain: Your child compares different geographic viewpoints on a historical issue.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze patterns of conflict and cooperation that resulted from human migration and the economic, political, ethnic, and social implications of those interactions.
description_plain: Your child analyzes how moving people caused conflicts and cooperation, affecting economics, politics, and society.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the push and pull factors that shaped immigration to and migration within the early United States. For example: Economic opportunities, religious refuge, and forced migration.
description_plain: Your child studies reasons why people moved to or within early America, like jobs, religion, or forced moves.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize the impact of the competition for control of land and resources in early American history.
description_plain: Your child recognizes how fighting for land and resources affected early American history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Demonstrate ways to adapt and reach workable solutions when considering the use of limited resources and their impact upon political and historical events.
description_plain: Your child shows ways to adjust and find solutions when using limited resources affects politics and history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Organize geographic information using technologies to develop greater understanding of a historical event or action (Media Literacy, Data Literacy).
description_plain: Your child uses technology to organize geographic information and better understand historical events.

subject: Social Studies
description: Synthesize ideas in insightful or innovative ways to demonstrate understanding of demographic patterns of movement and their impact upon the physical systems of the United States (Creativity and Innovation).
description_plain: Your child combines ideas in creative ways to understand how people moving affects the land and environment.

subject: Social Studies
description: Interpret information and draw conclusions using demographic information based on the best analysis to understand patterns of change in human and physical systems (Critical Thinking and Analysis).
description_plain: Your child looks at population data and draws conclusions to understand changes in people and the environment.

subject: Social Studies
description: Calculate and analyze population trends in relation to historical phenomena.
description_plain: Your child calculates and studies population changes related to historical events.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare the relationship between human settlement and the environment to recognize important regional connections to early U.S. historical events.
description_plain: Your child compares how people’s settlements and the environment connect to important early U.S. history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize patterns of land acquisition and apply to events of the past.
description_plain: Your child recognizes patterns of land being gained and relates them to past events.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the spatial distribution of cultural groups and human migration patterns.
description_plain: Your child identifies where cultural groups lived and how people moved.

subject: Social Studies
description: Interpret primary and secondary geographic sources to analyze a historic issue. For example: Using both historical and contemporary maps to understand the changes in American boundaries.
description_plain: Your child uses old and new maps and other sources to study a historical issue.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use geographic tools to research and analyze patterns in human and physical systems in the United States.
description_plain: Your child uses maps and tools to study patterns in people and the environment in the U.S.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze ideas that are critical to the understanding of early United States history. For example: Ideas involved in major events and movements such as settler colonialism, the changing definition of liberty and citizenship, enslavement of African and Indigenous Peoples, federalism, nativism of the Antebellum period, emancipation, and expansionism.
description_plain: Your child analyzes important ideas that help understand early U.S. history, like settler colonialism, citizenship, slavery, and expansion.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the cause and effect relationships of major conflicts from the origins of the American Revolution through Reconstruction.
description_plain: Your child can explain what caused major conflicts from the American Revolution through Reconstruction and what effects those conflicts had.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate the impact of various gender, age, race, ethnicity, religion, and class during this time period and the impact of these demographic groups on the events of the time period.
description_plain: Your child understands how different groups like men and women, people of different ages, races, religions, and social classes influenced events during this time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine the causes and effects of the territorial, political, and economic expansion of the United States.
description_plain: Your child can describe why and how the United States grew in land, politics, and economy during this period.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate continuity and change over the course of United States history by examining various eras and determining major sources of conflict and compromise both in Colorado and across the nation. Including but not limited to: the Indian Removal Act, the Buffalo Soldiers, and the Sand Creek Massacre.
description_plain: Your child can identify what stayed the same and what changed in U.S. history by looking at important conflicts and compromises, including events in Colorado like the Indian Removal Act and the Sand Creek Massacre.

subject: Social Studies
description: Determine and explain the historical context of key people and events from the Revolutionary War Era through Reconstruction including the examination of different perspectives. For example: Grievances from the colonists against the British Parliament, the Constitutional Convention, the role of abolitionists, contributions and grievances from Indigenous Peoples and African Americans, and the causes and effects of the Civil War.
description_plain: Your child can explain important people and events from the Revolutionary War to Reconstruction by looking at different points of view, like those of colonists, abolitionists, Indigenous Peoples, and African Americans.

subject: Social Studies
description: Develop a contextual understanding of the historical eras, individuals, groups, ideas, and themes from the origins of the American Revolution through Reconstruction.
description_plain: Your child understands the main events, people, groups, ideas, and themes from the start of the American Revolution through Reconstruction.

subject: Social Studies
description: Look for and find value in perspectives expressed by others.
description_plain: Your child can listen to and appreciate different opinions and viewpoints.

subject: Social Studies
description: Make predictions and design data/information collection to analyze conflicting perspectives.
description_plain: Your child can make guesses about what might happen and plan how to collect information to understand different opinions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Synthesize ideas in insightful ways by examining multiple perspectives from the American Revolution through Reconstruction.
description_plain: Your child can combine ideas by looking at many different viewpoints from the American Revolution through Reconstruction.

subject: Social Studies
description: Interpret information and draw conclusions based on deep analysis of primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child can read and study original documents and other sources to understand and make conclusions about history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Synthesize data from multiple sources in order to connect those sources to the traditional historical narrative.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources and connect it to the usual stories told about history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Construct a written historical argument supported by relevant evidence and logical reasoning demonstrating the use or understanding of primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child can write a clear argument about history using evidence and good reasons from original and other sources.

subject: Social Studies
description: Critique data for point of view, historical context, distortion, or propaganda and relevance to historical inquiry.
description_plain: Your child can check information for bias, the time it was created, mistakes, or attempts to mislead, and decide if it helps answer historical questions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze evidence from multiple sources including those with conflicting accounts about specific events in both Colorado and United States history. For example: Indigenous Peoples', Hawaiian/Pacific Islander, and African American perspectives on Western colonization and enslavement; Asian American and Latinos' perspectives on immigration; and the Indian Removal Act, the Buffalo Soldiers, and the Sand Creek Massacre.
description_plain: Your child can study evidence from many sources, even when they tell different stories, about events in Colorado and U.S. history, including views from Indigenous Peoples, African Americans, Asian Americans, and Latinos.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use and interpret documents and other relevant primary and secondary sources pertaining to United States history from multiple diverse perspectives.
description_plain: Your child can use and understand documents and other sources about U.S. history from many different points of view.

subject: Social Studies
description: Investigate and evaluate primary and secondary sources from multiple diverse perspectives about United States history from the American Revolution through Reconstruction to formulate and defend claims with textual evidence and logical reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can investigate and think about many sources from different viewpoints about U.S. history from the American Revolution through Reconstruction and use evidence to support their ideas.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply economic reasoning skills to make informed personal financial decisions.
description_plain: Your child can use economic thinking to make smart choices about their own money.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the origins, structures, and functions of governments to evaluate the impact on citizens and the global society.
description_plain: Your child can study how governments start, work, and affect people and the world.

subject: Social Studies
description: Express an understanding of how civic participation affects policy by applying the rights and responsibilities of a citizen.
description_plain: Your child understands how being a citizen and taking part in the community can change laws and rules.

description_plain: Your child can look at how limited resources are shared through choices, markets, and government rules.

description_plain: Your child learns how to ask questions and think about how history is made and understood.

description_plain: Your child can plan and organize how they behave and manage themselves.

description: Understand the structure of the American judicial system, the process of judicial appointments and key court decisions, in both Colorado and the United States, that affect the system of checks and balances and interactions of the local, state, tribal, and federal systems. Including but not limited to: significant Colorado court decisions such as Francisco Maestas et al. v. George H. Shone (1914), U.S. Term Limits, Inc. v. Thornton (1995), Romer v. Evans (1996), Colorado Union of Taxpayers Found. v. City of Aspen (2018); and landmark U.S. Supreme Court Cases such as Korematsu v. United States (1944), Tinker v. Des Moines Independent Community School District (1969).

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate continuity and change over the course of world history. For example: Social and political movements related to nationality, ethnicity, and gender; revolutions; the World Wars; the Cold War; independence movements/decolonization and 19th, 20th and 21st century genocides such as the Armenian Genocide; the Holocaust perpetrated by the fascist German Nazi Party (National Socialist German Workers Party) and its collaborators; the Sand Creek Massacre, Cambodian Genocide; the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics murder of Ukrainian nationals; genocides conducted by the Communist Party of China (CPC) against its political opponents during the Totalization Period, Collectivization, the Great Leap Forward, the Cultural Revolution, and Mao's Famine; and the current genocide of the Uyghurs; as well as genocides that have taken place in Rwanda, Darfur and Bosnia.
description_plain: Your child understands how things have stayed the same or changed in world history, including social and political movements, wars, independence efforts, and genocides.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the complexity of events throughout United States history. For example: The Civil Rights Movement (e.g., Double V Campaign, the Voting Rights Act of 1965, and the Stonewall Riots); migration, immigration, and displacement (e.g., immigration and citizenship legislation, Japanese American incarceration, and debates over tribal sovereignty); landmark court cases (e.g., Keyes v. School District #1 Denver, Brown v. Board of Education, and Obergefell v. Hodges), and the war on terror (e.g., 9/11, Afghanistan and Iraq wars, Middle Eastern discrimination, and the evolution of U.S. counterterrorism efforts).
description_plain: Your child can analyze complex events in U.S. history, such as the Civil Rights Movement, immigration, important court cases, and the war on terror.

description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the literal and figurative meanings of words in context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and use
description_plain: Your child understands how words are related and how to use them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Access reference materials (i.e., dictionary, word prediction) to verify the meaning of the word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use tools like dictionaries to check the meaning of words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Seek clarification and meaning support when unfamiliar words are encountered while reading or communicating
description_plain: Your child asks for help or looks for meaning when they find unfamiliar words while reading or talking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring root words (e.g., like) and the words that result when affixes are added (e.g., liked, disliked, liking)
description_plain: Your child uses common root words and knows how adding beginnings or endings changes their meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to determine which word is missing from a text
description_plain: Your child can figure out which word is missing in a sentence by using the surrounding words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of word meanings
description_plain: Your child knows what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast how different texts on the same topic present the details
description_plain: Your child can compare and explain how different texts about the same topic share details differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how a claim or reason fits into the overall structure of an informational text
description_plain: Your child can explain how a claim or reason fits into the whole informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare a text to an audio, video or multimedia version of the same text
description_plain: Your child can compare a written text with an audio, video, or multimedia version of the same content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author’s purpose or point of view
description_plain: Your child understands why the author wrote the text or what their point of view is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how a fact, step, or event fits into the overall structure of the text
description_plain: Your child can explain how a fact, step, or event fits into the overall text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how words or phrases are used to persuade or inform a text
description_plain: Your child understands how words or phrases are used to persuade or inform in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how two individuals, events or ideas in a text are related
description_plain: Your child can explain how two people, events, or ideas in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more central ideas in a text
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze text to identify where information is explicitly stated and where inferences must be drawn
description_plain: Your child can tell where information is clearly stated and where they need to make guesses based on clues.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of text while actively engaging in reading or listening to stories, dramas, and poetry
description_plain: Your child shows they understand stories, dramas, and poems while reading or listening to them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare a fictional time, place, or character in one text with the same time, place, or character portrayed in an historical account
description_plain: Your child can compare a made-up time, place, or character in a story with the same time, place, or character in a real historical account.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare a text version of a story, drama, or poem with an audio, video, or live version of the same text
description_plain: Your child can compare a written story, drama, or poem with an audio, video, or live version of it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare the points of view of two or more characters or narrators in a text
description_plain: Your child can compare the viewpoints of two or more characters or narrators in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare the structure of two or more texts (e.g., stories, poems, or dramas)
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more stories, poems, or dramas are organized.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of simple idioms and figures of speech as they are used in a text
description_plain: Your child understands the meaning of simple idioms and figures of speech used in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how two or more story elements are related
description_plain: Your child can explain how two or more parts of a story are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify events in a text that are related to the theme or central idea
description_plain: Your child can find events in a story that relate to its main theme or idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze text to identify where information is explicitly stated and where inferences must be drawn
description_plain: Your child can tell where information is clearly given and where they need to make guesses based on clues.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicate precisely (i.e., provide complete information) or efficiently (i.e., telegraphic communication) as required by the context, task, and communication partner
description_plain: Your child can communicate clearly and with the right amount of detail depending on who they are talking to and the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select or create audio recordings and visual/tactile displays to emphasize specific points in a presentation
description_plain: Your child can choose or create audio or visual materials to highlight important points during a presentation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present findings on a topic including relevant descriptions, facts, or details
description_plain: Your child can share information about a topic using relevant facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine whether the claims made by a speaker are fact or opinion
description_plain: Your child can tell if what a speaker says is a fact or just their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify details related to the main idea of a text presented orally or through other media
description_plain: Your child can find details that support the main idea when listening to a story or presentation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Remain on the topic of the discussion when answering questions or making other contributions to a discussion
description_plain: Your child stays focused on the topic when answering questions or joining a discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, follow simple, agreed-upon rules for discussions, and carry out assigned roles
description_plain: With help, your child follows simple rules during discussions and does their assigned part.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared to share information
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready to share what they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions
description_plain: Your child takes part in group discussions with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply Essential Elements of Grade 8 Reading Standards to informational texts (e.g., “Use relevant and sufficient for supporting the claims and argument.”)
description_plain: Your child uses important reading skills to understand and support ideas in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply Essential Elements of Grade 8 Reading Standards to literature (e.g., “Compare and contract themes, patterns of events, or characters across two or more stories or dramas.”)
description_plain: Your child compares themes, events, or characters in two or more stories or plays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information from literary and informational text to support writing
description_plain: Your child uses information from stories and facts to help with their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select quotes providing relevant information about a topic from multiple print of digital sources
description_plain: Your child picks out important quotes about a topic from books or online sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer and pose questions based on one source of information
description_plain: Your child does short research projects using one source to answer and ask questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for a variety of tasks, purposes, and audiences
description_plain: Your child writes regularly for different tasks, reasons, and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce writing to interact and collaborate with others
description_plain: Your child uses technology like the Internet to write and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan before writing and revise own writing
description_plain: Your child plans their writing before starting and makes changes to improve it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce writing that is appropriate for the task, purpose, or audience
description_plain: Your child writes in a way that fits the task, reason, or audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use to-be verbs (am, is, was, were, be, become, became) accurately when writing and communicating
description_plain: Your child uses verbs like am, is, was, and were correctly when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use language to achieve desired outcomes when communicating
description_plain: Your child uses words to clearly say what they want when talking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of letter-sound relationships and/or common spelling patterns
description_plain: Your child spells words by sounding them out and using common spelling patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use end punctuation and capitalization when writing a sentence or questions
description_plain: Your child uses capital letters and punctuation correctly in sentences and questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of conventions of standard English
description_plain: Your child shows they understand the basic rules of standard English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate verbs to match nouns
description_plain: Your child can choose the right verbs to match the nouns in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use simple verb tenses (e.g., I walked, I walk, I will walk)
description_plain: Your child can use simple verb tenses like past, present, and future correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate standard English grammar and usage when communicating
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and sentence structure when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words to describe the feelings of characters or provide other sensory information about the setting, experiences or events
description_plain: Your child uses words to show how characters feel or to describe sights, sounds, and other details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use temporal words (e.g., first, then, next) to signal order
description_plain: Your child uses words like first, then, and next to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write a narrative about a real or imagined experience introducing the experience, at least one character, and two or more events
description_plain: Your child writes a story about a real or made-up experience, including an introduction, at least one character, and two or more events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write about events or personal experiences
description_plain: Your child writes about things that happen or personal experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use domain specific vocabulary related to the topic
description_plain: Your child uses special words related to the topic they are writing about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write complete thoughts as appropriate
description_plain: Your child writes complete sentences that make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write on or more facts or details related to the topic
description_plain: Your child writes one or more facts or details about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly and write to convey ideas and information about it including visual, tactual, or multimedia information as appropriate
description_plain: Your child clearly introduces a topic and writes information about it, using pictures or other media when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to share information supported by details
description_plain: Your child writes to share information and supports it with details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write reasons to support a claim about a topic or text
description_plain: Your child writes reasons to support their opinion about a topic or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the claim and provide reasons or pieces of evidence to support it
description_plain: Your child introduces their opinion and gives reasons or evidence to back it up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write claims about topics or texts
description_plain: Your child writes opinions about topics or texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use general academic and domain-specific words and phrases across contexts
description_plain: Your child uses general and topic-specific words in different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use descriptive words to add meaning when writing and communicating
description_plain: Your child uses descriptive words to make their writing and speaking more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of common words to understand the meaning of compound and complex words in which they appear (e.g., birdhouse, household)
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about common words to understand longer or compound words like birdhouse or household.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the use of multiple meaning words
description_plain: Your child understands that some words have more than one meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and use
description_plain: Your child understands how words are related and how to use them correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Seek clarification and meaning support when unfamiliar words are encountered while reading or communicating (e.g., printed, adapted, or electronic dictionary, thesaurus, or glossary)
description_plain: Your child can use tools like dictionaries or glossaries to understand words they don’t know when reading or talking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring root words (e.g., like) and the words that result when affixes are added (e.g., liked, disliked, liking)
description_plain: Your child can recognize root words and understand how adding beginnings or endings changes their meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to determine which word is missing from a content area text
description_plain: Your child can figure out which word is missing in a sentence by looking at the words around it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of word meanings
description_plain: Your child understands what different words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding while actively reading or listening to literary nonfiction
description_plain: Your child understands what they read or hear when reading or listening to true stories and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify where two different texts on the same topic differ in their interpretation of the details
description_plain: Your child can find differences in how two texts explain the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the argument made by an author in an informational text
description_plain: Your child can figure out the main point or argument an author is making in an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine whether a topic is best presented as audio, video, multimedia, or text
description_plain: Your child can decide if a topic is best learned by listening, watching, using multimedia, or reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author’s purpose or point of view and identify examples from text that describe or support it
description_plain: Your child can tell why an author wrote a text and find parts that show the author’s opinion or purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate the topic sentence and supporting details in a paragraph
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea sentence and the details that support it in a paragraph.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine connotative meanings of words and phrases in a text
description_plain: Your child understands the feelings or ideas that words suggest beyond their basic meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount events in the order they were presented in the text
description_plain: Your child can retell events in the order they happened in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a summary of familiar informational text
description_plain: Your child can give a short summary of an informational text they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite text to support inferences from informational text
description_plain: Your child can find parts of a text that support their ideas or guesses about what the text means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of text while actively engaged in reading or listening to stories, dramas, and poetry
description_plain: Your child understands stories, plays, and poems while reading or listening to them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast themes, patterns of events, or characters across two or more stories or dramas
description_plain: Your child can compare themes, events, or characters in two or more stories or plays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a text version of a story, drama, or poem with an audio, video, or live version of the same text
description_plain: Your child can compare a story, play, or poem in text form with its audio, video, or live version.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the difference in the points of view of a character and the audience or reader in a text with suspense or humor
description_plain: Your child can explain how a character’s point of view is different from the audience’s in funny or suspenseful texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the structure of two or more texts
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts are organized or put together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine connotative meanings of words and phrases in a text
description_plain: Your child understands the feelings or ideas that words suggest beyond their basic meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify which elements in a story of drama lead to subsequent action.
description_plain: Your child can find parts of a story or play that cause what happens next.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount an event related to the theme or central idea, including details about character and setting
description_plain: Your child can tell about an event in a story that shows the main idea, including details about the characters and where it happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite text to support inferences from stories and poems
description_plain: Your child can use parts of a story or poem to explain ideas they understand from reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt communication to a variety of contexts and tasks
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak or write depending on who they are talking to or what they are doing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia and visual information into presentations
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, videos, or other media to make presentations more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present descriptions, facts, or details supporting specific points made on a topic
description_plain: Your child can share facts and details that support their main points when talking about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the argument made by a speaker on a topic
description_plain: Your child can figure out what opinion or argument a speaker is making about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the purpose of information presented in graphic, oral, visual, or multimodal formats
description_plain: Your child can understand why information is shared in pictures, talks, videos, or other formats.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others in a discussion and relate it to own ideas
description_plain: Your child can listen to new ideas in a discussion and connect them to their own thoughts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Remain on the topic of the discussion when asking or answering questions or making other contributions to a discussion
description_plain: Your child can stay focused on the topic when asking or answering questions during a discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow simple rules and carry out assigned roles during discussions
description_plain: Your child can follow simple rules and do their part during group talks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared to share information previously studied
description_plain: Your child can come to discussions ready to share what they have learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions
description_plain: Your child can take part in group discussions with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify information, objects, or events that relate to personal experiences (EE.W.K.8).
description_plain: Your child can recognize information, things, or events that connect to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather and explore relevant information (e.g., library books, internet, magazine).

subject: Language Arts
description: Answer questions of interest through shared resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to improve thinking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to gather information on a chosen topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a clear purpose for research or inquiry.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (EE.W.K.7).

subject: Language Arts
description: Explore the purposes for research and inquiry by accessing resources in collaborative settings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate emerging understanding of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify answers to simple questions (e.g., who, what) from an array of choices (EE.L.K.1d).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring plural nouns (EE.L.K.1c).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring nouns in communication (EE.L.K.1b).
description_plain: Your child uses common nouns when talking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between letters and other symbols or shapes of letter and word use (EE.L.K.1a).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between letters and other shapes or symbols.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate emerging understanding of letter and word use (EE.L.K.1).
description_plain: Your child is learning how letters and words are used in reading and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate mechanics and conventions when creating simple texts.
description_plain: Your child uses basic rules like capitalization and punctuation when making simple texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explore a variety of digital tools to produce individual or group writing (EE.W.K.6).
description_plain: Your child tries different digital tools to create writing alone or with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respond to suggestions to add one detail to strengthen writing.
description_plain: Your child listens to suggestions and adds one detail to make their writing better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select an event and use drawing, dictating, or writing and share information about it (EE.W.K.3).
description_plain: Your child picks an event and uses drawing, talking, or writing to tell about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write real or imagined narratives using labels, dictation, and drawing.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imaginary things using labels, talking, and drawing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explore a variety of digital tools to produce individual or group writing (EE.W.K.6).
description_plain: Your child explores different digital tools to create writing alone or with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respond to questions by drawing, dictating, or writing to strengthen writing about a familiar topic.
description_plain: Your child answers questions by drawing, talking, or writing to improve their writing on a familiar topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select a familiar topic and use drawing, dictating, or writing to share information about the topic (EE.W.K.2).
description_plain: Your child chooses a familiar topic and uses drawing, talking, or writing to share information about it.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child writes simple informative or explanatory texts using labels, talking, and drawing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explore a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing.
description_plain: Your child tries different digital tools to create and share their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add details based on given feedback, to strengthen writing.
description_plain: Your child adds details to their writing when given helpful feedback.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select a familiar book and use drawing, dictating, or writing to state an opinion about it (EE.W.K.1).
description_plain: Your child can pick a book they know and use drawing, talking, or writing to say what they think about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinions using labels, dictation, and drawing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify initial or final sounds in consonant-vowel-consonant words (excluding blends).
description_plain: Your child can hear and say the first or last sounds in simple three-letter words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate an emerging understanding of multiple meaning words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child understands that some words can have more than one meaning.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child enjoys reading familiar books together with you and pays attention to the story.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify single-syllable spoken words with the same onset (beginning sound) as a familiar word (EE.RF.K.2c).

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

description: Demonstrate emerging understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes) (EE.RF.K.2).

subject: Language Arts
description: During shared reading, demonstrate understanding that books are read one page at a time from beginning to end (EE.RF.K.1a).

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate emerging understanding of the organization of print (EE.RF.K.1).
description_plain: Your child is learning how books and print are organized and how to use them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop basic reading skills through the use of foundational skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in shared reading of informational text (EE.RI.K.10).

description: Identify illustrations or objects/tactual information that go with a familiar text (EE.RI.K.7).

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Indicate when an unknown word is used in a text (EE.RI.K.4).
description_plain: Your child can tell when they see a word they don’t know in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify individuals, events, or details in a familiar informational text (EE.RI.K.3).
description_plain: Your child can point out people, events, or details in a familiar informational book.

subject: Language Arts

description: Identify a detail in a familiar text (EE.RI.K.1).

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and apply the concepts of print and comprehension of informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in shared reading (EE.RL.K.10).

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the adventures or experiences of a character in a familiar story (EE.RL.K.9).

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between words and illustrations in a story (EE.RL.K.6).

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize familiar texts (e.g., storybooks, poems) (EE.RL.K.5).

description: Indicate when an unknown word is used in a text (EE.RL.K.4).
description_plain: Your child can say when they see a word they don’t know in a story.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and apply the concepts of print and comprehension of literary texts.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child will start using letter sounds to help read simple words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of complex spelling patterns (orthography) and word meanings (morphology) to decode words with accuracy.
description_plain: Your child can use spelling patterns and word parts to read words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in shared reading of informational text including history/SS, science, and technical texts (EE.RI.2.10).
description_plain: Your child will join in reading informational books about history, science, or technology.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a common element between the two texts on the same topic (EE.RI.2.9).
description_plain: Your child can find something that is the same in two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify points the author makes in an informational text (EE.RI.2.8).
description_plain: Your child can point out important ideas the author shares in an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify illustrations or objects/tactual information that go with a text (EE.RI.2.7).
description_plain: Your child can match pictures or objects to the information in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the role of the author and the illustrator (EE.RI.2.6).
description_plain: Your child understands what the author and the illustrator each do in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify details in informational text or its graphic representations (EE.RI.2.5).
description_plain: Your child can find details in an informational text or its pictures and charts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words related to a topic of a text (EE.RI.2.4).
description_plain: Your child can find words that relate to the topic of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify individuals, events, or details in an informational text (EE.RI.2.3).
description_plain: Your child can find people, events, or details mentioned in an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the topic of the text (EE.RI.2.2).
description_plain: Your child can tell what the main topic of a text is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Answer who and what questions to demonstrate understanding of details in a familiar text (EE.RI.2.1).
description_plain: Your child can answer who and what questions to show they understand a familiar text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply specific skills to comprehend and fluently read informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses reading skills to understand and read informational texts smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in shared reading of stories and poetry for clearly stated purposes (EE.RL.2.10).
description_plain: Your child will join in reading stories and poems for clear reasons, like enjoyment or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify similarities between two episodes in a story (EE.RL.2.9).
description_plain: Your child can find ways two parts of a story are alike.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify illustrations or object/tactual information in print or digital text that depict characters (EE.RL.2.7).
description_plain: Your child can find pictures or objects that show the characters in a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the speakers in a dialogue (EE.RL.2.6).
description_plain: Your child can tell who is talking in a conversation in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the beginning and ending of a familiar story with a logical order (EE.RL.2.5).
description_plain: Your child can figure out the beginning and end of a familiar story in the right order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use rhyming or repetition to identify words that meaningfully complete a familiar story, poem, or song (EE.RL.2.4.).
description_plain: Your child uses rhymes or repeated words to help finish a story, poem, or song.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the actions of the characters in a story (EE.RL.2.3).
description_plain: Your child can tell what the characters do in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using details from text, recount events from familiar stories from diverse cultures (EE.RL.2.2).
description_plain: Your child can retell events from familiar stories from different cultures using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Answer who and where questions to demonstrate understanding of details in a familiar text (EE.RL.2.1).
description_plain: Your child can answer who and where questions to show they understand details in a story they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply specific skills to comprehend and fluently read literary texts.
description_plain: Your child uses specific skills to understand and read stories smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use focused vocabulary (e.g., core vocabulary, fringe vocabulary) within a discussion.
description_plain: Your child uses important vocabulary words during conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Combine words when communicating to provide clarification (EE.SL.2.6).
description_plain: Your child puts words together to make their meaning clearer when talking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select visual, audio, or tactual representations to depict a personal experience (EE.SL.2.5).
description_plain: Your child chooses pictures, sounds, or touch items to show a personal experience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Express ideas, thoughts, experiences or facts about familiar people, places, things, or event within a discussion.
description_plain: Your child shares ideas, thoughts, or facts about people, places, things, or events they know during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Express an idea, feeling, or thought about a provided topic within a discussion.
description_plain: Your child shares an idea, feeling, or thought about a given topic during a talk.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a photograph or object that reflects a personal experience and tell one detail about it.
description_plain: Your child picks a photo or object that shows a personal experience and tells one detail about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Deliver presentations while maintaining focus on topic and be prepared to discuss.
description_plain: Your child gives presentations that stay on topic and is ready to talk about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Answer questions about the details provided by the speaker (EE.SL.2.3).
description_plain: Your child answers questions about details shared by someone speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: During shared reading activities, ask and answer questions about details presented orally or through other media (EE.SL.2.2).
description_plain: During group reading, your child asks and answers questions about details heard or seen.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask for clarification and further explanation as needed about the topics and texts under discussion (EE.SL.2.1c).
description_plain: Your child asks for more explanation when they don’t understand topics or texts being discussed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others’ talk in conversations by linking their comments to the remarks for others (EE.SL.2.1b).
description_plain: Your child adds to conversations by connecting their comments to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in multiple-turn exchanges with peers with support from an adult (EE.SL.2.1a).
description_plain: With some help, your child takes turns talking with friends in conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in conversations with adults and peers (EE.SL.2.1).
description_plain: Your child joins in conversations with adults and other children.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in dialogue and learn new information through active listening.
description_plain: Your child listens carefully and talks with others to learn new things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort information on a topic or personal experience into two provided categories and write about each one (EE.W.3.8).
description_plain: Your child sorts information about a topic or experience into two groups and writes about each one.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present a brief report of research findings to an audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop supporting visual information (e.g., charts, maps, illustrations, models).
description_plain: Your child can create pictures, charts, maps, or models to help explain information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret and communicate the information learned by developing a summary statement and at least one detail.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify information about a topic for a research project (EE.W.3.7).
description_plain: Your child can find important facts about a topic for a research project.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather, interpret, and communicate information discovered during short research projects.
description_plain: Your child can collect information, understand it, and share what they learned from short research projects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for a variety of tasks, purposes, and audiences (EE.W.10).
description_plain: Your child writes regularly for different reasons and for different readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology to produce writing while interacting and collaborating with others (EE.W.6).
description_plain: Your child uses computers or tablets to write and works with others while doing it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce writing that expresses more than one idea (EE.W.3.4).
description_plain: Your child writes pieces that include more than one idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use language to make simple requests, comment, or share information (EE.L.3.3a).
description_plain: Your child uses words to ask simple questions, make comments, or share information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use language to achieve desired outcomes when communicating (EE.L.3.3).
description_plain: Your child uses language to get what they want or to communicate clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult print in the environment to support reading and spelling (EE.L.3.2g).
description_plain: Your child looks at signs, labels, or printed words around them to help with reading and spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use spelling patterns in familiar words with common spelling patterns to spell words with the same spelling patterns (EE.L.3.2f).
description_plain: Your child uses common spelling patterns to spell new words that sound the same.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use resources as needed to spell common high-frequency words accurately (EE.L.3.2e).
description_plain: Your child uses tools like dictionaries or word lists to spell common words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: During shared writing, indicate the need to add a period at the end of a sentence (EE.L.3.2b).
description_plain: Your child knows when to put a period at the end of a sentence during shared writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize the first letter of familiar names (EE.L.3.2a).
description_plain: Your child capitalizes the first letter of familiar names.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of conventions of standard English (EE.L.3.2).
description_plain: Your child understands and uses basic grammar and writing rules correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask simple questions (EE.L.3.11).
description_plain: Your child asks simple questions to learn more or understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common adjectives (EE.L.3.1g).
description_plain: Your child uses common describing words (adjectives) when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use present and past tense verbs (EE.L.3.1d).
description_plain: Your child uses verbs that show actions happening now or in the past.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use regular plural nouns in communication (EE.L.3.1b).
description_plain: Your child uses plural nouns correctly when talking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use noun + verb, noun + adjective, and subject + verb + object combinations in communication (EE.L.3.1a).
description_plain: Your child puts words together in simple sentences like noun + verb or noun + adjective.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate standard English grammar and usage when communicating (EE.L.3.1).
description_plain: Your child can use correct English grammar and rules when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a recursive process to plan, draft, revise, and edit writing, applying knowledge of the conventions of grammar, capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or sentence in a narrative.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use transitional words (e.g., first, next, then) when writing about a series of events.
description_plain: Your child can use words like first, next, and then to show the order of events in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts and feelings to develop a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select an event or personal experience and write about it including the names of people involved.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write about events or personal experiences (EE.W.3.3).
description_plain: Your child can write about things that happened or personal experiences.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or sentence for an informational/explanatory text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a linking word or phrase (e.g., also, another, and, more, but) to connect ideas.
description_plain: Your child can use words like also, another, and, or but to connect ideas in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compose a topic sentence and generate one factual sentence about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select a topic and write about it including one fact or detail (EE.W.3.2a).
description_plain: Your child can choose a topic and write about it with at least one fact or detail.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to share information supported by details (EE.W.3.2).

description_plain: Your child can write informative texts using facts and details, and finish with a related ending sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement to summarize the information presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a sentence at the end that sums up the information they shared.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Select text and write an opinion about it (EE.W.3.1a).
description_plain: Your child can choose a text and write their opinion about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinions about topics or text (EE.W.3.1).
description_plain: Your child can write opinions about topics or texts.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and use (EE.L.3.5).

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of spelling patterns (orthography), word meanings (morphology), and word relationships to decode words and increase vocabulary.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify common elements in two stories in a series (EE.RL.3.9)

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can point out parts of pictures or touchable items that show where or when something happens in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify personal point of view about a text (EE.RL.3.6).
description_plain: Your child can share their own opinion about a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the beginning, middle, and end of a familiar story with a logical order (EE.RL.3.5).

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine words and phrases that complete literal sentences in a text (EE.RL.3.4).
description_plain: Your child can find the right words or phrases to complete sentences in a story.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can tell how characters in a story feel.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can connect details to events in stories from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Answer who and what questions to demonstrate understanding of details in a text (EE.RL.3.1).
description_plain: Your child can answer who and what questions to show they understand details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply strategies to fluently read and comprehend various literary texts.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions (EE.SL.3.1.).

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate cooperatively in group activities.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall and sort information from personal experiences or a topic into given categories (EE.L.4.8).

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for a variety of tasks, purposes, and audiences (EE.L.4.10).

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce writing that expresses more than one idea (EE.L.4.4).
description_plain: Your child can write pieces that include more than one idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use language to express emotion (EE.L.4.3a).
description_plain: Your child understands how to use words to show feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use language to achieve desired outcomes when communicating (EE.L.4.3).
description_plain: Your child uses words to get their message across and reach their goals when talking or writing.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize the first word in a sentence (EE.L.4.2a).
description_plain: Your child knows to start a sentence with a capital letter.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of conventions of standard English (EE.L.4.2).
description_plain: Your child understands and uses the basic rules of correct English writing.
